### 2026-04-30 — Corrections to R1 audit per user pushback (orchestrator-outside-cycle)

**Authors:** John M. Urban (decisions + concrete evidence); Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max) (transcription).

**Status:** This subsection is an orchestrator-outside-cycle corrections appendix to the R1 audit below. R1's audit is preserved verbatim per append-only discipline. Surviving R1 work (F1, F2, F8, F9, F10, F11 + Stage B.4/B.5/B.6 contracts + Implementation order + Cross-pipeline parity table) remains the primary contract. Corrections below ONLY override F3/F4/F5 + add new Stage A.5/A.6 investigation surfaces + conditional Stage B.7/B.8 repair contracts.

**R2 (Copilot) reads BOTH the original R1 audit AND this corrections appendix.** Where the two conflict, this appendix takes precedence on F3/F4/F5 framing only.

**User pushback (verbatim 2026-04-30):**

> *"the detector logic may still be part of the problem, even after ref-stage norm SAG40 has a bump that is not flagged as a bump; it is smaller than it is before ref-stage norm, but it is there. The underlying signal is not totally 'normalized away'. This is what we are trying to avoid for sure - but flat sample detection still should have flagged SAG40 even when it was refstage normalized"*
>
> *"the 5+ missing amplicons also are almost certainly not being normalized away. ... I would bet money there are no bumps. The bumps are at the major early firing amplicons II/9A and II/2B. There may be slight bumps on some of the taller ones."*
>
> *"I reject the premise that the flat detection and amplicon reliability are working"*

**Concrete evidence the user supplied (orchestrator verified at HEAD — cmds + outputs):**

| Stage | II amplicon count | Source |
|---|---|---|
| step09 (summit states) | **12** | `awk '$1=="II"' dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/09-summitStates/5.TPM.medNorm.stage_5_median.zeroBinsRemoved.RCN_pseudo0.medSmoothedHW3.expdecay.states.collapsed.aboveBackgroundRegions.summitStateRegion.bed \| wc -l` |
| step13 (summit refinement) | **12** | `ls dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/13-summit-refinement/II_* \| wc -l` |
| step14 (multistage unification) | **3** | `awk '$1=="II"' dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/14-multistage-unification/hmm_unified_calls_aps.bed \| wc -l` |

The 3 surviving II amplicons at step14 (II:5975000-6515000, II:22215000-22480000, II:33175000-33795000) match exactly what `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/16-aps/amplicon_reliability.tsv` reports. **The 9 II amplicons that survive step09 + step13 and disappear by step14 prove the loss is at HMM step14 multistage unification, NOT upstream of `run_step16_hmm_aps` as R1's F5 speculated.**

#### Correction CR1 — Override of F3 framing

**R1 said:** *"The flat-detection mechanism CANNOT detect this artifact on its own because the very signal it would detect has already been normalized away."*

**Correction:** The signal is NOT totally normalized away. Per user direct observation of `01-prior/01-hmm/05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/` bedGraphs, SAG40 has a residual bump at II/9A even after ref-stage normalization (smaller than the pre-normalization bump, but visible). The detector SHOULD have caught the residual bump. R1's framing absolves detector logic of any bug; that's wrong.

**The chicken-and-egg story is part of the picture but not the whole picture.** Two coupled defect classes are present:
1. **Pollution suppresses signal magnitude** (R1's chicken-and-egg story; partially correct).
2. **Detector logic fails to catch residual signal even when it's present** (R1 missed this; this is what user pushback exposes).

#### Correction CR2 — Override of F4 framing

**R1 said:** *"This is conservative, not permissive. Threshold tuning is NOT the right fix."*

**Correction:** The detector IS still permissive in the relevant sense — it failed to flag SAG40 even given the residual bump. Whether the fix is threshold tuning vs detector-logic bug remediation depends on Stage A.6 investigation outcomes (see below). **R1's premise that "the detector logic is correct, only the inputs are corrupted" is REJECTED** — the detector may have a real bug that surfaces independently of input quality.

#### Correction CR3 — Override of F5 framing

**R1 said:** *"The 5+ missing chromosome-II amplicons are missing UPSTREAM of `run_step16_hmm_aps` ... likely the same chicken-and-egg root cause — stage-1 ref-stage normalization median pollution suppresses the RCN signal that HMM state-emission detection (step 6) consumes."*

**Correction:** This was **speculation, not confirmed**. The concrete evidence shows the loss is at **HMM step14 multistage unification**, NOT at HMM detection (steps 6-10). R1 never actually checked step14 vs upstream. The chicken-and-egg story doesn't apply to the missing amplicons because SAG40 + SAG41 (the only stage-1 samples that could pollute the median) almost certainly do NOT have bumps at the locations of the 9 missing amplicons — their bumps are concentrated at the major early-firing amplicons II/9A and II/2B (with possible slight bumps on the taller ones). The other 7-9 missing-from-step14 II amplicons are NOT being normalized away by stage-1 pollution. **Different defect class entirely.**

#### New Stage A.5 — Investigate HMM step14 multistage-unification filtering

**Stage A.5.a:** Read `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/14-multistage-unification/` and identify what's at step14 input vs output. Likely candidate inputs: per-stage trajectory files from step12-13. Likely candidate outputs: `hmm_unified_calls_aps.bed` (3 II rows) + `all_trajectories.multistage_unification.tsv` (or similar).

**Stage A.5.b:** Identify the multistage-unification logic in `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py` (likely in or adjacent to `run_step14_*` or similar; SPEC15.5 territory). Trace the filter chain: shape-filter at step14 (HMM `step14_shape_score_threshold`), gap-analysis filter (cycle 15.4b SPEC15.22 step15 — but step15 fires AFTER step14, so not the immediate cause), or a multistage-unification-internal filter (e.g., requiring N≥k stages of presence, requiring shape filter PASS at last stage, requiring overlap-resolution survival).

**Stage A.5.c:** For each of the 9 missing II amplicons (the 12 step13 II amplicons MINUS the 3 step14 II amplicons), find the rejection reason. Likely candidates: (i) failed shape filter at step14; (ii) failed multistage stage-coverage threshold (e.g., didn't appear in N≥k stages); (iii) deduplicated/merged into one of the 3 surviving amplicons via overlap resolution; (iv) some other gating.

**Stage A.5.d:** Cross-check: does Growth pipeline (or RMS pipeline) running on the same data lose the same 9 II amplicons at its analogous step? If YES — defect is in shared logic. If NO — defect is HMM-specific. Run by-eye comparison vs `tests/full_chrom_training_data/amplicons.by-eye.bed`.

**Stage A.5 deliverable:** Append a "Stage A.5 report" subsection to this audit log section explaining (a) what filtered the 9 missing amplicons; (b) whether the filter is correct-but-mistuned vs broken; (c) cross-pipeline applicability of the defect. Repair contract Stage B.7 is conditional on this report.

#### New Stage A.6 — Test detector residual-signal capability

**Stage A.6.a:** Construct a synthetic test fixture: a sample with a small (but real) bump matching the magnitude of SAG40's post-normalization residual at II/9A. The exact magnitude can be measured from `01-prior/01-hmm/05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/` SAG40 RCN at II/9A bin range.

**Stage A.6.b:** Pass the fixture through `flat_sample.compute_flat_sample_sidecar` with default thresholds. Verify whether the detector classifies the synthetic sample as flat (BUG: detector failed to detect residual) or as not-flat (OK: detector worked; residual was below detection threshold).

**Stage A.6.c:** If the detector failed to detect the residual: trace why. Possible causes — (i) `fold_threshold=1.25` is too high to catch a residual that's smaller than the pre-normalization signal; (ii) `prob_threshold=0.9` is too high; (iii) `min_flat_fraction=1.0` is the wrong rollup rule (a single non-flat locus should un-flat the sample, but if all loci look flat-ish post-normalization, the rule fires); (iv) actual code bug in the per-locus test logic.

**Stage A.6 deliverable:** Append a "Stage A.6 report" subsection explaining whether the detector correctly handles residual signal + concrete trace of failure if it doesn't. Repair contract Stage B.8 is conditional on this report.

#### Conditional Stage B.7 — Step14 multistage-unification filter fix (scope decision contingent on Stage A.5)

If Stage A.5 reveals a clear bug (e.g., shape filter at step14 is rejecting amplicons with valid shape signatures because the threshold is wrong; or multistage stage-coverage threshold is too strict for real amplicons that legitimately appear in only 2-3 stages):
- **(i) Small-fix scope-fit case** (parameter adjustment, threshold change, narrow logic fix): R2 absorbs into cycle 15.4a-S2 as Stage B.7. Validate via Stage A.5 cross-check + ground-truth comparison; the 9 missing II amplicons should reappear in step14 output.
- **(ii) Large-fix scope-extension case** (architectural change, new SPEC priority required, multi-cycle work): R2 surfaces as a finding (no fix in this cycle); orchestrator scopes a new cycle 15.4a-S3 OR re-opens SPEC15.5 OR defers to a future phase. R2's audit log report explicitly recommends which.

**Cross-pipeline applicability:** if the filter is HMM step14-specific, fix at HMM only; if it's shared logic also affecting Growth/RMS analogous steps, apply cross-pipeline per `feedback_cross_pipeline_parity_blindspot.md` discipline.

#### Conditional Stage B.8 — Detector logic bug fix (scope decision contingent on Stage A.6)

If Stage A.6 reveals the detector fails to catch residual signal:
- Identify whether the failure is threshold-tuning-fixable (adjust defaults) or logic-bug-fixable (code change in `flat_sample.compute_flat_sample_sidecar` per-locus test or rollup logic).
- **Fix in cycle 15.4a-S2 unless the fix requires a redesign** (e.g., new statistical formulation; new feature). Redesign-class fixes defer to a future phase per the cycle's "tune don't redesign" boundary.

If Stage A.6 reveals the detector correctly handles residual signal at the test magnitude (i.e., the residual is genuinely below detection threshold):
- The detector is functioning as designed; user's pushback is fully addressed by Stage B.1's chrom-median path (which exposes a stronger signal than the residual).
- Document this in the Stage A.6 report; no Stage B.8 work needed.

### Pushback on the original scaffold's framing

#### F1 — **Push back on Finding 6:** "APS not emitted by HMM at all" is **factually incorrect on the live tree**.

**Live evidence:** `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:1614-1648` invokes `run_step16_hmm_aps(...)` UNCONDITIONALLY whenever the HMM engine runs and `len(_hmm_non_ref_stages) >= 1`. `run_step16_hmm_aps` at `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:183-386` emits the full APS schema including `onionskin_aps.tsv`, `locus_contributions.tsv`, `flat_samples.tsv`, `amplicon_reliability.tsv`, `aps_matrix_raw.tsv`, `aps_matrix_zscore.tsv`, `aps_clusters.tsv`, `genome_stage_medians/` (per-stage RCN/log2RCN/MAD bedGraphs), and PCA artifacts when feature mode requires them.

User's run output `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/16-aps/` confirms this: it contains `amplicon_reliability.tsv`, `aps_clusters.tsv`, `aps_matrix_raw.tsv`, `aps_matrix_zscore.tsv`, `flat_samples.tsv`, `genome_stage_medians/`, `locus_contributions.tsv`, `onionskin_aps.tsv`. Cycle 15.4a Stage J + cycle 15.6a SPEC15.10/SPEC15.11 already brought HMM APS up to the cross-pipeline-IDENTICAL schema for SPEC15.6 deliverable 5.

**What is actually thinner on HMM:** `aps_amplicon_importance.tsv`, `aps_dendrogram_order_*.tsv`, `aps_order_*.tsv`, `aps_stage_regression_report.tsv`, and `write_aps_plots()` plot outputs. These are tracked in `[SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:7]` and addressed by SPEC15.17 deliverables 12-15 in cycle 15.8a (separately authorized 2026-04-30 scope expansion). **OUT OF SCOPE for cycle 15.4a-S2.**

**Repair instruction for Role 2:** Treat Finding 6's framing as resolved-by-clarification. The actual gap that Finding 6 was reaching for is the HMM-only short-circuit failing to RUN posterior with this APS (Findings 1+2). HMM's APS schema itself is already in place; do NOT re-implement APS for HMM. Do NOT touch `run_step16_hmm_aps` to add APS code paths — the work needed is in `onionskin.py` controller logic only (wire the HMM-only short-circuits to invoke posterior helpers that consume HMM's already-emitted APS at `01-hmm/16-aps/`).

#### F2 — **Reframe Finding 2:** `--compute-aps` controller-gate is selectively absent for HMM, by design

`run_step16_hmm_aps` runs UNCONDITIONALLY inside the HMM engine, NOT gated by `args.compute_aps`. Compare:
- Growth at `onionskin.py:795` (`_phase2_postprocess`): `if getattr(args, "compute_aps", False) and manifest_path:` → APS gated by flag.
- RMS at `onionskin.py:3647-3670` (per `_run_rcn_mean_shift_controller`): also gated by `args.compute_aps`.
- HMM at `engines/hmm_engine.py:1614`: NOT gated by any flag, always runs when `len(_hmm_non_ref_stages) >= 1`.

This pre-existing inconsistency means `--compute-aps off` does not currently suppress HMM APS, and `--compute-aps on` is not strictly required for HMM-only runs to emit APS. The user's command at `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:3]` includes `--compute-aps`, so HMM APS ran cleanly.

**Implication for driver 2:** the HMM-only short-circuit at `onionskin.py:4672-4691` + `:4941-4959` already invokes `_run_hmm_engine`, which already emits HMM APS. The Finding 2 work is NOT "make HMM emit APS" (it does); it is **"make HMM-only short-circuit invoke posterior helpers that read the already-emitted HMM APS"**. The `_run_hmm_posterior` repair surface is the controller, not the engine.

**OPTIONAL hygiene scope decision (R1 surfaces; user decides):** should HMM APS become controller-gated by `args.compute_aps` for cross-pipeline parity? Two readings:
- **Yes:** treats `--compute-aps` as universal across all three pipelines per its help text; aligns HMM with Growth + RMS gating; users opt out of HMM APS by passing `--compute-aps off`.
- **No (current behavior):** HMM APS is upstream of HMM-pipeline outputs that need it (timing, clustering); gating it by a CLI flag risks breaking existing HMM-pipeline contracts. Less risky.

**R1 recommendation:** defer the gating decision OUT of cycle 15.4a-S2 (current behavior preserved); document the asymmetry as `[ISSUE:2026-XX-XX:N]` for future cycle-15.10a SPEC15.20 closeout sweep, alongside `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:2]` and `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:1]` other documented asymmetries. **Do NOT add `args.compute_aps` gate to `run_step16_hmm_aps` in this cycle** — keeping HMM's existing always-on APS emission is necessary for the posterior helper (Stage B.4 below) to find APS files reliably.

---

### Driver 1 audit — Diagnostic question responses (a)-(e)

#### F3 — Diagnostic Q(a): Stage-1 ref-stage normalization median pollution. **CHICKEN-AND-EGG ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED.**

**Live surface:**
- `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:_step2_groupmedians` (line ~280) — computes per-bin median across samples within each stage. **All stage-1 samples contribute to the stage-1 per-bin median, including amplified samples (SAG40 in the user's data).**
- `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:_step4_rcn_norm` (line 475-531) — chromosome-specifically normalizes test groups 2..N by dividing by group 1's stage-median bedGraph. Does NOT touch stage 1 itself; stage 1 in step 5 (`05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/`) appears as RCN-relative-to-stage-1 (which is ~1.0 by construction at the per-bin-median level).
- Per-sample tracks: HMM step 5 emits `01-hmm/05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/` per-sample bedGraphs that are smoothed RCN. These per-sample tracks feed `compute_aps_tables` (downstream of `compute_sample_rcn_tracks_from_track_manifest` at `hmm_ported_analyses.py:239`).

**The chicken-and-egg dependency:**

1. SAG40's amplification at II/9A pollutes the stage-1 per-bin median at II/9A computed in step 2.
2. Step 4 divides stages 2..N by the (polluted) stage-1 median, so stages 2..N RCN at II/9A is artificially LOW.
3. Step 5 smooths the per-sample RCN tracks. SAG40's RCN at II/9A in step 5 is computed against the same polluted stage-1 median (relative to itself + SAG41) — appears as ~1.0 (close to baseline).
4. `run_step16_hmm_aps` reads step 5's per-sample tracks (`sample_tracks_manifest=os.path.join(_indiv_dir(steps["step5"]), "sample_tracks.tsv")`) → builds `contrib_df` with `summit_rcn` column from these tracks.
5. `summit_rcn[SAG40, II/9A]` ≈ 1.0 (post-polluted-normalization).
6. `compute_flat_sample_sidecar` (cycle 15.4a Round 4 deliverable, in `flat_sample.py`) tests `per_locus_flat_test(summit_rcn=1.0)` against `baseline_rcn=1.0` with `fold_threshold=1.25` → returns True (flat).
7. SAG40 tested flat at every locus → `flat_fraction=1.0` ≥ `min_flat_fraction=1.0` → SAG40 flat-classified.
8. Auto-switch-to-chrom-median DOES NOT fire (n_flat=2 > 0) → posterior continues with ref-stage normalization → posterior re-uses SAG40 + SAG41 as anchors → continued summit-position distortion.

**The flat-detection mechanism CANNOT detect this artifact on its own** because the very signal it would detect has already been normalized away.

**Verifiable claim Stage A.1 must check:** SAG40's `summit_rcn` value at II/9A in `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/16-aps/locus_contributions.tsv` should be close to 1.0 (post-polluted-normalization). If not, hypothesis is wrong; if so, hypothesis is confirmed.

**Repair instruction (Stage B.1):** see Stage B section below. Fix path: run flat-detection against an alternative signal that is independent of the (polluted) ref-stage normalization — chrom-median-normalized signal is the cleanest candidate (signal/chrom-median-of-signal at each bin; no inter-sample dependency).

#### F4 — Diagnostic Q(b): Flat-detector permissiveness. **NOT actually permissive — the inputs are corrupted, not the detector logic.**

`flat_sample.compute_flat_sample_sidecar` defaults: `prob_threshold=0.9`, `fold_threshold=1.25`, `min_flat_fraction=1.0`, `detector="bayesian_posterior"`. Logic: a sample is flat-classified iff EVERY reliable locus tests flat (`min_flat_fraction=1.0`).

**This is conservative, not permissive.** Even ONE reliable locus showing growth would un-flat a sample. SAG40's data shows growth at II/9A in the upstream pre-normalization bedGraphs, so per F3 above, the issue is that the post-normalization summit_rcn no longer reflects the raw growth.

**Threshold tuning is NOT the right fix** — tightening prob_threshold or fold_threshold (making the per-locus test MORE strict to call a locus flat) would call FEWER loci flat → MORE loci showing growth → samples LESS likely to be flat-classified. But the loci that need to test as growth are the very loci that have been normalized to baseline by pollution. Tightening doesn't expose the underlying signal.

**Loosening thresholds (lower fold_threshold or lower min_flat_fraction)** would call MORE samples flat — but that doesn't help; SAG40 is already being called flat. The user wants SAG40 NOT to be flat.

**Repair instruction (Stage A.2 + Stage B.1):** see Stage A.2 trace + Stage B.1 fix-path below. Threshold tuning is NOT the answer; the answer is to run the per-locus test against an alternative input signal (chrom-median-normalized RCN, not ref-stage-normalized RCN).

#### F5 — Diagnostic Q(c): Reliability-scorer false-negative amplicons. **NOT the bottleneck.**

The 7 PASSING reliability rows have scores 0.84-0.92 (well above 0.50 default class threshold). The 1 FILTER row (X:9555000-9900000) is filtered by `gap_suspect=True` (cycle 15.4b SPEC15.22 hard-exclude axis), NOT by the reliability score. **The reliability scorer is correctly calling-as-reliable the calls it sees.**

**The 5+ missing chromosome-II amplicons (II.7.2-7.6, II.17.3-17.8, II.19.4-19.7, II-4 = II:41,413,288-41,906,860, II-5 = II:46,060,415-46,458,919, II-6 = II:47,143,836-48,353,675, II-7 = II:54,625,426-55,089,998) are missing UPSTREAM of `run_step16_hmm_aps`** — they're not in the trajectory set that step16 reads from `step14` (multistage-unification output). The reliability sidecar can only score calls that exist; calls that HMM detection + multistage-unification missed are not visible to the scorer.

**Why these amplicons are missing upstream:** likely the same chicken-and-egg root cause — stage-1 ref-stage normalization median pollution suppresses the RCN signal that HMM state-emission detection (step 6) consumes. Bins that should test as amplified state instead test as background → trajectory not formed → no call.

**Repair instruction (Stage A.3):** Stage A audit must confirm whether the missing II amplicons are absent from `01-hmm/14-multistage-unification/all_trajectories.multistage_unification.tsv` (upstream HMM detection failure) OR present-but-filtered downstream (filter-stage failure). The user-supplied run output at `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/14-multistage-unification/` (if present) is the truth source. If absent, the issue is HMM-detection-upstream — fixing it requires changing the prior-phase normalization (Stage B.1's flat-detection-on-chrom-median fix would feed into a posterior-phase auto-switch, which could partially rescue the missing amplicons in posterior outputs but NOT in the prior). **The user's pragmatic fix path is to recommend chrom-median as the durable default for HMM** (per question (d) below) — landing zone is SPEC15.16 cycle 15.7a, but cycle 15.4a-S2 can surface the recommendation.

#### F6 — Diagnostic Q(d): Chrom-median as durable default switch. **Cross-pipeline scope question; SPEC15.16 covers HMM only.**

SPEC15.16 (cycle 15.7a) covers the HMM `--norm-mode chrom-median` validation-driven default switch. Question for cycle 15.4a-S2: should we surface a parallel cross-pipeline scope expansion for Growth + RMS chrom-median default flips?

**R1 recommendation:** NO new cross-pipeline default-flip work in cycle 15.4a-S2. Reason: (1) Growth + RMS already default to `chrom-median` per the existing project convention (`--growth-norm-mode chrom-median` + `--rms-norm-mode chrom-median` are already common defaults — verify by cycle 15.4a-S2 audit code-dive). (2) HMM default-flip is properly scoped to SPEC15.16 / cycle 15.7a. (3) Cycle 15.4a-S2 is "tune don't redesign" — adding cross-pipeline default flips broadens beyond that boundary.

**Cross-pipeline scope decision deferred (R1 raises for user decision):**
- (a) If the user wants HMM chrom-median default flip done IN cycle 15.4a-S2 (vs SPEC15.16 / cycle 15.7a) for faster ground-truth-validation iteration — file as scope-expansion request. R1 weak prior: leave at 15.7a per existing scope.
- (b) If Growth + RMS already default to chrom-median, no new work needed cross-pipeline. R1 weak prior: confirm via Stage A.4 (lightweight) + close as already-done.

#### F7 — Diagnostic Q(e): Dual-mode (ref-stage AND chrom-median) outputs for diagnostic comparison. **STRONGLY RECOMMENDED.**

User's verbatim direction: *"This is all also why I think moving toward chrom-median defaults for all pipelines makes the most sense... or maybe doing both in some cases."*

**R1 recommendation: ADD a `--dual-norm-mode-outputs` (or similar) flag** that, when set, runs the prior-phase APS computation TWICE (once with ref-stage, once with chrom-median) and emits both side-by-side. Output schema additions (per-pipeline):
- `01-prior/01-hmm/16-aps/onionskin_aps.tsv` (existing) + `onionskin_aps.chrom_median.tsv` (NEW dual-mode artifact)
- Same pattern for `locus_contributions`, `flat_samples`, `amplicon_reliability` — emit both ref-stage and chrom-median variants
- Or alternative: a single flag `--aps-dual-norm` adds a `.dual_norm_diag.tsv` summary file comparing ref-stage vs chrom-median per-locus per-sample summit_rcn deltas, without duplicating every artifact.

**The lightweight diagnostic-summary alternative is preferred** for cycle 15.4a-S2 scope ("tune, don't redesign" — adding a complete dual output schema is broader than tune). The summary TSV would have columns: `sample_id, prior_stage, call_id, summit_rcn_ref_stage, summit_rcn_chrom_median, delta, ratio, divergence_flag` where `divergence_flag=True` when the two normalizations disagree on whether the locus shows growth.

**Scope decision (R1 surfaces for user; R2 implements per user direction):**
- **Option A (lightweight diagnostic summary):** new `aps_dual_norm_diag.tsv` per-pipeline. ~30-50 lines new code in `compute_and_write_aps` + `run_step16_hmm_aps`. Adds ONE artifact per pipeline. Easy to implement; high diagnostic value.
- **Option B (full dual schema):** `_chrom_median.tsv` variants of all APS artifacts + flat/reliability sidecars. ~200-400 lines new code; significant testing surface. Better long-term but bigger scope.
- **Option C (out of scope; defer):** the Stage B.1 flat-detection-on-chrom-median fix is ITSELF a partial dual-mode mechanism (the flat-detector consults chrom-median signal); a separate `--dual-norm-mode-outputs` is not needed if Stage B.1 is implemented. Defer dual-mode UX to user adjudication post-Stage-B.

**R1 weak prior: Option C** (Stage B.1 alone delivers the diagnostic value the user is asking for, by routing flat-detection through chrom-median-normalized signal). User can override to A/B if desired.

---

### Driver 2 audit — Diagnostic question responses (f)-(h)

#### F8 — Diagnostic Q(f): HMM-pipeline posterior architecture. **Recommend Option (i) — standalone `_run_hmm_posterior`.**

**Live surface:**
- `_run_posterior` at `onionskin.py:4378-4505` — builds posterior manifest from APS clusters, runs growth multistage + HMM engine + RMS controller all in one. ~125 lines.
- `_run_rcn_posterior` at `onionskin.py:4290-4376` — narrower; runs only RMS arm, optionally HMM engine alongside. ~85 lines.
- HMM-only equivalent: NONE.

**R1 architectural recommendation: Option (i) — write standalone `_run_hmm_posterior` sister helper at ~80-100 lines.** Rationale:
- **Conservative + low-risk.** Adds new code without modifying existing `_run_posterior` / `_run_rcn_posterior` logic. The two existing posterior helpers + the new `_run_hmm_posterior` form a clean 3-pipeline parallel structure that mirrors the prior-phase code paths.
- **Less coupling, easier to audit.** Option (ii) — unified `_run_posterior_for_pipeline(pipeline, ...)` — would require refactoring all three call sites + handling the now-divergent posterior workflows (growth-multistage vs RMS-only vs HMM-only) inside a single function with branching logic. The branching would not be much smaller than the three separate functions.
- **Anticipates SOUP Item 6 future work, not preempts it.** SOUP Item 6 (cross-pipeline structural unification) covers the broader unification of all three posterior helpers. Doing that here couples cycle 15.4a-S2 to a future-phase refactor; better to leave the three helpers as-is and let SOUP Item 6 unify them holistically when that work fires.

**`_run_hmm_posterior` contract:** mirror `_run_rcn_posterior` structure (the narrower of the two existing helpers). Read APS cluster files from `prior_layout["hmm_aps_dir"]` (NEW key — see F10 below); apply per-pipeline auto-switch via the new per-pipeline `_flat_sample_auto_switch_hmm` flag (see F9); call `build_posterior_manifest` with the HMM-pipeline flat_sample_ids; build post_layout with `include_hmm=True, include_growth_model=False, include_rcn_mean_shift=False`; invoke `_run_hmm_engine` on the posterior manifest.

**Caveat:** `build_posterior_manifest` reads APS cluster files. Confirm at Stage B.4 that the cluster file names HMM emits (`aps_clusters.tsv` per `hmm_ported_analyses.py:380`) match what `build_posterior_manifest` expects (`aps_clusters_raw.tsv` per `_POSTERIOR_REQUIRED` list at `onionskin.py:4009`). **There may be a naming mismatch between HMM's `aps_clusters.tsv` and the Growth/RMS `aps_clusters_raw.tsv` + `aps_clusters_zscore.tsv`.** Stage B.4 must reconcile.

#### F9 — Diagnostic Q(g): Per-pipeline flat-sample-state refactor. **Recommend Option (a) — per-pipeline attributes.**

**Live surface:**
- `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch(args, aps_dir, pipeline_name)` at `onionskin.py:97-147` sets `args._flat_sample_auto_switch = True` (single shared flag).
- Called at `onionskin.py:852` (Growth, post-`compute_and_write_aps` in `_phase2_postprocess`), `:3697` (RMS, post-`run_rcn_mean_shift`), `:3962` (HMM, post-`_run_hmm_engine`).
- `_resolve_posterior_norm_mode(args, requested_norm_mode)` at `:175-187` reads single shared flag; consumed by no current code path (helper exists for future use).
- `_run_posterior` at `:4402-4411` reads single shared flag; applies override across `growth_norm_mode` / `rms_norm_mode` / `hmm_norm_mode` simultaneously.
- `_run_rcn_posterior` at `:4290-4376` does NOT read the flag; RMS posterior is not auto-switched. **This is a separate inconsistency the audit surfaces — Stage B.5 should fix this too** so RMS posterior also honors the per-pipeline auto-switch.

**Behavior of the single flag (re-checked):** `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch` only ever SETS the flag to True (never to False). Once set by any pipeline, it stays True for the rest of the run. **The flag is monotonically True — "any-pipeline-with-zero-flats wins globally."** This forces ALL pipelines to chrom-median override even if only one pipeline detected zero flats.

**Concrete divergent-flat-status example (the bug):** HMM detects 0 flats (because of the F3 chicken-and-egg — every locus normalizes to baseline, so the per-locus test reports flat for everyone, but the rollup sees full-flat fraction across all samples — wait that gives many flats, not zero. Need a different scenario.) Better example: Growth detects 5 flat samples (correct; reliable amplicons + correct stage-1 anchor); HMM has its step16 APS raise an exception OR no flat_samples.tsv emitted (n_flat=0 path triggered) → HMM auto-switch fires → all three pipelines forced to chrom-median in posterior. Or the converse: Growth has 0 flat (auto-switch fires); HMM has 4 flat (no auto-switch from HMM); flag sticks True from Growth → HMM also switched to chrom-median in posterior even though HMM had genuine flat anchors.

**R1 recommendation: Option (a) — per-pipeline attributes** `args._flat_sample_auto_switch_growth`, `args._flat_sample_auto_switch_rms`, `args._flat_sample_auto_switch_hmm`. Rationale:
- **Minimal disruption to existing code.** `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch(args, aps_dir, pipeline_name)` already takes `pipeline_name` parameter; just thread it into the attribute name (`f"_flat_sample_auto_switch_{pipeline_name.replace('-','_')}"`).
- **Matches existing project style.** Other per-pipeline state (e.g., `--growth-norm-mode`, `--rms-norm-mode`, `--hmm-norm-mode`) is per-pipeline-attribute, not dict-of-pipelines.
- **Future-extensible.** Adding new per-pipeline state (the broader theme Finding 3 implies) is a one-attribute-per-call pattern; consistent.
- **Reading `_resolve_posterior_norm_mode`** becomes pipeline-aware: `_resolve_posterior_norm_mode(args, requested_norm_mode, pipeline_name)` reads `getattr(args, f"_flat_sample_auto_switch_{pipeline_name.replace('-','_')}", False)`.

Option (b) (dict) is functionally equivalent but introduces mutable-dict-on-args pattern that doesn't match existing style. Option (c) (per-pipeline-state object) is over-engineered for current scope; defer until a second per-pipeline state attribute emerges and a refactor justifies a dataclass.

**Cross-pipeline parity sweep result (Q(h)):** see F10 below.

#### F10 — Diagnostic Q(h): Cross-pipeline state-divergence sweep (Item 6c discipline). **Only `_flat_sample_auto_switch` is affected.**

**Sweep scope:** all `args._<state>` attributes set in pipeline-specific call sites and consumed shared at controller-level. Method: `grep -nE "args\._[a-zA-Z_]+" onionskin.py`.

**Sweep result (16 hits across the file):**
- `args._flat_sample_auto_switch` — **Per-pipeline-set, shared-consumed. THIS IS FINDING 3.** Repaired in Stage B.5.
- `args._shared_gap_analysis_done` (`onionskin.py:246`) — set ONCE in `_prepare_shared_gap_analysis`; controller-level guard for the shared gap mask. NOT per-pipeline-set; correct as a single shared flag.
- `args._shared_gap_analysis_grouping_dir` (`:247`) — same pattern; correct.
- `args._shared_gap_mask_bed` (`:248`) — same pattern; correct.
- `args._shared_gap_bedgraphs` (`:249`) — same pattern; correct.
- `args._active_pipelines` (`:4652`, `:4932`) — set ONCE at controller entry; correct as single state.

**No additional findings from the sweep beyond `_flat_sample_auto_switch`.** The other `args._<state>` attributes are genuinely controller-level (set-once, consumed-once or read-only). The Item 6c discipline note in the audit log is: this sweep is clean post-Stage-B.5; no future-phase issue surfaces from this round.

#### F11 — **NEW finding:** `_run_rcn_posterior` does not honor `_flat_sample_auto_switch` at all.

**Live surface:** `_run_rcn_posterior` at `onionskin.py:4290-4376` builds posterior manifest + runs HMM engine (if active) + runs RMS controller. **Never reads `args._flat_sample_auto_switch` (or any per-pipeline variant).** No norm-mode override applied for RMS posterior.

**Compare:** `_run_posterior` at `:4402-4411` correctly reads the flag and applies overrides.

**Severity:** medium-S2. RMS-only `--posterior` runs do not auto-switch when 0 flat samples are detected → posterior continues to use ref-stage normalization on a polluted anchor. Same defect class as Driver 2 Finding 1 (HMM-only `--posterior` no-op), but for RMS-only.

**Repair instruction (Stage B.5 extension):** when refactoring `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch` to per-pipeline state (Stage B.5), ALSO add per-pipeline auto-switch consumption to `_run_rcn_posterior` (read `args._flat_sample_auto_switch_rms` + `args._flat_sample_auto_switch_hmm`; override `args.rms_norm_mode` + `args.hmm_norm_mode` if either is True and the requested mode is `ref-stage`).

**Out-of-scope check:** this finding fits cycle 15.4a-S2 scope (it's the same defect class as Finding 3 + Findings 1+2, just for a different pipeline path). Fix in-scope. Do NOT defer to a separate cycle.

### Stage A — Diagnostic procedure (Role 2 contract)

#### Stage A.1 — Confirm chicken-and-egg root cause from F3

1. **A.1.a:** Read `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/16-aps/locus_contributions.tsv`. Filter rows where `sample_id == '1-SAG40_le8x4_rep1_quant.TPM'` and look at the `summit_rcn` column. If `summit_rcn ≈ 1.0` (within 0.05) at the II/9A locus (II:5975000-6515000 — the locus that DID survive into the reliability sidecar), the chicken-and-egg hypothesis is confirmed.
2. **A.1.b:** Compare to SAG40's RCN at II/9A in `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/` (per-sample bedGraph). If the per-sample bedGraph also shows ~1.0 at II/9A, the pollution-distortion is in step 5 (post-step-4 ref-stage normalization). If the bedGraph shows >1.25 but `summit_rcn` reports ~1.0, the distortion is in `compute_aps_tables`'s sample track loading — investigate.
3. **A.1.c:** Run a chrom-median variant: invoke the toy fixture or user fixture with `--norm-mode chrom-median` for HMM (`--hmm-norm-mode chrom-median`); confirm SAG40's `summit_rcn` at II/9A in the chrom-median run is genuinely > 1.25. If yes, the chrom-median signal is the right input for flat-detection.

#### Stage A.2 — Confirm the missing II amplicons are absent upstream of `run_step16_hmm_aps`

1. **A.2.a:** Read `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/14-multistage-unification/all_trajectories.multistage_unification.tsv` (or analogous `step14` trajectory output). Count rows whose locus interval falls on chromosome II. If count ~3-4, then upstream HMM detection is missing the amplicons (matches the 3 II amplicons in the reliability sidecar). If count ~10+, then they're being filtered between step14 and step16.
2. **A.2.b:** If the trajectories ARE present at step14 but missing at step16: investigate the gap-analysis filter (cycle 15.4b SPEC15.22 step15) — but the user's reliability sidecar shows only 1 gap_suspect filter (X:9555000-9900000), suggesting most filtering is NOT at step15.
3. **A.2.c:** If the trajectories are NOT present at step14: the issue is at HMM detection (steps 6-10) — confirms the chicken-and-egg root cause is far upstream. Document this as the structural-fix-out-of-scope finding (same upstream root cause as F3, deeper consequence).

#### Stage A.3 — Confirm flat-detection on chrom-median-normalized signal would un-flat SAG40

1. **A.3.a:** Run `python -m onionskin_core.flat_sample` (or equivalent test invocation) on the user's `locus_contributions.tsv` with `--hmm-norm-mode chrom-median`-normalized contrib_df instead of ref-stage-normalized. Confirm SAG40 at II/9A tests as growth (not flat) when summit_rcn comes from the chrom-median-normalized signal.
2. **A.3.b:** If A.3.a confirms, the Stage B.1 fix path is validated (flat-detection on chrom-median signal de-couples flat-classification from ref-stage anchor pollution).

#### Stage A.4 — Cross-pipeline default chrom-median check (lightweight)

1. **A.4.a:** Inspect `_resolve_growth_norm_mode`, `_resolve_rcn_mean_shift_norm_mode`, `_resolve_hmm_norm_mode` in `onionskin.py`. Confirm Growth + RMS default to chrom-median when no per-pipeline flag is set; confirm HMM defaults to ref-stage (per SPEC15.16 still pending).
2. **A.4.b:** Document the current cross-pipeline default state in DECISIONS.md `[2026-04-30] Phase 15 cycle 15.4a-S2 — cross-pipeline norm-mode default audit` entry. This is an evidence-only entry; SPEC15.16 cycle 15.7a is the right place for HMM default-flip implementation.

---

### Stage B — Repair contracts (Role 2 implementation surface)

#### Stage B.1 (driver 1) — Decouple flat-detection from ref-stage normalization pollution

**File: `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py`** + **call-site updates in `onionskin_core/aps.py`** (Growth + RMS APS) + **`onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:run_step16_hmm_aps`** (HMM APS).

**Mechanism:** `compute_flat_sample_sidecar` accepts a NEW optional kwarg `chrom_median_contrib_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None`. When provided, the per-locus flat test consumes `summit_rcn` from `chrom_median_contrib_df` (chrom-median-normalized) instead of the default ref-stage-normalized `contrib_df`. When not provided, falls back to current behavior (preserves backward-compat for callers that don't compute chrom-median signal).

**At each call site:**
- Growth `compute_and_write_aps` at `aps.py` calls `compute_sample_rcn_tracks(manifest, norm_mode="ref-stage", ref_stage=N)` for the primary contrib_df. **NEW:** if `flat_sample_use_chrom_median=True` (new arg, default True per R1 recommendation), ALSO call `compute_sample_rcn_tracks(manifest, norm_mode="chrom-median")` to build a chrom-median contrib_df + pass it to `compute_flat_sample_sidecar`'s new kwarg.
- HMM `run_step16_hmm_aps` at `hmm_ported_analyses.py:239` already calls `compute_sample_rcn_tracks_from_track_manifest(sample_tracks_manifest)` (which reads HMM's already-normalized step5 tracks). **NEW:** also call `compute_sample_rcn_tracks(manifest, norm_mode="chrom-median")` (using the raw manifest, not the HMM-normalized tracks) → build chrom-median contrib_df → pass to `compute_flat_sample_sidecar`. The chrom-median signal is CHEAP (no HMM pipeline needed) and gives a normalization-anchor-independent baseline.
- RMS `run_rcn_mean_shift` at `engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` — same pattern.

**Default for the new `flat_sample_use_chrom_median` flag: True.** Reason: ref-stage-pollution-induced false-positive flat calls are the user's verified defect; defaulting to chrom-median signal for flat-detection is the correct semantic. CLI flag `--flat-sample-norm-mode {chrom-median,ref-stage,both}` exposes the choice; default `chrom-median`.

**Validation:** Stage A.3 confirms this decouples SAG40's flat-classification from ref-stage pollution. Regression test 1 (`test_flat_sample_groundtruth.py`) constructs a synthetic SAG40-like fixture and asserts flat-classification matches expected (SAG40 = NOT flat under chrom-median signal).

#### Stage B.2 (driver 1) — Auto-switch trigger refinement (CONDITIONAL; depends on Stage B.1 outcome)

After Stage B.1, the auto-switch trigger condition can stay at `n_flat == 0` (current behavior) OR be refined. R1's recommendation: **keep the trigger at `n_flat == 0`** post-Stage-B.1, because Stage B.1 fixes the false-positive flat calls — SAG40 will correctly NOT be flat-classified, so the count of true-flats will be lower, and the auto-switch will fire correctly when no genuine flat-sample anchor exists. No threshold tuning needed.

**No-op deliverable contractually unless Stage A.3 reveals the auto-switch trigger should be refined.** R2 makes the call after Stage A's diagnostic outcomes are known.

#### Stage B.3 (driver 1; OPTIONAL — user adjudication) — Dual-mode diagnostic summary

Implement Option C (R1 weak prior): defer to user adjudication. If user wants Option A (lightweight `aps_dual_norm_diag.tsv` per-pipeline), implement; if Option C (rely on Stage B.1 alone), skip.

**Default action for Role 2: SKIP unless user redirects.** The Stage B.1 flat-detection-on-chrom-median fix addresses the diagnostic-value ask implicitly; explicit dual-mode UX is incremental.

#### Stage B.4 (driver 2) — `_run_hmm_posterior` standalone helper

**File: `onionskin.py`.**

**Add new function** at `onionskin.py:~4290` (sibling to `_run_rcn_posterior`):

```python
def _run_hmm_posterior(prior_layout: dict, args, chromosomes_override: Optional[str] = None) -> None:
    """Build a posterior manifest from standalone HMM APS clusters and re-run the HMM arm.

    Parallel to ``_run_rcn_posterior`` (RMS-only) and ``_run_posterior`` (multi-pipeline
    with growth at primary). HMM-only re-run consumes ``01-hmm/16-aps/`` cluster files
    via the new ``hmm_aps_dir`` layout key.
    """
    if not getattr(args, "compute_aps", False):
        print(
            "[onionskin] --posterior requires --compute-aps (APS cluster files not found). "
            "Re-run with both flags.",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return

    aps_dir = prior_layout["hmm_aps_dir"]  # NEW key — see Stage B.6
    out_dir = prior_layout["out_dir"]

    # Per-pipeline auto-switch (Stage B.5):
    if getattr(args, "_flat_sample_auto_switch_hmm", False):
        if getattr(args, "hmm_norm_mode", None) == "ref-stage":
            args.hmm_norm_mode = "chrom-median"
            print(
                "[flat-sample] Posterior auto-switch: hmm_norm_mode ref-stage → "
                "chrom-median (0 flat samples in HMM prior APS).",
                file=sys.stderr,
            )

    post_layout = build_output_layout(out_dir=out_dir, out_prefix=getattr(args, "out_prefix", None), grouping="02-posterior")
    ensure_layout_dirs(post_layout, include_growth_model=False, include_signal=False, include_rcn_mean_shift=False, include_hmm=True)

    posterior_manifest = os.path.join(post_layout["hmm_dir"], "posterior_manifest.tsv")
    hires_list = [h.strip() for h in (args.hires_manifest.split(",") if args.hires_manifest else []) if h.strip()]
    hires_post_paths = [os.path.join(post_layout["hmm_dir"], f"posterior_hires_manifest_{i}.tsv") for i in range(len(hires_list))]

    flat_ids_for_anchor = _read_flat_sample_ids_from_sidecar(aps_dir)
    try:
        build_posterior_manifest(
            aps_dir=aps_dir,
            prior_manifest_path=args.manifest,
            out_path=posterior_manifest,
            hires_manifest_paths=hires_list or None,
            hires_out_paths=hires_post_paths or None,
            flat_sample_ids=flat_ids_for_anchor,
        )
    except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as exc:
        print(f"[onionskin] HMM posterior manifest build failed: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
        return

    posterior_samples = read_manifest(posterior_manifest)
    _prepare_shared_gap_analysis(args, post_layout, posterior_samples, chromosomes_override)
    rc = _run_hmm_engine(
        args, post_layout, manifest_path=posterior_manifest, manifest_samples=posterior_samples,
        chromosomes_override=parse_csv_list(chromosomes_override) if chromosomes_override else None,
    )
    if rc != 0:
        print(f"[onionskin] HMM posterior run exited with code {rc}", file=sys.stderr)
    else:
        log.verbose("[onionskin] HMM posterior pipeline complete")
```

**Wire in two HMM-only short-circuits:**
- `onionskin.py:4672-4691` (multistage HMM-only): after `_run_hmm_engine` returns, before the `try: _write_output_readme(layout) ... return 0` block, insert:
  ```python
  if getattr(args, "posterior", False):
      _run_hmm_posterior(layout, args, chromosomes_override=shared_chromosomes)
  ```
- `onionskin.py:4941-4959` (single/two-stage HMM-only): same pattern after `_run_hmm_engine`.

**Naming reconciliation (Stage B.4 sub-contract):** verify `build_posterior_manifest` reads `aps_clusters_raw.tsv` per `_POSTERIOR_REQUIRED = ["aps_clusters_raw.tsv", "aps_order_area.tsv"]` at `onionskin.py:4009`. HMM emits `aps_clusters.tsv` (not `aps_clusters_raw.tsv`) per `hmm_ported_analyses.py:380`. Either:
- (a) Rename HMM's `aps_clusters.tsv` → `aps_clusters_raw.tsv` for cross-pipeline parity. **PREFERRED** — aligns with Growth + RMS naming, eliminates the asymmetry. Migration: also keep a backward-compat copy at the old name for one cycle to ease external consumers, with a deprecation comment.
- (b) Extend `_POSTERIOR_REQUIRED` to accept either naming. **NOT preferred** — perpetuates the asymmetry.
- (c) Update `build_posterior_manifest` to be HMM-aware and read `aps_clusters.tsv` for HMM. Same NOT preferred reason.

**R1 strong recommendation: Option (a).** Verify with Stage A audit that nothing externally consumes `aps_clusters.tsv` directly (grep + tracking-files audit).

#### Stage B.5 (driver 2) — Per-pipeline `_flat_sample_auto_switch_<pipeline>` refactor

**File: `onionskin.py`.**

1. **Update `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch`** at `:97-147`:
   ```python
   def _check_flat_sample_auto_switch(args, aps_dir: str, pipeline_name: str) -> None:
       # ... existing body ...
       if n_flat > 0:
           return
       allow_override = bool(getattr(args, "allow_nonflat_ref_stage", False))
       if allow_override:
           # ... existing warning ...
           return
       # ... existing print ...
       attr_name = f"_flat_sample_auto_switch_{pipeline_name.replace('-', '_')}"
       setattr(args, attr_name, True)
       # Backward-compat: also set the old shared flag so any code still reading
       # it sees the union behavior. Remove in cycle 15.10a SPEC15.20 closeout
       # once all consumers are migrated.
       setattr(args, "_flat_sample_auto_switch", True)
   ```
   The backward-compat shared-flag write keeps existing single-flag readers (e.g., `_resolve_posterior_norm_mode` if any consumer remains) functional during the migration. Stage B.5 closeout adds a TODO comment for cycle 15.10a SPEC15.20 to remove the shared flag entirely.

2. **Update `_resolve_posterior_norm_mode`** at `:175-187`:
   ```python
   def _resolve_posterior_norm_mode(args, requested_norm_mode: Optional[str], pipeline_name: str = "growth") -> Optional[str]:
       attr_name = f"_flat_sample_auto_switch_{pipeline_name.replace('-', '_')}"
       if not getattr(args, attr_name, False):
           return requested_norm_mode
       if requested_norm_mode == "ref-stage":
           return "chrom-median"
       return requested_norm_mode
   ```
   Default `pipeline_name="growth"` preserves existing call sites that don't pass it (no caller currently does, per audit grep). New call sites must pass it.

3. **Update `_run_posterior`** at `:4402-4411`:
   ```python
   for attr_pipeline_norm in (
       ("growth", "growth_norm_mode"),
       ("rcn-mean-shift", "rms_norm_mode"),
       ("hmm", "hmm_norm_mode"),
   ):
       pipeline_name, norm_attr = attr_pipeline_norm
       switch_attr = f"_flat_sample_auto_switch_{pipeline_name.replace('-', '_')}"
       if not getattr(args, switch_attr, False):
           continue
       current = getattr(args, norm_attr, None)
       if current == "ref-stage":
           setattr(args, norm_attr, "chrom-median")
           print(
               f"[flat-sample] Posterior auto-switch: {norm_attr} ref-stage → "
               f"chrom-median (0 flat samples in {pipeline_name} prior APS).",
               file=sys.stderr,
           )
   # Also handle the controller-level args.norm_mode (any pipeline auto-switch
   # forces controller-level chrom-median fallback if controller-level was ref-stage):
   if any(
       getattr(args, f"_flat_sample_auto_switch_{p.replace('-', '_')}", False)
       for p in ("growth", "rcn-mean-shift", "hmm")
   ):
       if getattr(args, "norm_mode", None) == "ref-stage":
           args.norm_mode = "chrom-median"
   ```

4. **Update `_run_rcn_posterior`** at `:4290-4376` (per F11): add per-pipeline auto-switch consumption block at the start (after `if not getattr(args, "compute_aps", False)` gate), reading `_flat_sample_auto_switch_rms` + `_flat_sample_auto_switch_hmm` and overriding `args.rms_norm_mode` + `args.hmm_norm_mode` if ref-stage.

5. **Update `_run_hmm_posterior`** (Stage B.4 deliverable): per-pipeline auto-switch consumption is built-in per the snippet above.

#### Stage B.6 (driver 2; minimal layout change) — Add `hmm_aps_dir` layout key

**File: `onionskin_core/output_layout.py:243-294`.**

**Add ONE new key:**
```python
layout = {
    # ... existing keys ...
    "hmm_dir": os.path.join(grouping_dir, "01-hmm"),
    "hmm_aps_dir": os.path.join(grouping_dir, "01-hmm", "16-aps"),  # NEW
    # ... existing keys ...
}
```

**Out of scope:** the broader Growth-anchored output layout refactor (Finding 5; SOUP Item 6b) — do NOT add `hmm_dir` parallel keys for `hmm_calls_dir`, `hmm_summit_refinement_dir`, etc. The cycle's minimal scope expansion is exactly ONE new key (`hmm_aps_dir`) needed for `_run_hmm_posterior` to find HMM's APS at `01-hmm/16-aps/`.

**Tracking note:** add a comment at the new key: `# Cycle 15.4a-S2 minimal layout addition; broader Growth-anchored refactor stays in CROSS_PIPELINE_UNIFICATION_SOUP Item 6b.`

---

### Stage C — Regression test contracts

#### Stage C.1 — `tests/test_flat_sample_groundtruth.py` (NEW)

Synthetic SAG40-like fixture asserting flat-classification matches expected outcomes:

1. **Test 1:** SAG40-shape signal (visible amplification at one locus, baseline elsewhere) → `compute_flat_sample_sidecar` with `chrom_median_contrib_df` provided → SAG40 NOT flat-classified (because the amplified locus correctly tests as growth).
2. **Test 2:** Same SAG40-shape signal → `compute_flat_sample_sidecar` WITHOUT `chrom_median_contrib_df` (using only the ref-stage-polluted contrib_df) → SAG40 IS flat-classified (reproducing current bug behavior).
3. **Test 3:** Genuinely flat sample (uniform RCN ~1.0 across all loci) → `compute_flat_sample_sidecar` with chrom_median path → flat-classified (correct).
4. **Test 4:** Reliability scorer on the SAG40-fixture's `amplicon_reliability.tsv` outputs ≥10 amplicons (matching by-eye expected count for chromosome II in `tests/full_chrom_training_data/amplicons.by-eye.bed`). NOTE: this test depends on Stage A.2 outcome — if upstream HMM detection is the bottleneck, this test may need to be a Stage-A-finding-test rather than a Stage-B-fix-test. R2 implementer adjusts test scope if needed.

#### Stage C.2 — `tests/test_hmm_only_posterior.py` (NEW)

End-to-end `--pipelines hmm --posterior --compute-aps` invocation against the toy fixture:

1. **Test 1:** Run `python onionskin.py --manifest tests/toy_dataset/manifest_5kb.tsv --pipelines hmm --chromosomes II --out-dir <tmp>/hmm_only_posterior --compute-aps --posterior --hmm-norm-mode chrom-median --growth-fit-method linear`. Assert exit code 0. Assert `<tmp>/hmm_only_posterior/02-posterior/01-hmm/` exists. Assert `<tmp>/hmm_only_posterior/02-posterior/01-hmm/posterior_manifest.tsv` exists.
2. **Test 2:** Same invocation but with `--hmm-norm-mode ref-stage` and synthesize a fixture where 0 flat samples are detected → assert `[flat-sample] Posterior auto-switch: hmm_norm_mode ref-stage → chrom-median` log message appears in stderr. Assert posterior runs with chrom-median (verified by examining the post_layout's HMM step5 outputs).

#### Stage C.3 — `tests/test_per_pipeline_flat_sample_state.py` (NEW)

Divergent-flat-status fixture verifying per-pipeline auto-switch decision is applied per-pipeline:

1. **Test 1:** Construct a synthetic test where HMM detects 0 flat samples (auto-switch fires) but RMS detects 5 flat samples (auto-switch does NOT fire). Run with `--pipelines hmm,rcn-mean-shift --posterior --compute-aps`. Assert HMM posterior is run with chrom-median override (`args.hmm_norm_mode == "chrom-median"`); assert RMS posterior is run with the user's requested mode (e.g., `ref-stage` if set, no override).
2. **Test 2:** Reverse — Growth detects 0 flats, HMM detects 4 flats. Assert Growth posterior overridden, HMM not overridden.
3. **Test 3:** All three pipelines detect 0 flats. Assert all three overridden.
4. **Test 4:** No pipeline detects 0 flats. Assert no override applied.

These tests verify the per-pipeline-attribute fix from Stage B.5 functions correctly. Use mock `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch` calls to construct the divergent state without running full pipelines.

- Flat-sample auto-switch decision is applied per-pipeline (Stage B.5).

### 2026-04-30 — Cycle scaffold + concrete evidence bundle from user

**Authors:** John M. Urban (decisions + evidence); Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max) (transcription).

**Status:** OPEN — queued. R1 audit not yet fired. Cycle 15.6b must close before this cycle fires (cycle 15.6b's strategy-selection determinism fix is load-bearing for cluster-stability-across-runs which the flat-detector tuning + reliability-scorer threshold calibration consume — without determinism, threshold calibration to ground-truth is confounded by run-to-run noise).

**Scope (locked at amendment authorization, 2026-04-30):** Tune existing SPEC15.6 deliverables (Bayesian per-locus flat-sample test in `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py`; cross-pipeline reliability scorer in `onionskin_core/reliability.py`; thresholds + posterior stage-1 anchoring + auto-switch-to-chrom-median controller logic in `onionskin.py`) to real ground-truth data. Detector REDESIGN is OUT OF SCOPE — design seeding for an alternative principled-thresholding / triangularity-BIC flat-detector that user added to `[ISSUE:2026-04-14:1]` "Ideas" subsection is future-phase work. GC-aware chrom-median normalization is OUT OF SCOPE per `multi-agent/plans/next/GECKO_SOUP.md`. See `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_STRATEGY.md` amendment-log dated 2026-04-30 for the 5-point scope-boundary lock.

**Concrete data evidence (carried forward from `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:3]` filing 2026-04-30 10:10 AM EST):**

User-supplied run output at `dev/share/res20260430-1/`. Specific surface examples:

1. **SAG40 + SAG41 II/9A signal at multiple HMM step paths:**
   - `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/` (post-step-5 median smoothing): SAG40 has a visible bump at the II/9A summit position — concrete early-active-origin signal. Despite this, the flat-detector at cycle 15.4a SPEC15.6 deliverable 3 calls SAG40 flat. This violates the user's stated intent (*"the exact opposite of what we want"* when flat-classified amplified samples are used as ref-stage in the posterior phase).
   - `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/` (same dir): SAG41 has a "negative bump" at the SAME II/9A bp position. This is an artifact of stage-1 ref-stage normalization median pollution: SAG40's real signal raises the stage-1 per-bin median at the II/9A position; SAG41 (also stage-1 in the manifest) appears below the polluted median at the same position, producing artificial below-baseline RCN values.
   - `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/04-chrom-renorm/indiv_samples/` (post-step-4 ref-stage chrom-renormalization): the negative bump in SAG41 is ALREADY visible here — confirms the ref-stage-renorm step is where the artifact originates (not a smoothing artifact from step 5).
   - `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/03-removeZeroBins/indiv_samples/` (pre-ref-stage-renorm): SAG40 amplification is even more obvious here without normalization. Possibly SAG41 has a tiny bit of amplification too — strict-vs-permissive flat-classification is ambiguous on SAG41 without further analysis.

2. **`amplicon_reliability.tsv` returns too few amplicons:**
   - `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/16-aps/amplicon_reliability.tsv`: 8 amplicons. By-eye expected on chromosome II alone: 10-13 amplicons. There are at least 5-7 confident ones on II. Reliability scorer's thresholds appear too strict, producing false-negative amplicons.

3. **Comparison reference:** `tests/full_chrom_training_data/amplicons.by-eye.bed` is the ground-truth fixture for chromosome II reliability comparison.

**Specific defects observed:**

| Defect | Evidence path | Class |
|---|---|---|
| SAG40 called flat despite II/9A summit-region amplification | `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/` | Flat-detector too permissive (false-positive flat call) |
| SAG41 has artificial negative bump at II/9A from stage-1 median pollution | `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/04-chrom-renorm/indiv_samples/` | Stage-1 ref-stage normalization median is contaminated by amplified samples |
| Posterior phase distortion if flat-misclassified samples used as ref-stage | (downstream effect of above) | Auto-switch-to-chrom-median controller (cycle 15.4a Round 4 deliverable) may not be triggering correctly |
| 8 amplicons in `amplicon_reliability.tsv` vs by-eye 10-13 on II | `dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/16-aps/amplicon_reliability.tsv` | Reliability scorer thresholds too strict (false-negative amplicons) |

**User direction (verbatim 2026-04-30):**

> *"address while we are in Phase 15 so that the net effect of Phase 15 is that it behaves how we intended it to."*
>
> *"This is exactly what we are trying to prevent. The input manifests can have errors like this in the 'prior' stage that we are trying to detect to then come up with a better posterior manifest and better results in the posterior phase. The little bump at the summit position in SAG40 is a very early active origin signal... and that is being used to distort the summit positions when ref-stage normalized."*
>
> *"This is all also why I think moving toward chrom-median defaults for all pipelines makes the most sense... or maybe doing both in some cases. The thing we miss out on when we don't do ref-stage norm is accounting for all the technical biases that change the shape of the data — various GC biases and sequence prep biases, etc. Ref-stage normalization helps 'subtract' those out, but chrom-median does not."*

**Diagnostic questions cycle 15.4a-S2 R1 audit must address:**

(a) **Stage-1 median pollution by amplified samples.** Is the stage-1 ref-stage normalization computing the median over ALL stage-1 samples without first identifying + excluding amplified ones? Verify by direct inspection of `onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py` (or wherever ref-stage median is computed) + flag which samples contribute to stage-1 median + whether the auto-switch-to-chrom-median controller from cycle 15.4a Round 4 is triggering correctly.

(b) **Flat-detector permissiveness on early-amplification samples.** Why is SAG40 called flat despite visible II/9A amplification? Trace `flat_sample.compute_flat_sample_sidecar` against SAG40's signal at the II/9A bp range and identify which threshold rejects classification. Is the per-locus Bayesian ODW transition test using too-permissive `--flat-sample-prob-threshold` (default 0.9) or `--flat-sample-fold-threshold` (default 1.25)? Or is the sample-level rollup `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction` (default 1.0) too forgiving (i.e., requiring ALL loci to test flat is the opposite — TOO STRICT to call flat — but the ISSUE is FALSE-POSITIVE flat calls, so this needs more careful tracing)?

(c) **Reliability-scorer false-negative amplicons.** Why does `amplicon_reliability.tsv` return only 8 amplicons when by-eye expects 10-13? Is the `reliability_score >= class_threshold` filter at `reliability.py:178-185` applied with too-strict class thresholds? Are too many amplicons being filtered as `gap_suspect` (cycle 15.4b SPEC15.22 hard-exclude) when they shouldn't be? Cross-check the `amplicon_reliability.tsv` row-by-row against the by-eye fixture for the missing 2-5 amplicons.

(d) **Chrom-median as durable default switch.** SPEC15.16 (cycle 15.7a) covers HMM `--norm-mode chrom-median` validation-driven default switch. Is the user's question — *"should chrom-median be default for all pipelines"* — already on the answer-track via SPEC15.16, or does cycle 15.4a-S2 need to surface a cross-pipeline scope expansion? Likely answer: SPEC15.16 covers HMM; cycle 15.4a-S2 may surface the same question for Growth + RMS as a cycle-15.10a or future-phase consideration.

(e) **Dual-mode (ref-stage AND chrom-median) outputs for diagnostic comparison.** Should cycle 15.4a-S2 add a flag/mode that emits BOTH ref-stage-normalized AND chrom-median-normalized outputs side-by-side, so users can compare and the posterior-phase manifest decision is informed by both? Useful diagnostic surface but adds output schema scope. R1 to weigh.

**Cross-pipeline parity guard:** Same as cycle 15.6b — flat-detector + reliability-scorer fixes must apply cross-pipeline (Growth + RMS + HMM); no X-pipeline-only patches. The user-supplied evidence is HMM-pipeline-specific (`dev/share/res20260430-1/01-prior/01-hmm/`), but the underlying SPEC15.6 deliverables are cross-pipeline.

**Validation gates required at cycle close:**
- New regression test (e.g., `tests/test_flat_sample_groundtruth.py` or similar) — synthetic fixture that mirrors the SAG40 / SAG41 pattern + asserts flat-classification + reliability-scoring outcomes match expected.
- `make test` — full suite.
- `make toy` — clean re-run.
- `make twin` + `make full-twin`.
- `make puff-compare` — **CRITICAL gate** (HMM detection bit-for-bit unchanged by flat-detector + reliability-scorer threshold calibration).
- Cross-check final outputs at `dev/share/res20260430-1/` re-run against `tests/full_chrom_training_data/amplicons.by-eye.bed` — manual user-eyes verification of resolved defects.

**Exit conditions:**
- SAG40 (or equivalent test sample with early II/9A amplification) is NOT called flat.
- SAG41 negative-bump artifact is either resolved (by stage-1 median pollution fix) or explicitly documented as expected behavior with auto-switch-to-chrom-median triggering correctly when SAG40 is identified as not-flat.
- `amplicon_reliability.tsv` returns ≥10 amplicons on chromosome II (matches by-eye expected count of 10-13).
- All new regression tests pass.
- PuffStep parity 28/28.

**Out-of-scope items (correctly deferred):**
- Detector redesign per `[ISSUE:2026-04-14:1]` "Ideas" subsection (principled-thresholding + triangularity-BIC) — future-phase via SOUP or BRAINSTORM at user direction.
- GC-aware chrom-median normalization — `multi-agent/plans/next/GECKO_SOUP.md` (created 2026-04-30; future-phase work).
- HMM step-number leakage refactor (`[ISSUE:2026-04-30:2]`) — cycle 15.9a SPEC15.21 sub-deliverable + future structural-unification phase.

### 2026-04-30 — Scope expansion: absorb `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:4]` HMM-posterior unit (4 findings; cycle remains defect-fix supplemental within SPEC15.6 scope, no new SPEC priority)

**Authors:** John M. Urban (decisions + scope direction); Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max) (transcription).

**Triggered by:** Orchestrator-outside-cycle audit 2026-04-30 (post-cycle-15.6b-closeout) prompted by direct user observation that `--pipelines hmm --posterior` produces no posterior outputs and no error. Investigation surfaced 4 silent-no-op findings (S2 severity per `feedback_cross_pipeline_parity_blindspot.md` discipline) that intersect this cycle's existing SPEC15.6 scope (deliverables 3 + 5 + 6). User direction 2026-04-30: *"I need this done ASAP in 15.4a-S2."*

**Findings absorbed (all S2 — silent feature no-op or silent miscalculation risk):**

1. **Finding 1 — `--posterior` silently no-ops for HMM-only.** `onionskin.py:4672-4691` (multistage controller HMM-only short-circuit) + `onionskin.py:4941-4959` (single/two-stage controller HMM-only short-circuit) both `return` before `args.posterior` is checked. The two `_run_posterior(...)` call sites at `onionskin.py:4712` + `onionskin.py:4770` live inside the multistage+growth branch gated by `if dec.mode == "multistage" and _pipelines_include(args, "growth")`. The multistage RMS branch at `onionskin.py:4773-4806` correctly honors `--posterior` via `_run_rcn_posterior`; HMM has no equivalent. Repair: wire HMM-only branches to honor `args.posterior` via a new HMM-pipeline posterior code path (sister to `_run_posterior` and `_run_rcn_posterior`). New helper module candidate: extend `onionskin.py` with `_run_hmm_posterior(...)` OR thread HMM through a unified `_run_posterior_for_pipeline(...)` helper — R1 audit chooses the architecture.

2. **Finding 2 — `--compute-aps` not invoked for HMM-only prior runs.** Same HMM-only short-circuits return before `_phase2_postprocess` (where Growth's APS computation lives at `onionskin.py:795-830`) and before the RMS engine's APS step at `onionskin.py:3647-3670`. Without APS, Finding 1's posterior fix has no input to consume — `_run_posterior` requires APS cluster files (gates at `onionskin.py:4296-4302` + `onionskin.py:4385-4390`). Repair: HMM-only branches MUST invoke APS computation when `args.compute_aps` is true. APS outputs land in HMM's output tree (specifically NOT under `02-growth-model/` per Finding 5's broader layout-asymmetry concern; flag for R1 to weigh whether layout adjustment is in 15.4a-S2 scope or deferred to SOUP Item 6b).

3. **Finding 3 — Flat-sample auto-switch is per-pipeline-set but consumed as a single shared flag.** The `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch(...)` helper at `onionskin.py:97-147` IS called separately for each pipeline (sites at `onionskin.py:852` Growth, `:3697` RMS, `:3962` HMM) and each call sets `args._flat_sample_auto_switch = True` independently — last-write-wins. `_run_posterior` at `onionskin.py:4402-4411` then reads the single shared flag and applies the same norm-mode override across `growth_norm_mode` / `rms_norm_mode` / `hmm_norm_mode`. If pipelines' flat-sample status diverges (one pipeline detects 0 flats but another detects ≥1), the posterior norm-mode override is wrong for at least one pipeline. Repair: refactor `args._flat_sample_auto_switch` from a single shared flag to per-pipeline state. Three options:
   - (a) Per-pipeline attributes: `args._flat_sample_auto_switch_growth`, `_rms`, `_hmm`, each set by the matching `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch(...)` call site; `_run_posterior` consumes the per-pipeline attribute matching the pipeline whose norm-mode is being overridden.
   - (b) Dictionary form: `args._flat_sample_auto_switch = {"growth": ..., "rms": ..., "hmm": ...}` set per-pipeline; consumed per-pipeline.
   - (c) Per-pipeline-state object: a small dataclass-like container holding the per-pipeline flat-sample state. Useful if other per-pipeline state needs to be tracked alongside (which Finding 3's broader theme suggests is likely).
   R1 audit chooses among (a)/(b)/(c) based on existing project style + future-extensibility considerations.

4. **Finding 6 — APS not emitted by HMM at all.** Growth and RMS have explicit APS code paths; HMM has none. Coupled with Findings 1+2 — same fix surface. The `--compute-aps` help text at `onionskin.py:1716-1718` documents this flag as universal across all three pipelines, but the implementation is Growth+RMS-only. Repair: HMM gets an APS code path that emits the standard APS sidecar TSVs and cluster outputs. Per SPEC15.6 deliverable 5 contract: cross-pipeline IDENTICAL schema across HMM + Growth + RMS for downstream consumers.

**Cross-pipeline parity guard.** Same as cycle 15.6b — fixes must apply cross-pipeline. The `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch` per-pipeline-state refactor (Finding 3) is the broader principle: any per-pipeline state set in pipeline-specific call sites must NOT collapse into a single shared flag at the consumption point. R1 audit should sweep for analogous patterns in `onionskin.py` (per the SOUP Item 6c recurring-audit discipline) and either fix in this cycle if scope-fit OR file as `[ISSUE:...]` for next-phase landing.

**Additional validation gates (on top of those from the original `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:3]`-driven scope at the top of this scaffold):**
- New regression test (e.g., `tests/test_hmm_only_posterior.py` or similar) — end-to-end `--pipelines hmm --posterior --compute-aps` invocation against toy fixture; PASSES at cycle close.
- `make puff-compare` 28/28 CRITICAL gate held — non-negotiable. HMM detection bit-for-bit unchanged by posterior wiring.
- The original `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:3]` validation gates remain in effect (SAG40 not flat, SAG41 negative-bump-resolved-or-documented, ≥10 amplicons on II, regression test, full suite, ground-truth comparison).

**Additional exit conditions (on top of those from the original `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:3]`-driven scope):**
- `--pipelines hmm --posterior --compute-aps` produces a `posterior/` directory with HMM-pipeline outputs.
- `tests/test_hmm_only_posterior.py` PASSES.
- Flat-sample auto-switch decision is applied per-pipeline (each pipeline's posterior norm-mode override consults its OWN prior-run flat-sample state, not a shared flag). Verifiable by R1 audit code-reading + by a regression test that constructs a divergent-flat-status fixture across pipelines.

**Out-of-scope additions (deferred to other landing zones):**
- Finding 4 (Growth-only `--posterior` re-run shortcut at `onionskin.py:4708-4713`) → `multi-agent/plans/next/CROSS_PIPELINE_UNIFICATION_SOUP.md` Item 6a.
- Finding 5 (Growth-anchored output layout dict keys at `onionskin_core/output_layout.py:243-294`) → SOUP Item 6b.
- Finding 7 (HMM-missing diagnostic plots — APS, summit, profile, shape-filter, QC) → cycle 15.8a SPEC15.17 second scope expansion 2026-04-30 (deliverables 16-19; see `multi-agent/plans/PHASE15_SPEC.md`).

**R1 audit must address (additional to the original (a)-(e) diagnostic questions at the top of this scaffold):**

(f) **HMM-pipeline posterior architecture.** Implement HMM posterior as: (i) standalone `_run_hmm_posterior` sister to `_run_posterior` and `_run_rcn_posterior`, OR (ii) unified `_run_posterior_for_pipeline(pipeline, ...)` helper that all three pipelines share. R1 weighs maintainability vs implementation cost vs the upcoming SOUP Item 6 future-phase unification work (option (ii) anticipates that future direction; option (i) is more conservative).

(g) **Per-pipeline flat-sample-state refactor architecture.** Pick option (a) per-pipeline attributes / (b) dict / (c) per-pipeline-state object per Finding 3 above. Justify the choice with consideration of future per-pipeline state additions that Finding 3's broader theme implies are likely.

(h) **Cross-pipeline state-divergence sweep (Item 6c discipline).** While in `onionskin.py` for the Finding-3 refactor, sweep for analogous shared-flag patterns. Examples: `args._<state>` attributes that are set in multiple per-pipeline call sites and consumed at controller-level decisions. Document findings in the audit log even if no fix is in-scope; the broader audit becomes Item 6c future work.

**Updated next-assignee notes:** Cycle 15.4a-S2 R1 audit scope IS broadened (per this 2026-04-30 amendment); R1 launcher will be updated separately by the orchestrator to reflect the broadened scope before R1 fires. R1 reads BOTH the original 2026-04-30 scaffold (top of this section) AND this scope-expansion appendix.

**Stage B live-code re-audit (per-file, against the R1 repair contract).**

- **`onionskin_core/common.py` (NEW grid-contract helpers).** `assert_shared_track_grid` + `assert_shared_chrom_track_grid` added. Both helpers compare `(chrom, start, end)` triples across all sample IDs vs the first sample, raise `AssertionError` with diagnostic context on mismatch, and return the validated reference DataFrame for caller reuse. The `ref_sid = sample_ids[0]` first-sample reference is preserved (correct per Stage A.3's empirical confirmation that the invariant holds), but the silent-determinism-risk has been converted into a loud failure on any future divergence — the F3.b contract.
- **`onionskin_core/aps.py:457` (`build_aps_shape_matrix`).** `ref_sid = sample_ids[0]` replaced by `ref_df = assert_shared_track_grid(...)` called once before the locus loop; the cached `ref_df` is reused inside the loop instead of `sample_tracks[ref_sid]` per iteration. Comment locks the invariant ("Invariant locked at cycle 15.6b Stage A.3").
- **`onionskin_core/aps_pca.py` (`compute_amplicon_layer1_pca` + `compute_sample_layer2_pca_raw`).** Both call sites now pass a validated `ref_df` (from `assert_shared_track_grid`) into `_locus_bin_matrix`; the formerly per-loop `ref_df = sample_tracks[sample_ids[0]]` is gone. `compute_sample_layer2_pca_reduced` (line 164) operates on a pre-computed `layer1_matrix` rather than `sample_tracks`, so it correctly does not need the assertion.
- **`onionskin_core/engines/growth_model_engine.py` (5 sites + sort lock).** `apply_ref_stage_baseline:332`, `stage_median_track:360`, `compute_stage_medians_all:916`, `build_model_peak_profile:1003`, and the `_run_argv` per-chrom growth-track loop at `:1245` all replace `sample_tracks[sample_ids[0]].get(chrom)` with `assert_shared_chrom_track_grid(...)`. Stage-median enumeration in `_run_argv:1968` swaps `for _fn in os.listdir(...)` for `for _fn in sorted(os.listdir(...))` — the explicit B-Common sort-order lock for the post-`max_rcn_stage` fix-up surface. Note that `stage_median_track` correctly uses the per-stage subset of sample IDs (`stage_sample_ids = [sample_ids[i] for i in idx]`) rather than the full set, which is the right scoping for that helper.
- **`onionskin_core/engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py` (3 surfaces).** `_per_stage_one`'s base + hires per-chrom loops both replace the `sample_tracks[sample_ids[0]].get(chrom)` first-sample pattern with `assert_shared_chrom_track_grid(...)` scoped to the per-stage `stage_sample_ids` (lines 240 + 463). The post-`as_completed` worker-result swallow at the original line 618 is replaced by collecting failures in a `failures: List[Tuple[int, Exception]]` list and re-raising a single `RuntimeError` naming the first failed stage's exception (Option B-i'-b from the audit's repair contract — the explicitly-recommended option). The broad `try/except Exception` around `run_rms_active_stage_convergence(...)` at the original line 730 is removed entirely; convergence failures now propagate up. The remaining `except Exception` blocks at lines 278/408/514/787/806 are genuinely defensive numeric/plot-fallback catches with deterministic alternatives, not silent-orchestration swallows — correct judgment to leave.
- **`onionskin_core/rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py` (2 sites).** `_per_stage_one`'s base + hires per-chrom loops mirror the engine's hardening with `assert_shared_chrom_track_grid(...)` scoped to per-stage `stage_sample_ids`.
- **`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:1665-1734` (active-stage summit refinement narrowing).** The outer `try/except Exception as exc: print(f"[hmm] active-stage summit refinement warning: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)` at the original lines 1721-1734 is removed. A local `_safe_copy(src, dst)` helper wraps **only** `shutil.copy2(...)` calls, catching **only** `OSError` (not bare `Exception`). All non-FS operations — `masks_from_timing(...)`, both `run_step18_hmm_timing(...)` calls, `csv.DictReader(...)` parsing, and `int(float(raw))` conversion — now propagate up to the engine's outer error path. The inner `try/except (TypeError, ValueError): continue` at the per-row sliding-offset parse is preserved (correct — that narrow handling was always intentional). This is exactly the B-Branch (i) contract.
- **`onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py:295` (RMS active-stage convergence).** `for fname in os.listdir(summit_refinement_dir)` → `for fname in sorted(os.listdir(summit_refinement_dir))`. The B-Common sort-order lock for the per-stage `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` enumeration that feeds `_selected_summit_from_stage_rows`.
