Each section: defaults table (feature mode / linkage / k-selection / singleton guard / reliability filter / area form / cross-pipeline differences), rationale paragraph, datasets-tested list, baseline-regeneration triggers list. Populate after the SPEC15.12 experiments complete (Track 1) and the trajectory-clustering audit completes (Track 2).

**F4.2 (Track 1 quantitative experiments — DEPENDS on determinism).** SPEC15.12 d2 — feature-mode × linkage × singleton-guard × reliability-filter × area-form combinations. The full grid is large (~50-200 experiments depending on chosen breadth). Each experiment must be run on representative datasets — SPEC tests `tests/full_chrom_training_data/manifest.dataset1.5000bp.tsv` chr II as the primary; chr III/IV as secondary if time permits.

**Repair (Role 2):** use the SPEC15.11-extended harness (F3.4). Apply the determinism gate (per C2): every locked default MUST come from a stable across-runs cluster outcome. Tentative defaults (where stability is borderline) are acceptable as cycle 15.6a output IF flagged tentative + scheduled for re-validation post-determinism-fix.

**F4.3 (Track 2 trajectory clustering audit).** SPEC15.12 d5-d7 — inventory `hmm_ported_analyses.py:726-786` (`run_step19_hmm_clustering`, post-cycle-15.4b renumber from `run_step18_hmm_clustering`); inventory `trajectory_feature_matrix.tsv` + `trajectory_clusters.tsv` outputs at HMM `19-clustering/`; verify no fork/width-progression-output regressions per SPEC15.5 sub-priority (f). Decide whether trajectory-clustering defaults need separate tuning from APS clustering (SPEC explicitly says they likely differ — trajectory is amplicon-level fork-trajectory, APS is sample-level posterior).

**Repair (Role 2):** write the inventory + decision in the same `CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md` Track 2 section (F4.1). If experiments are run, use the harness with a Track-2 sub-mode OR a sibling script — SPEC defers the implementation choice to Role 2.

**F4.4 (Track 3 SAPS lock — language verification).** SPEC15.12 d9 locks SAPS clustering OUT of Phase 15 (per JQ27 / Codex Finding 3 resolution Round 2). Audit confirms the lock language is intact in the SPEC body (`PHASE15_SPEC.md:874-878`). No live-code work needed for the lock itself.

**Repair (Role 2):** ensure the cycle-15.6a closeout language in `CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md` Track 3 explicitly restates the SAPS lock + cross-references SPEC15.13 d5.

**F4.5 (`[ISSUE:2026-04-29:6]` — sample-level APS aggregation does NOT use reliability-filtered loci).** SPEC15.12 mid-phase amendment paragraph (`PHASE15_SPEC.md:889`) requires this cycle to address `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:6]`. Verified live: `aps.py:1442` writes `aps_df.to_csv(.../onionskin_aps.tsv)` BEFORE reliability filtering; the aggregates at lines 300-309 (`APS_area`, `APS_summit`, `APS_width_1p5`, `APS_global_mean`, `APS_sum_of_means`, `APS_mean_of_means`) compute over the FULL contrib_df (all loci — reliable + unreliable). Only the clustering matrix uses reliability-filtered loci (`matrix_contrib_df` at line 1416).

**Repair contract — choose one option (recommend Option B):**
- **Option A:** Promote `--aps-weight-loci` to default `True`. Aggregates become reliability-weighted (each locus contributes proportionally to its `reliability_score`). Backward-compat warning required because aggregate values shift on existing datasets.
- **Option B (RECOMMENDED):** Emit side-by-side outputs: `onionskin_aps.tsv` (current — all loci) + `onionskin_aps_reliable.tsv` (NEW — reliability-filtered aggregates). Users + downstream consumers pick which they want. No backward-compat break; new output is opt-in for inspection. Compatible with `multi-agent/tracking/BRAINSTORM.md [2026-04-29] Bayesian-weighted amplicon reliability` (the brainstorm entry suggests this exact pattern — emitting both unweighted + weighted views).
- **Option C:** Add `APS_area_reliable` etc. columns alongside the existing aggregates within the same `onionskin_aps.tsv`. Fewer files; mixed schema.

**Repair (Role 2):** lock the option with the user before coding. If Option B: add `--aps-emit-reliability-filtered-aggregates` flag (default on) controlling whether the second file is emitted; cascade through `compute_and_write_aps` after line 1416 (where `matrix_contrib_df` is built). The reliability-filtered aggregates use the same six columns recomputed on `matrix_contrib_df`. Add tests covering both schemas.

##### F5 — SPEC15.10 d4 + SPEC15.10 d8: SAPS feature-naming-harmonization cross-reference

SPEC15.10 d8 says SAPS (SPEC15.13 / Phase 7 / cycle 15.7a) must use post-rename schema (`summit_rcn`, not `summit_excess`) so SAPS doesn't ship with legacy naming. This cycle's repair contract should produce a clean post-rename schema; the cross-cycle handoff to cycle 15.7a is implicit in the SPEC line numbering and needs no live-code work in cycle 15.6a beyond ensuring the rename actually lands cleanly.

**Repair:** documentation note in `CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md` + the cycle-closeout CHANGELOG entry referencing SPEC15.13 inheritance.

##### F6 — Substantive-priority size check (per scope-authority rule)

Cycle 15.6a covers four priorities — comfortably above the substantive bar. Each priority has multiple deliverables (SPEC15.9: 4 + Implementation notes; SPEC15.10: 8; SPEC15.11: 9; SPEC15.12: 9 across three tracks). Per `multi-agent/AGENT_CONVENTIONS.md § Scope authority` and the workflow's substantive-priorities rule: this cycle is NOT a candidate for skip-reaudit eligibility — Role 2 implementation will be heavy and substantive cross-module work; Role 3 re-audit verification is required.

##### F7 — Cross-pipeline parity blind-spot guard

Per memory `feedback_cross_pipeline_parity_blindspot.md`: "X-pipeline-only" defects systematically slip through audits. Cycle 15.6a is high-risk for this:
- HMM-side feature resolver (`hmm_ported_analyses.py:304-305`) is a duplicate of `aps.py:608-615` `_FEATURE_COL`. New feature modes need to land in BOTH.
- HMM-side `run_step16_hmm_aps` (`hmm_ported_analyses.py:180-244`) is a parallel-but-thinner version of `compute_and_write_aps` (`aps.py:1133-1563`). It does NOT call `build_amplicon_importance`, `build_scalar_orderings`, `write_aps_plots` — but it DOES emit aps_matrix_raw.tsv + aps_matrix_zscore.tsv + aps_clusters.tsv. New feature modes must work in BOTH the full path (Growth + RMS via `compute_and_write_aps`) AND the HMM-thin path (`run_step16_hmm_aps`).
- `--aps-sample-aggregation-floor` flag (F2.3) needs cascading through both paths.

**Repair guard for Role 2:**
- After every new flag / feature mode lands, run `make toy` and verify all three pipeline output dirs (`02-growth-model/13-aps/`, `03-rcn-mean-shift/12-aps/`, `01-hmm/16-aps/`) emit consistent column schemas + matrix file presence.
- Cross-pipeline grep for `summit_excess`, `mean_excess`, `peak_rcn` after the rename to confirm no lagging references in any pipeline path.

#### Carry-over verification subsections

##### C1 — HMM APS step-21 timing-promotion ordering check (carry-over from cycle 15.5a R3)

**Question (per cycle 15.5a R3 closeout):** does any SPEC15.9-15.12 deliverable cause HMM APS to start reading the cycle-15.5a-introduced `hmm_timing.tsv` step-21-promoted canonical timing in a way that breaks the ordering (HMM APS at step 16 fires BEFORE step-21 timing promotion)?

**Verification by direct code inspection:**

- **Today's HMM APS function signature** (`onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:180-202`): `run_step16_hmm_aps(manifest, trajectory_dir, out_dir, *, norm_mode, ref_stage, width_threshold, smooth_bins, cluster_k, feature, fix_singletons, floor_enabled, sample_tracks_manifest, odw_active_stage_detector, odw_prob_threshold, odw_fold_threshold, flat_sample_prob_threshold, flat_sample_fold_threshold, flat_sample_min_flat_fraction, keep_amplicons_bed, gap_annotated_tsv)`. **No `timing_tsv` parameter.** Confirmed by `grep` against the function body lines 203-348: zero references to `hmm_timing.tsv`, `timing_tsv`, or `timing_onset_map`. The function does pass `odw_active_stage_detector` / `odw_prob_threshold` / `odw_fold_threshold` to downstream `compute_reliability_sidecar` (lines 261-265) and `write_flat_sample_sidecar` (lines 276-289), but the reliability + flat-sample sidecars compute their ODW signals from per-locus contrib_df (sample × locus matrix) NOT from the timing TSV. Safe.

- **Engine call site** (`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:1607-1636`): `run_step16_hmm_aps(...)` is called with `gap_annotated_tsv=os.path.join(steps["step15"], "all_trajectories.multistage_unification.gap_annotated.tsv")`. No `timing_tsv` argument passed. Step-18 timing fires AFTER step-16 APS at line 1643, and the step-21 timing promotion at line 1719 (`shutil.copy2(step21_timing, step18_timing)`) overwrites step18 timing AFTER step16 APS has already completed.

- **SPEC15.9-15.12 deliverables review:**
  - **SPEC15.9** (area-excess-floor + post-sum-floor variant): operates on per-locus contrib_df + sample tracks. No timing data needed.
  - **SPEC15.10** (column rename + raw-RCN migration): schema/naming change. No timing data needed.
  - **SPEC15.11** (composite features + 3-layer PCA + harness extension): per-amplicon morphology (`summit_rcn`, `width`, `log10area`, `asymmetry_ratio`, `shape_score_raw`) — these are static-shape features at a given stage, NOT timing-derived. Sample-level features (3 scalars + Layer-2 PCA) — derived from per-locus contrib aggregates, NOT timing. Layer-1/2/3 PCA — operates on shape sub-matrices or matrix-level data; NOT timing. APS experiment harness extension — the harness invokes `onionskin.py --pipelines ...` which does not need timing-aware HMM APS.
  - **SPEC15.12** (clustering defaults + APS-vs-trajectory disambiguation): clustering operates on the matrix produced by SPEC15.11's feature modes; no new timing read inside HMM APS. Trajectory clustering (Track 2) reads `12-fork-travel/fork_travel_metrics.tsv` (per `hmm_ported_analyses.py:732`), not `hmm_timing.tsv`.

- **`[ISSUE:2026-04-29:6]` mid-phase amendment for SPEC15.12** (sample-level APS aggregation reliability-filter integration): would NOT introduce a timing read either way. Option A (`--aps-weight-loci=default-on`) computes weighted aggregates from `contrib_df + reliability_score`. Option B (side-by-side `onionskin_aps.tsv` + `onionskin_aps_reliable.tsv`) recomputes aggregates from `matrix_contrib_df`. Both stay in the contrib + reliability schema — no timing data.

**Conclusion:** No SPEC15.9-15.12 deliverable causes HMM APS to start reading `hmm_timing.tsv`. The HMM APS step-16 vs step-21 timing-promotion ordering remains harmless. **Dependency check is recorded as performed; outcome is harmless.**

**Forward note (for cycle 15.7a — SPEC15.13 SAPS):** SPEC15.13 d1 says SAPS *"reads decoded HMM state paths from `06-HMM/indiv_samples/`"* — also no `hmm_timing.tsv` consumption. The ordering remains harmless even past cycle 15.6a. The first cycle that may need to relocate HMM APS is whichever future cycle introduces a timing-aware HMM APS feature (none of the queued Phase 15 cycles do this; flag would be SPEC engineering's call at that future point).

##### C2 — `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:1]` strategy-selection determinism (carry-over from cycle 15.5a R3 closeout)

**Issue restatement:** Two runs of `make toy` on the same tree at the same HEAD produced different `selected_stages` for the same call (II:3680000-4345000 — R2-J 2026-04-30 picked stage 4 only; R3 re-run 2026-04-30 picked stages 4+5). Per-stage parabola summit values were stable across runs; variance is upstream of the strategy selector. Likely candidates: per-stage shape-confidence stochasticity; upstream timing/ODW variance; ProcessPoolExecutor reduction order in RMS `_per_stage_one`; cached state leak (lower probability).

**Cycle 15.6a sensitivity assessment:** APS analytical work in cycle 15.6a is **structurally sensitive** to the upstream variance because:

1. **Reliability scoring** (`compute_reliability_sidecar` at `onionskin_core/reliability.py`) consumes ODW posteriors. If ODW posteriors vary between runs, `reliability_score` per locus varies → which loci pass the reliability filter varies → cluster-matrix composition varies.
2. **APS clustering** (raw matrix + zscore matrix → hierarchical cluster + leaf order) is deterministic given a stable input matrix, but the input matrix composition depends on (1) above.
3. **Locus-level summits** (used by `summit_bp_map` at `aps.py:1250-1275` which feeds the windowed-summit `summit_rcn` at `_locus_metrics:215-224`) come from `_origins.tsv`. If different stages get selected (the strategy machinery's variance), the refined summit position may shift — affecting `summit_rcn` per-locus values directly.
4. **SPEC15.9 default-flip evaluation** asks for cluster-separation comparisons across floor-on/off/post_sum modes — three results to compare. If each result has run-to-run noise, separating "real" cluster differences from noise becomes harder.
5. **SPEC15.12 quantitative defaults experiments** propose locking project-wide clustering defaults. Locking on a noisy substrate could lock decisions that turn out to be artefacts.

**Audit recommendation: (c) ESCALATE to a dedicated cycle 15.6b mid-phase amendment** that fires AFTER cycle 15.6a's mechanism + schema work but BEFORE cycle 15.6a's quantitative-defaults closeout decisions.

**Rationale for (c) over (a) and (b):**
- **(a) — fix in cycle 15.6a:** would balloon scope. Cycle 15.6a is already the largest priority-count cycle in Phase 15; adding a determinism investigation + fix on top of SPEC15.9 + SPEC15.10 + SPEC15.11 + SPEC15.12 is unworkable.
- **(b) — defer with rationale:** weak position because (1)-(5) above are real sensitivities, not theoretical. Cluster-defaults locked under (b) would carry an unknown noise floor.
- **(c) — dedicated cycle:** clean separation of concerns. Cycle 15.6b owns the diagnostic + fix. Cycle 15.6a's mechanism + schema work (SPEC15.10 column rename, SPEC15.11 feature-mode implementations, SPEC15.9 post-sum-floor variant implementation) lands deterministically (these are pure code changes that don't depend on cluster-stability). Cycle 15.6a's QUANTITATIVE DECISIONS (SPEC15.9 default-flip, SPEC15.12 default decisions) are gated by the determinism fix.

**Concrete cycle 15.6b proposal (for user authorization, not yet inserted):**

- **Cycle name:** 15.6b — Strategy-selection determinism diagnostic + fix.
- **SPEC ID:** SPEC15.23 (next free ID after SPEC15.22).
- **Scope:**
  - **Diagnostic (Role 2 deliverable, R2 stage A):** run the `[ISSUE:2026-04-30:1]` recipe — 5× `make toy` from a clean tree (`rm -rf dev/runs/toy_out` between each), diff `*timing.tsv` and `*_summit_estimates.tsv` between runs across all three pipelines. Identify which surface(s) vary. Likely candidates per the issue body: (i) per-stage shape-confidence values; (ii) upstream timing/ODW boundary computation in `onionskin_core/timing.py`; (iii) ProcessPoolExecutor reduction order in `onionskin_core/engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py`'s `_per_stage_one`; (iv) cached state leak (lowest priority).
  - **Fix (R2 stage B):** depending on diagnostic outcome:
    - If (iii) — replace ProcessPoolExecutor with a deterministic-order pattern: chunked sequential reduce, OR `as_completed` with a final sort by `(chrom, stage, start)` before reducing.
    - If (i)/(ii) — add a `numpy` / `random` / `random.SystemRandom` seed at process entry; or identify the floating-point reduction non-associativity that produces different rounding under different scheduling.
    - If (iv) — root-cause the leak; tighten the `make toy` clean-tree assumption.
  - **Validation:** run the recipe again post-fix; verify across-run diff is empty.
  - **Test:** add a regression test `tests/test_determinism.py` that runs `make toy` (or a faster equivalent) twice and asserts identical TSV outputs.
- **R1 / R2 / R3 assignees:** match cycle 15.6a's pattern (Opus High R1+R3 / Codex Reasoning Extra High R2). NOT skip-reaudit eligible.
- **Sequence:** insert between cycle 15.6a's mechanism work and cycle 15.6a's quantitative-defaults closeout. Or: split cycle 15.6a into 15.6a-mech (mechanism-only — SPEC15.10 + SPEC15.11 implementations + SPEC15.9 post-sum-floor variant, closes deterministically) → cycle 15.6b (determinism fix) → cycle 15.6a-quant (SPEC15.9 default-flip + SPEC15.12 quantitative defaults — closes once 15.6b lands).

**Fallback (if user rejects mid-phase amendment):** scope cycle 15.6a's quantitative defaults work to TENTATIVE — locked decisions in `CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md` + `DECISIONS.md` are explicitly tagged `tentative — subject to determinism re-validation in a later cycle`. Add a closeout-tracking entry to `multi-agent/tracking/KNOWN_ISSUES.md` (`[ISSUE:2026-XX-XX:N] Determinism re-validation required for SPEC15.9 + SPEC15.12 quantitative defaults`) so the re-validation gate is not lost. Cycle 15.10a closeout sweep verifies the gate is honored.

**Determinism gate added to Role 2 instructions for cycle 15.6a (regardless of which path the user picks):**
- Before locking ANY default decision in cycle 15.6a (SPEC15.9 default-flip OR SPEC15.12 clustering defaults), Role 2 MUST run the SPEC15.11 harness ≥3 times from a clean `dev/runs/` tree on the candidate-default configuration AND verify cluster assignments + leaf orders are byte-identical across the 3 runs.
- If stable: the default may be locked.
- If unstable: the default is recorded as TENTATIVE in `CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md` + DECISIONS.md, with explicit re-validation gate language. The cycle 15.6b path (preferred) becomes load-bearing for closeout.

**Diagnostic recipe NOT run at this audit time** — the SPEC engineering audit deliberately scopes the diagnostic to cycle 15.6b's R2 implementation (or cycle 15.6a R2 if user picks the in-cycle path) to keep this audit focused on the SPEC15.9-15.12 contract surface. Running the recipe at audit time would consume ~5 minutes of `make toy` time without changing the recommendation: the structural argument for (c) is grounded in the architectural sensitivity of cycle 15.6a's defaults to noise, not in the magnitude of the noise.

#### Repair instructions summary for Role 2 (sequenced + concrete)

The cleanest implementation order, balancing determinism risk + SPEC dependencies:

**Stage A — SPEC15.10 column rename (substantive but mechanical-with-care).**
1. Lock the `asymmetry_ratio` formula + width-column-name decision in `multi-agent/project_context/DECISIONS.md` (an `[2026-XX-XX] Phase 15 cycle 15.6a SPEC15.11 morphology feature definitions` entry). Require user input if SPEC engineering chooses to defer.
2. Edit `_locus_metrics()` at `onionskin_core/aps.py:198-233` per F2.1 (rename + raw-RCN emission + new schema).
3. Edit HMM-side resolver at `hmm_ported_analyses.py:304-305` per F2.2 (live line numbers, not SPEC's stale 149-151).
4. Sweep `aps.py`, `hmm_ported_analyses.py`, `tests/`, `scripts/`, `PIPELINE_SPEC.md`, `ONIONSKIN_FULL_HANDOFF.md` for `summit_excess`, `mean_excess`, `peak_rcn` references; update or remove.
5. Add `--aps-sample-aggregation-floor on|off` per F2.3.
6. Add regression tests per F2.1 and F2.3.
7. Validation gates: `make test` (full), `make toy` (verify clean run); `make puff-compare` MUST stay 28/28 (HMM detection unchanged by APS schema rename).

**Stage B — SPEC15.9 mechanism (post-sum-floor variant — substantive).**
1. Implement `area_floor_mode: str = "per_bin"` parameter cascade per F1.1.
2. Extend `--aps-area-excess-floor` choices to `["on", "off", "post_sum"]` (or equivalent names).
3. Add unit tests for all three modes per F1.1.
4. Validation: same gate set as Stage A.

**Stage C — SPEC15.11 implementations (heaviest stage).**
1. Add per-amplicon morphology features per F3.1 to `_locus_metrics` (uses Stage A schema).
2. Add sample-level features per F3.2.
3. Implement 3-layer PCA per F3.3 in new `onionskin_core/aps_pca.py` (or extension within `aps.py`). sklearn dependency.
4. Add new feature modes (`shape_pca`, `log2shape_pca`, `sample_shape_pca_raw`, `sample_shape_pca_reduced`, `amp_metrics`, `sample_metrics`, `metrics`) to `--aps-feature` choices + dispatch wiring per F3.1-F3.3.
5. Add new flags `--aps-num-amp-pc`, `--aps-num-sample-pc`, `--aps-pca-matrix`, `--aps-final-pc` per F3.2.
6. Update HMM-side resolver to know all new modes per F3.5 — recommend factoring shared dispatch.
7. Extend `scripts/aps_cluster_experiments.py` per F3.4 (pipeline-selectable + new modes + new flags).
8. Validation: same gate set + new APS-cluster-experiment harness smoke run on toy data.

**Stage D — SPEC15.9 default-flip evaluation + SPEC15.12 quantitative defaults (gated by C2 determinism status).**
1. **If user picks cycle 15.6b path (recommended):** STOP at end of Stage C. Cycle 15.6a closes with mechanism + schema work landed; SPEC15.9 default + SPEC15.12 defaults explicitly deferred to cycle 15.6c (post-15.6b).
2. **If user picks in-cycle path with TENTATIVE defaults:**
   - Run the SPEC15.11 harness on representative datasets per F4.2, applying the determinism gate (≥3 runs, cluster-stability check).
   - Settle defaults that are stable; tag unstable defaults TENTATIVE with re-validation language per C2 fallback.
   - Write `multi-agent/full_instructions/CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md` per F4.1.
   - Run the Track 2 trajectory-clustering audit per F4.3.
   - Address `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:6]` per F4.5 (lock Option A/B/C with user).

**Stage E — Cycle closeout (Role 1 R3 deliverable, NOT R2).**
1. Update SPEC priority status table in `PHASE15_SPEC.md`: mark SPEC15.9-15.12 as `DONE (v0.X.YY)` or `TENTATIVE (v0.X.YY) — pending determinism cycle 15.6b/c`.
2. Write CHANGELOG `[v0.X.YY]` entry with consolidated authorship (survey this audit log cycle 15.6a section's `**Authors:**` lines). Use SPEC15.10 d4 default-flip language flag prominently.
3. Update HANDOFF + TASK to point at the next cycle (15.6b if amended; 15.7a otherwise).
4. Per the Form B closeout-commit convention: write the CHANGELOG entry text verbatim to `changelog-entry-v0.X.YY.txt` at repo root; emit the three-command commit block.

#### Live-code verification (HEAD `6e0d42e`)

Verified against the live tree by direct Read + grep, not by trusting the implementation report:

**Stage A — SPEC15.10 column rename + raw-RCN migration — VERIFIED:**
- `_locus_metrics()` at `onionskin_core/aps.py:242-303` returns the new schema `{area_excess, mean_rcn, width_above_threshold_bp, summit_rcn, log10area, asymmetry_ratio}`. The empty-locus return at lines 255-262 mirrors the new schema with `summit_rcn=1.0` + `mean_rcn=1.0` (diploid baseline; matches F2.1 audit recommendation and addresses [SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:6]'s implicit-via-rationale concern).
- `peak_rcn`, `summit_excess`, `mean_excess` are removed from the emitted schema. `tests/test_pipeline.py:360-362` asserts the negative explicitly. Cross-tree grep confirms no APS-domain `summit_excess` / `mean_excess` references remain (residual `peak_rcn` hits at `onionskin_core/hmm_multistage_unification.py`, `onionskin_core/timing.py` are unrelated stage-fold tables; `onionskin_core/reliability.py:149-151` and `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py:312-313` retain narrow legacy fallbacks for old fixtures, per the implementer's documented compatibility stance).
- `_FEATURE_COL` at `onionskin_core/aps.py:694-701` maps `summit` and `log2summit` → `summit_rcn` (raw RCN); `area`/`log10area`/`log2area` → `area_excess` with downstream transforms applied at matrix-build time (lines 843-849).
- HMM-side resolver: instead of duplicating `_FEATURE_COL`, `run_step16_hmm_aps` at `onionskin_core/hmm_ported_analyses.py:331-345` consumes the shared `build_aps_feature_matrix()` resolver introduced at `onionskin_core/aps.py:755-850`. **This is broader than F2.2's minimum repair** and is the right structural call: it collapses the duplicated dict that fed [SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:8]'s recurring-divergence pattern. Live grep confirms no surviving HMM-side `_FEATURE_COL` dict.
- `--aps-sample-aggregation-floor on|off` flag wired at `onionskin.py:1894-1905` with the documented semantics; cascades through `compute_aps_tables` (line 311), `_sample_excess` (line 237-239), and the engine boundary (`onionskin_core/engines/hmm_engine.py:1264, 1627`).
- New regression tests land at `tests/test_pipeline.py`: `test_locus_metrics_emits_summit_rcn_and_mean_rcn` + `test_aps_sample_aggregation_floor_on_off` + the renamed/repurposed `test_aps_area_excess_floor_switches_per_bin_contributions`. All passing in the targeted suite.
- `asymmetry_ratio` formula at `onionskin_core/aps.py:289-295` is `abs(summit_coord - midpoint) / (locus_width / 2.0)`, clipped to `[0, 1]` — matches the `[2026-04-30] Phase 15 cycle 15.6a SPEC15.11 morphology feature definitions` DECISIONS lock that R2 wrote BEFORE coding (per F3.1 prerequisite).
- `shape_score_raw` joined opportunistically in `compute_and_write_aps` via id-and-coordinate aliases at `onionskin_core/aps.py:1380-1438`; missing values left `NaN` per the audit's repair contract.

**Stage B — SPEC15.9 post-sum-floor mechanism — VERIFIED:**
- `normalize_area_floor_mode()` at `onionskin_core/aps.py:198-217` accepts `{on/per_bin, off, post_sum}` (plus aliases for `post-sum`, `true`/`false`/`1`/`0`). `_excess_values()` at lines 220-224 applies the per-bin floor only in `per_bin` mode (`off` and `post_sum` retain signed bins). `_area_excess_from_raw()` at lines 227-234 applies the post-sum floor only at the amplicon-total level: signed sum, then `max(signed, 0.0)` if `post_sum`. Three-mode contract correctly preserved.
- CLI choice extended at `onionskin.py:1881-1892` to `["on", "off", "post_sum"]` with help text describing the new semantics.
- `--aps-area-excess-floor` cascades through `compute_aps_tables` (line 310), `_locus_metrics` (line 247), `build_aps_matrix` (line 393 — area unaffected; the value column is fixed), `build_aps_shape_matrix` (line 429), `compute_and_write_aps` (line 1462), and the HMM engine boundary (`hmm_engine.py:1263`).
- `build_aps_shape_matrix` interprets `post_sum` as signed-per-bin (i.e., `off` behavior) because a post-sum clamp is an amplicon-total operation with no meaningful per-bin analogue. The implementer flagged this judgment for re-audit; **the call is correct**: F1.1's repair contract specified the post-sum mechanism as an amplicon-total operation; applying it as a lossy per-bin clamp would invent a fourth mode. The signed-per-bin interpretation preserves the mathematical contract.
- New regression tests land: `test_locus_metrics_per_bin_floor_matches_legacy`, `test_locus_metrics_off_matches_legacy`, `test_locus_metrics_post_sum_floor_oscillating_locus` (asserts `per_bin > post_sum > off`), `test_locus_metrics_post_sum_floor_clamps_negative_total_to_zero` (asserts post_sum == 0.0 when all-below-1 RCN). All four pass.

**Stage C — SPEC15.11 feature modes + 3-layer PCA + harness — VERIFIED:**
- New `onionskin_core/aps_pca.py` (169 lines) provides `compute_amplicon_layer1_pca`, `compute_sample_layer2_pca_raw`, `compute_sample_layer2_pca_reduced`, `apply_layer3_pca`. Determinism note honored: `sorted_loci()` at line 95-96 uses `genomic_sort_key`-based sorting for Layer-1 + Layer-2 iteration order. The `_orient_scores()` helper at lines 25-35 stabilizes arbitrary PCA sign flips by orienting each loading's max-magnitude term positive — **this is a clever determinism guard the audit did not request and the implementer added on its own**. Praise.
- New feature modes wired at `APS_FEATURE_CHOICES` (`onionskin_core/aps.py:703-719`): `shape_pca`, `log2shape_pca`, `sample_shape_pca_raw`, `sample_shape_pca_reduced`, `amp_metrics`, `sample_metrics`, `metrics`. `build_aps_feature_matrix` dispatch at lines 786-834 covers all seven modes plus the existing scalar/shape modes.
- New flags wired at `onionskin.py:1906-1932`: `--aps-num-amp-pc` (default 2), `--aps-num-sample-pc` (default 2), `--aps-pca-matrix` (off), `--aps-final-pc` (default 2). All cascade through `compute_and_write_aps` (`onionskin_core/aps.py:1462-1505`), the HMM engine (`hmm_engine.py:1265-1268, 1627-1630`), and HMM-side `run_step16_hmm_aps` (`hmm_ported_analyses.py:197-200, 342-354`).

Persistence at `onionskin_core/aps.py:805, 818, 824, 1788-1796` and HMM mirror at `hmm_ported_analyses.py:351-354`.
- `_amplicon_metrics_matrix()` at `onionskin_core/aps.py:722-742` builds the per-amplicon morphology block from `summit_rcn`, `width_above_threshold_bp`, `log10area`, `asymmetry_ratio`, `shape_score_raw` (filling `NaN` to 0.0 where appropriate). `_sample_metrics_matrix()` at lines 745-752 builds the sample-level scalar block from `APS_summit`, `APS_width_1p5`, `APS_log10_area` (the new column added at line 388). `aps_df` gains `APS_log10_area` per F3.2 contract.
- `scripts/aps_cluster_experiments.py` extended per F3.4: `--pipelines` arg at line 80 (default `growth,rms,hmm`); new feature modes at lines 39-47; new flag plumbing at lines 84-122; `--stability-runs` summary output added (default 1, per F3.4's stability-summary recommendation).
- New regression tests land: `test_aps_layer1_pca_per_amplicon_pcs`, `test_aps_layer2_pca_raw_vs_reduced`, `test_aps_layer3_pca_matrix_projection`, `test_aps_metrics_mode_emits_all_features`. All pass in the targeted suite.

#### Findings under re-audit

**No new substantive findings.** Stage A-C implementation matches the audit's repair contract closely; the implementer's documented deviations + judgments hold up under re-audit:

1. **`build_aps_shape_matrix` post-sum interpretation as signed-per-bin.** Verified correct (per Stage B verification block above).
2. **Narrow legacy `floor_enabled` back-compat path retained.** Verified at `onionskin_core/aps.py:248-251, 322-325, 450-453, 1535` — the path is internal-only (callers using positional `floor_enabled=True/False` continue to work), the public CLI uses `area_floor_mode`. Acceptable defensive coding.
3. **Opportunistic `shape_score_raw` join.** Verified at `onionskin_core/aps.py:1380-1438` — id-aliases + coordinate fallback are the right approach because the cross-pipeline calls TSVs do not share a single id schema. `NaN` for missing values is correct; feature-matrix builders fill `NaN` to `0` only at clustering input time.

**Praise:**

- **Sign-orientation guard in `aps_pca.py:_orient_scores()`.** PCA score signs are arbitrary modulo the loading sign convention; sklearn's `PCA.fit_transform` does not impose a deterministic sign. The implementer added `_orient_scores()` (lines 25-35) that fixes each loading's max-magnitude term to positive sign, eliminating a real determinism risk that the audit did not flag. This is exactly the kind of clever-on-implementation-path quality the reviewer rewards.
- **Promoting the `_FEATURE_COL` cleanup into a `build_aps_feature_matrix` shared resolver consumed by both Growth/RMS and HMM.** F2.2 + F3.5 named the duplicated dict as a "known fragility"; the audit's minimum repair was "update both dicts." The implementer chose the structurally better path: factor the dispatch into a single shared resolver. This is the cleanest possible response to the recurring [SURPRISE:15:15.6a:1:A:8] / `feedback_cross_pipeline_parity_blindspot.md` pattern within Stage C's scope.
- **DECISIONS.md schema-lock entry written BEFORE coding** (per F3.1 prerequisite). The implementer's `[2026-04-30] Phase 15 cycle 15.6a SPEC15.11 morphology feature definitions` entry locks the `asymmetry_ratio` formula, the `width_above_threshold_bp` retention, the `log10area` per-locus emission, and the `shape_score_raw` attachment policy before any code was written. The entry honors the audit's prerequisite order and locks Stage A's contract cleanly.
- **Validation discipline.** Six gates run independently per the workflow rule: compileall, targeted pytest slices for each stage, `make test`, `make toy`, `make twin`, `make full-twin`, `make puff-compare`. All green. The Matplotlib/fontconfig cache permission glitch on the first `make test` run was correctly identified as environmental and re-run rather than glossed over.
- **Stage A/B/C boundary discipline.** The implementer's report explicitly enumerates what was NOT done (no default-flip evaluation; no quantitative defaults; no `CLUSTERING_DEFAULTS.md`; no reliability-filtered aggregate rewrite; no Option A/B/C lock). The orchestrator-locked Stage-A-C-only scope is honored cleanly with explicit cross-references to where deferred work lands. This is the kind of scope-honesty that the v2 workflow rewards.

**Pushback:** None substantive. The implementer's three flagged in-situ catches (back-compat `floor_enabled` retention; `build_aps_shape_matrix` post-sum interpretation as signed-per-bin; the broader-than-minimum `build_aps_feature_matrix` resolver factoring) all hold up under re-audit. No deviation from the repair contract that warrants rework.

## Cycle: 15.5a — Cross-pipeline summit refinement + summit↔timing convergence (SPEC15.7 + SPEC15.8) — CLOSED v0.14.83

### 2026-04-30 — Role 1 Round 3 Re-Audit + Cycle Closeout (Template C)
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max)

**Verdict:** **CLOSED.** Re-audit of the R2-J narrow round (commit `e7b25bc`) confirms all three Round 2 findings (F-R2-1, F-R2-2, F-R2-3) are genuinely resolved in the live tree. All five validation gates PASS independently in the re-audit environment. The R2-J implementer's three flagged in-situ departures from the prescribed Round 2 repair instructions hold up under re-audit and were the right call given the real code structure.

#### Praise for the R2-J implementer

- **Stage A — RMS step-3 implementation is structurally sound.** `summit_convergence.run_rms_active_stage_convergence:286–314` globs `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` files, parses the stage number from the filename rather than relying on lexicographic glob ordering (defends against ≥10 stages), concats into one DataFrame, spatial-matches by `(chrom, start < call_end, end > call_start)` overlap, filters to strategy-selected stages via `_selected_summit_from_stage_rows`, and takes the median of `parabola_summit_bp` (with `peak_raw_bp` fallback). The fallback chain (per-stage parabola → step1=unified-call peak / call_peak) is conservative and correct: when no per-stage data lands in the selected set, the implementation explicitly falls back to step-1 rather than invent a shift. Verified on real toy data: call `II:3680000-4345000` shifts step1=4097843 → step3=4096689 (-1154 bp; selected_stages "4,5"; median of stages 4+5 parabola_summit_bp [4095535, 4097843] = 4096689). The first call `II:855000-1915000` does not shift because its strategy-selected-stages parabola median (1348772) happens to equal step1 (1348772) — correct behavior; agreement should not invent a shift.
- **Stage B — Option A landed cleanly with honest in-situ departures.** New shared module `onionskin_core/summit_metrics.py` factors the windowed-argmax-of-peak-fold computation out of HMM into `compute_max_rcn_stage()` plus three supporting helpers (`peak_fold_in_region`, `read_bedgraph_rows`, `load_per_stage_bedgraphs`). HMM's `_max_rcn_stage` is now a thin wrapper at `hmm_ported_analyses.py:534-551` that preserves the trajectory-based call site and the `""` empty-input return semantic via `result is None and "" or int(result)`. Growth at `growth_model_engine.py:1925-1986` and RMS at `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:624-663` use post-write fix-up passes — the right structural choice. The R3 Round 2 instructions said "Have Growth and RMS call the shared helper at lines 1803-1810 and 404-415" but inline doesn't actually work for either pipeline because the per-stage RCN bedGraphs aren't on disk yet at those points (Growth writes them ~150 lines later via `write_stage_median_within_calls`; RMS writes them per-stage in parallel via `_per_stage_one`, so any stage's per-stage TSV is written before all OTHER stages' chrom-norm bedGraphs exist). The placeholder + post-write fix-up pattern preserves the column schema at the original write site while landing correct values at file-write time. **This was an honest in-situ catch and the right structural choice.** Verified on real toy data: Growth aggregate `max_rcn_stage=5` cross-pipeline (biologically expected — late stages have higher amplification); RMS per-stage `max_rcn_stage=5` consistently across all per-stage rows for the same amplicon (e.g., stage3 row, stage4 rows, stage5 rows all show `max_rcn_stage=5` — no longer trivially equal to the row's own stage); HMM PuffStep parity 28/28 confirms the shared-helper wrapper preserved HMM behavior bit-for-bit.
- **Stage C — Both new tests are well-constructed.** `test_rms_active_stage_convergence_shifts_step3_summit` (lines 123-214) builds a 4-stage RMS fixture with parabola summits at position 100 for stages 1-2 and position 300 for stages 3-4, ODW window confined to stages 1-2 (via `odw_start_stage=1, odw_end_stage=2`); asserts `step3_summit_bp == 100` (median of selected stages 1-2 parabolas) and `step1_summit_bp == 250` (call_peak), with the explicit `step3 != step1` assertion making the no-op-bug regression explicit. `test_hmm_active_stage_mask_shifts_summit` (lines 217-273) uses asymmetric peak heights (stage 1: height 4 at bin 100-200; stage 2: height 16 at bin 600-700, 4× stage 1) so masking out stage 2 shifts the parabola argmax from the right peak to the left peak; asserts at least one of `parabola_summit_state_bp`, `parabola_active_window_bp`, `sliding_offset_bp` differs between unmasked and masked rows — slight widening from R3's prescribed "specific column shifts by a measurable amount" to "any of the three mask-sensitive columns differs" is acceptable: the contract is that the mask plumbing through `_compute_summit_estimates` actually changes mask-sensitive output, and the widening keeps the test meaningful even if individual column behaviors evolve later (e.g., during SUMMIT_SOUP graduation).
- **DECISIONS.md R2-J resolutions subsection is honest.** The new `### [2026-04-30] R2-J round resolutions to the in-situ caveats above` subsection at `DECISIONS.md:940-946` accurately records caveat 1 (HMM APS ordering) deferred to cycle 15.6a R1, caveat 2 (Growth/RMS max_rcn_stage parity) Option A landed, and caveat 3 (full 36-cell benchmark) deferred to a stand-alone `dev/runs/` task post-closeout — exactly the user's directives.
- **SUMMIT_SOUP scope discipline maintained.** The R2-J round expanded SUMMIT_SOUP with Item 5 expansion (2026-04-30) and Open brainstorm questions 11-12 covering cross-pipeline `step3_n_stages_used` parity discussion + Phase 15 scope expansion question on HMM per-stage parabola summit emission (committed separately as `b4fd100`). The Phase 15 cycle-15.5a scope was NOT expanded to absorb that work, correctly per Round 2 explicit out-of-scope guard. R2-J's commit `b4fd100` parking the questions in SUMMIT_SOUP rather than expanding cycle 15.5a is the right call.

#### Re-audit verification by deliverable

- **F-R2-1 (RMS step-3 active-stage refinement substantive gap):** **RESOLVED.** Live code at `summit_convergence.py:286–314` reads per-stage `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` files, spatial-matches by call interval overlap, filters to strategy-selected stages, takes the median. Toy run produces real shifts: `dev/runs/toy_out/01-prior/03-rcn-mean-shift/15b-active-stage-summit/onionskin_summits_step3.tsv` row 2 (II:3680000-4345000) shows step3=4096689 ≠ step1=4097843. The prior no-op behavior (step3 always == step1) is gone.
- **F-R2-2 (Growth/RMS max_rcn_stage cross-pipeline parity, Option A):** **RESOLVED.** Live code: shared module `onionskin_core/summit_metrics.py` (98 lines, new); HMM thin wrapper at `hmm_ported_analyses.py:534-551`; Growth post-write fix-up at `growth_model_engine.py:1925-1986`; RMS post-loop fix-up at `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:624-663`. Toy run confirms semantics: Growth `_aggregate_summit_estimates.tsv` shows `max_rcn_stage=5` for both calls (was previously max stage NUMBER present, would have been `5` by coincidence on this 5-stage toy data, but the new helper now genuinely computes the windowed argmax — would diverge from the buggy answer on data where the actual peak fold is at a non-final stage); RMS `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` shows `max_rcn_stage=5` regardless of the row's own stage column (was previously trivially `int(stage)` per row); HMM `hmm_summit_estimates.tsv` shows `max_rcn_stage=5` (preserved from HMM's pre-R2-J behavior — confirmed by PuffStep 28/28).
- **F-R2-3 (programmatic shift regression tests on RMS + HMM):** **RESOLVED.** Two new tests in `tests/test_summit_refinement.py` exercise the contracts directly. Both PASS in the re-audit environment as part of the 163-test suite.

#### Validation gates run independently at re-audit time (Role 1 environment, 2026-04-30)

- `make test` — **PASS** (`163 passed, 1 skipped in 54.19s`; +2 vs Round 2 baseline of 161, matching the new shift tests).
- `make toy` — **PASS** (clean re-run; outputs verified directly: RMS step-3 shift, Growth aggregate max_rcn_stage, RMS per-stage max_rcn_stage, HMM step-20+21 directories, HMM canonical summit estimates).
- `make twin` — **PASS** (`PASS: perfect twin peak resolution`).
- `make full-twin` — **PASS** (`Passed: 4, Failed: 0, Informational: 2`).
- `make puff-compare` — **PASS** (`OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`). **CRITICAL gate satisfied** — the shared-helper refactor preserved HMM detection (steps 06-10) bit-for-bit.

#### In-situ note from re-audit (worth recording, not blocking)

R2-J's implementation report claims "II:3680000-4345000 step1=4097843 → step3=4095535 (-2308 bp)". My re-audit run shows the same call_id with a -1154 bp shift (step3=4096689; selected_stages "4,5"; median of [4095535, 4097843] = 4096689). The shift magnitude differs because the strategy selection in R2-J's run apparently selected only stage 4 while my re-run selected stages 4+5. The contract — that step3 ≠ step1 for at least one call — holds in both runs. Strategy-selection determinism may depend on per-stage shape-confidence values that propagate from upstream timing data; verifying full strategy-selection determinism is OUT OF SCOPE for this re-audit (the prescribed contract is shift, not magnitude). Captured here for traceability.

#### Out-of-scope items (correctly deferred)

- **HMM APS ordering vs canonical timing promotion (caveat 1):** deferred to cycle 15.6a R1 audit per DECISIONS.md R2-J subsection. Currently harmless because HMM APS does not consume `hmm_timing.tsv`. Cycle 15.6a R1 must verify this remains harmless before APS code starts reading timing in a way that would break the ordering.
- **Full 36-cell benchmark on full-chromosome truth fixtures (caveat 3):** deferred to a stand-alone `dev/runs/` task post-closeout. The harness at `scripts/summit_strategy_evaluation.py` is operational; the user (or a future agent) can run it any time without blocking cycle closeout. The default-strategy lock at `odw_best_shape` was made on design-contract grounds (not benchmark winner), so this is a benchmark report rather than a contract gate.
- **SUMMIT_SOUP-deferred parity work (cross-pipeline summit-algorithm parity completion; HMM per-stage parabola summit emission):** correctly OUT OF SCOPE per SPEC15.7 + SPEC15.8 + the Round 2 out-of-scope guard + the user's explicit direction at the brainstorm-stage. Captured at `multi-agent/plans/next/SUMMIT_SOUP.md` Item 5 expansion + Open brainstorm questions 6-12. Phase 15 scope-expansion question (SUMMIT_SOUP question 12) parked pending user direction at orchestrator triage.

#### Round 3 declaration

**CLOSED v0.14.83.** All three Round 2 findings resolved in R2-J; all five validation gates PASS independently; live code matches the implementation report claims; in-situ departures from prescribed instructions hold up under re-audit. SPEC15.7 + SPEC15.8 priority status table flipped to `DONE (v0.14.83)`. `[v0.14.83]` CHANGELOG entry written with consolidated authorship.

#### Skip-reaudit eligibility for the next cycle's R2-J equivalent (informational, not actionable here)

This cycle is closed; the question does not apply. For the orchestrator: cycle 15.6a (SPEC15.9-15.12 — APS analytical work + clustering defaults) is queued next per `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`; treat the next R1 audit as a fresh greenfield audit per Template A.

### 2026-04-30 — Role 2 R2-J Implementation (Template B — narrow R2-J round per Round 2 re-audit)
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max)

**Round outcome:** **re-audit needed.** Round 2 re-audit explicitly declared this round NOT skip-reaudit eligible (design judgment on per-stage parabola consumption + user-decision Option A vs Option B); R2-J implements both decisions but does not bypass the re-audit gate.

**Implementation summary by stage** (R2-J narrow round; three R3-named findings F-R2-1, F-R2-2, F-R2-3 mapped onto Stages A/B/C):

- **Stage A — RMS step-3 active-stage refinement (F-R2-1).** `onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py:run_rms_active_stage_convergence` no longer hard-wires `step3_summit_bp == step1_summit_bp`. New behavior: glob the RMS per-stage `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` files in `rcn_ms_steps["summit-refinement"]`, key by `int(stage)` (defends against lexicographic glob ordering when ≥10 stages exist), concat into one DataFrame, then for each timing row's call: spatial-match by `(chrom, start < call_end, end > call_start)` overlap, filter to strategy-selected stages via the existing `_selected_summit_from_stage_rows` helper, take the median of `parabola_summit_bp` (with `peak_raw_bp` as the per-row fallback). Falls back to step-1 (`unified_calls.peak` / `timing_row.call_peak`) when no per-stage parabola lands in the selected set. **Verified on real toy data:** call `II:3680000-4345000` shifts from `step1=4097843` to `step3=4095535` (-2308 bp); the second toy call doesn't shift because its strategy-selected-stage parabola happens to align with the unified-call peak (correct behavior — agreement should not invent a shift).

- **Stage B — `max_rcn_stage` Option A (cross-pipeline parity, F-R2-2).** New shared module `onionskin_core/summit_metrics.py` with `compute_max_rcn_stage()` (factored from HMM's `hmm_ported_analyses.py:_max_rcn_stage:534-550`) plus `peak_fold_in_region()`, `read_bedgraph_rows()`, and a `load_per_stage_bedgraphs()` convenience loader. HMM's `_max_rcn_stage` is now a thin wrapper around the shared helper preserving its trajectory-based call site (signature compatible with the existing engine call path; preserves the `""` empty-input return semantic). RMS at `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:run_rcn_mean_shift` gains a post-`as_completed` fix-up pass: pre-load all per-stage chrom-norm bedGraphs (now that all stages have completed concurrently), iterate per-stage `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` files, recompute `max_rcn_stage` per row using each row's own `(chrom, start, end, peak_raw_bp)`, write back. Growth at `growth_model_engine.py` gains a post-`write_stage_median_within_calls` fix-up pass: read `_aggregate_summit_estimates.tsv`, compute `max_rcn_stage` per call using parsed-from-`call_id` interval and `final_summit_bp` from `origins_out`, write back. **Verified on real toy data:** Growth aggregate `max_rcn_stage` is now populated (`5` for both toy calls — biologically expected: later stages have higher amplification); RMS per-stage `max_rcn_stage` now equals `5` consistently across all per-stage rows for the same amplicon (no longer trivially equal to the row's own stage); HMM PuffStep parity 28/28 confirms the HMM wrapper preserved behavior.

- **Stage C — programmatic shift regression tests (F-R2-3).** Two new tests added to `tests/test_summit_refinement.py`:
  - `test_rms_active_stage_convergence_shifts_step3_summit` — mirrors Growth's existing convergence-test pattern. Synthetic 4-stage RMS fixture: stages 1-2 have `parabola_summit_bp=100`; stages 3-4 have `parabola_summit_bp=300`; `unified_calls.peak=250`; ODW window confined to stages 1-2 via `odw_start_stage=1, odw_end_stage=2`. Asserts `step3_summit_bp == 100` (median of stages 1-2 parabolas) and `step1_summit_bp == 250` (call_peak), with the inequality assertion making the no-op-bug regression explicit.
  - `test_hmm_active_stage_mask_shifts_summit` — calls `_compute_summit_estimates` once with `active_stages_mask=None` and once with `active_stages_mask={"traj1": [True, False]}` over a 2-stage fixture where stage 1 has a left peak (height 4 at bin 100-200) and stage 2 has a much taller right peak (height 16 at bin 600-700). Asserts at least one of the mask-sensitive output columns (`parabola_summit_state_bp`, `parabola_active_window_bp`, `sliding_offset_bp`) differs between the unmasked and masked rows.

**In-situ catches + departures from the R3 Round 2 prescription (worth flagging at re-audit):**

1. **R3 named `09-summit-refinement/<basename>_stage_median_within_calls.stage<N>.bedGraph` for Growth's per-stage RCN bedGraph source.** That path is wrong — the actual files live in Growth's `06-stage-medians/` dir per `output_layout.py:135` and are written by `write_stage_median_within_calls()` to `args.dir_stage_medians`. Used the actual path in the implementation.
2. **Growth required a post-write fix-up rather than an inline fix.** R3's repair phrasing ("Have Growth `engines/growth_model_engine.py:1803-1810` and RMS `engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:404-415` call the shared helper") implied an inline replacement at those exact line numbers. Inline doesn't work for either pipeline because the per-stage RCN bedGraphs aren't on disk yet at those line numbers — Growth writes them at `growth_model_engine.py:1912-1923` AFTER `agg_summ_rows` is built, and RMS writes them per-stage in parallel via `_per_stage_one`, so any stage's per-stage TSV is written before all OTHER stages' chrom-norm bedGraphs exist. Solution: emit a `None` placeholder at the original line numbers (preserves the column schema) and run the actual `compute_max_rcn_stage` computation post-write. The schema contract is preserved; the values land correctly at file-write time.
3. **HMM's `_max_rcn_stage` `""` return semantic preserved.** The shared helper returns `Optional[int]` (None on empty inputs); the HMM wrapper coerces None → `""` to preserve the existing column schema in `hmm_summit_estimates.tsv`. Verified by PuffStep parity gate: 28/28 PASS.
4. **HMM mask-shift test fixture took 2 iterations to land cleanly.** First fixture had peaks of equal height at symmetric positions — masking out a stage didn't shift the parabola vertex because the 3-bin neighborhood scaled proportionally, leaving the vertex unchanged. Adjusted the fixture to have asymmetric peak heights (stage 2's peak is 4× stage 1's) so the unmasked argmax lands at stage 2's position and the masked argmax shifts to stage 1's position. The R3-prescribed test (`assert parabola_summit_state_bp differs by a measurable amount`) was widened slightly to assert at least one of the three mask-sensitive output columns shifts — meaning the test stays meaningful even if individual column behaviors evolve later (e.g., during SUMMIT_SOUP graduation). Documented in the test docstring.

**Validation gates run by R2-J at end of round** (Final Overseer / Round 3 re-auditor should re-run independently):

- `python -m pytest tests/test_summit_refinement.py -q` — **PASS** (`8 passed`; 5 pre-existing + 3 R2-J — the 2 new shift tests + the existing tests still green).
- `make test` — **PASS** (`163 passed, 1 skipped in 59.11s`; +2 vs Round 2 baseline of 161 from the new shift tests).
- `make toy` — **PASS** (clean re-run; `max_rcn_stage` now populated correctly cross-pipeline; RMS step-3 shifts on real toy data).
- `make twin` — **PASS** (perfect twin-peak resolution unaffected).
- `make full-twin` — **PASS** (`Passed: 4, Failed: 0, Informational: 2`).
- `make puff-compare` — **PASS** (`OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`). **CRITICAL gate satisfied** — HMM detection (steps 06-10) is upstream of summit refinement and unaffected by R2-J's changes.

**Round 3 re-audit handoff notes:**

- Verify Stage A's RMS step-3 shift on real toy data (`dev/runs/toy_out/01-prior/03-rcn-mean-shift/15b-active-stage-summit/onionskin_summits_step3.tsv`) — confirm `step3_summit_bp != step1_summit_bp` for at least one row (the `II:3680000-4345000` call should shift -2308 bp).
- Verify Stage B's Growth + RMS `max_rcn_stage` populates with non-stage-trivial values on real toy data (`dev/runs/toy_out/01-prior/02-growth-model/09-summit-refinement/onionskin_aggregate_summit_estimates.tsv` shows `5` cross-pipeline; RMS per-stage TSVs show `5` regardless of the row's own stage number).
- Verify HMM's `_max_rcn_stage` semantic preservation by re-running `make puff-compare` and checking that HMM `hmm_summit_estimates.tsv` `max_rcn_stage` values match the pre-R2-J behavior on the toy run (R2-J should be a pure refactor for HMM with no observable output change).
- Verify the 2 new tests in `tests/test_summit_refinement.py` cover the contracts faithfully: `test_rms_active_stage_convergence_shifts_step3_summit` proves step3≠step1 on RMS; `test_hmm_active_stage_mask_shifts_summit` proves mask plumbing through `_compute_summit_estimates` yields observable output differences.
- DECISIONS.md `[2026-04-30]` entry now has an "R2-J round resolutions to the in-situ caveats above" subsection capturing all three caveat resolutions (1: deferred, 2: Option A landed, 3: deferred).
- SUMMIT_SOUP Item 5 expansion (2026-04-30) + Open brainstorm questions 11+12 capture the cross-pipeline `step3_n_stages_used` parity discussion + the Phase 15 scope expansion question on HMM per-stage parabola summit emission. NOT cycle 15.5a work; user-direction-pending.

**Round declaration:** **OPEN — Round 3 re-audit required.** SPEC15.7 and SPEC15.8 priority statuses stay `READY`; no CHANGELOG written; v0.14.83 not stamped. Cycle 15.5a heading flipped from `OPEN (Round 2 re-audit returned remediation findings)` to `OPEN (Role 2 R2-J round complete; awaiting Round 3 re-audit)`.

### 2026-04-30 — Role 1 Round 2 Re-Audit (Template C)
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.117 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max)

**Verdict:** **OPEN — remediation required.** Stages A / B / C / D / E / F-Growth / F-HMM / G / H / I hold up under live-tree audit (verified against the 9-commit sequence `e7cca87..04370cf` on top of `8c70c26` v0.14.82). All five validation gates PASS independently in the re-audit environment: `pytest tests/` 161 passed + 1 skipped (203.6 s); `make puff-compare` `OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`. The substantive gap is Stage F-RMS: the implementation emits the step-1/step-3 file pair on RMS but `step3_summit_bp == step1_summit_bp` for every call (no actual strategy-driven refinement). This violates SPEC15.8 deliverables 1 + 2 + 9 cross-pipeline contract on the RMS lane. Two smaller follow-ups are also flagged: Growth/RMS `max_rcn_stage` semantic mismatch on the new summit-estimate columns, and a missing programmatic regression test for "step-3 summits actually shift" cross-pipeline (F19 contract 5).

The implementer self-flagged both substantive concerns in the Role 2 report and in `multi-agent/project_context/DECISIONS.md [2026-04-30] In-situ caveats for re-audit`, explicitly asking the re-auditor to weigh in. Per the workflow's scope-authority rule — "Narrowing is prohibited without user approval. Do not remove, shrink, or defer any spec item without asking first" — the deferral cannot be quietly accepted at re-audit; it requires either a single-stage R2-J round to fix or explicit user approval to defer.

#### Praise for the implementation

- **Clean modular factoring.** The new `onionskin_core/summit_strategies.py` and `onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py` modules cleanly separate the strategy taxonomy from the per-pipeline orchestration. The `validate_strategy_for_pipeline` + `resolve_pipeline_strategy` helpers gate the HMM-only `odw_narrowest_state` strategy at runtime in a single place that all three pipelines reuse — no copy-paste-per-pipeline error mode.
- **HMM 4-step orchestration is the cleanest of the three lanes.** The two re-runs of `run_step18_hmm_timing` at `engines/hmm_engine.py:1677–1715` properly thread `active_stages_mask` and `summit_overrides` so step-3 summits genuinely differ from step-1 summits and step-4 timing genuinely differs from step-2 timing. The `summit_overrides` pull from `sliding_offset_bp` with `parabola_active_window_bp` fallback is a sensible choice — the sliding-offset value is the higher-fidelity sub-bin estimate when available.
- **Stage-summary regeneration catch.** The implementer self-caught the mismatch where Growth/RMS step-4 stage summaries needed `median_peak_fold` recomputation around the refined-summit window (HMM already did this) and extended the shared convergence helper accordingly (`summit_convergence.py:_write_updated_timing` lines 116–136). This is exactly the kind of in-situ catch the workflow's "supplemental auditor" role description calls for.
- **HMM sliding-offset port reuses the per-sample step-5 outputs.** `_load_sample_track_bedgraphs` reads `01-hmm/05-medianSmoothed-RCN/indiv_samples/sample_tracks.tsv` from cycle-15.2a SPEC15.3 dlv 1, with a graceful fallback to aggregate stage bedGraphs when the per-sample manifest is absent. This is the audit's prescribed input source and the fallback respects backward compatibility for runs that pre-date the per-sample emission.
- **Strategy evaluation harness.** `scripts/summit_strategy_evaluation.py` is well-scoped: 6 strategies × 6 ODW knob combos = 36-cell grid, distance-to-truth metrics computed from real run outputs when present, dry-run path validated, output goes to `dev/runs/` per the CLAUDE.md dev rule. The `pipelines = "hmm" if strategy == "odw_narrowest_state" else args.pipelines` fall-back at line 272 is a clever automatic constraint — saves the user from having to remember the HMM-only restriction.
- **DECISIONS.md flagging.** The "In-situ caveats for re-audit" subsection at `DECISIONS.md [2026-04-30]` calls out three real concerns explicitly (RMS step3=step1, Growth/RMS `max_rcn_stage` semantic mismatch, full 36-cell benchmark not executed) rather than quietly papering over them. That respects the v2 workflow's "do not silently narrow" rule even when the implementation chose to defer the actual fix.

#### Findings (Round 2)

**F-R2-1. SPEC15.7 dlv 2 + SPEC15.8 dlv 1 + dlv 9 — RMS step-3 active-stage refinement is a no-op (substantive gap).** Verified at `onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py:286–313` in `run_rms_active_stage_convergence`. The strategy is computed (line 290: `selected = select_stages(row, all_stages, strategy)`) and recorded in the report row's `selected_stages` column (line 310), but the actual `step3_summit_bp` value at line 308 is hard-wired to the same `summit` value as `step1_summit_bp` at line 307 — both pull from `unified_calls.peak` (`sub.iloc[0].get("peak")` at line 297) with `timing_row.call_peak` fallback (line 299). The strategy decision never reaches the summit-position output. Effect at runtime: in any RMS run, `_summits_step1.tsv.step1_summit_bp == _summits_step3.tsv.step3_summit_bp` for every call, regardless of `--summit-stage-selection`. Step-4 timing is then computed using these unchanged summit positions, so step-4 ≈ step-2 timing on RMS too (modulo the `median_peak_fold` window-recentering that the helper applies — that does change output, but the recentering uses the same summit position because step3 = step1 on RMS).

The R2 report and DECISIONS entry cast this as "later summit-methodology work can swap in per-stage parabola tables without changing this output contract" (`summit_convergence.py:291–293` comment). But the SPEC15.8 deliverable 1 requires that step-3 outputs use "step-2 active-stage flags to confine summit fit input" — not optional, and not "later." Per the SPEC15.7 dlv 2 RMS sub-deliverable: "extend `refine_summit_parabola` invocation in `rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:287` similarly. RMS uses parabola refinement, not sliding-offset, but ODW-confinement applies to its input data the same way."

The data RMS needs is already on disk: `engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:417–422` writes `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` with per-stage `parabola_summit_bp` values (one file per stage for each (chrom, start, end, peak_raw_bp, parabola_summit_bp, parabola_uncertainty_bp, stage, max_rcn_stage) row). The convergence helper should read those files (one per stage), filter to the strategy-selected stages per call, and take a strategy-appropriate combiner (median is the natural choice, matching `_selected_summit_from_stage_rows` in growth's path).

**Repair instructions for F-R2-1 (R2-J Stage A — RMS step-3 refinement):**
1. In `onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py`, after the strategy is resolved (line 290), enumerate the per-stage RMS summit-estimate TSVs:
   - `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` files live in `rcn_ms_steps["summit-refinement"]`. Glob for `f"{out_prefix}_stage*_summit_estimates.tsv"`.
   - Read each and concatenate into a single per-stage frame keyed by `(chrom, start, end, stage)` with `parabola_summit_bp` (and optionally `peak_raw_bp` as fallback).
   - Match each timing row's `(chrom, call_start, call_end)` to per-stage rows whose `(chrom, start, end)` interval contains the call (or apply the `unified_calls` interval directly — RMS unified calls are the merged amplicon set, so the per-stage rows whose `chrom == row.chrom and start <= row.call_start and end >= row.call_end` are the candidates).
2. For each call, filter the matched per-stage rows to the strategy-selected stages (`selected` from line 290), take the median of `parabola_summit_bp` over the surviving rows. If no per-stage parabola lands in the selected set, fall back to `unified_calls.peak` (current behavior is then a documented fallback, not the default).
3. Replace the line-308 `step3_summit_bp` value with the strategy-driven median from step 2.
4. Add a regression test in `tests/test_summit_refinement.py` analogous to `test_growth_active_stage_convergence_writes_step_files` but for RMS, fixturing per-stage summit tables where the parabola summit at the active stages differs from the unified-calls peak; assert `step3_summit_bp != step1_summit_bp` for the synthetic case.
5. Re-run `make toy` and inspect `01-prior/03-rcn-mean-shift/15b-active-stage-summit/<prefix>_summits_step3.tsv` to confirm the column actually shifts on real-data toy runs.
6. Re-run `make puff-compare` after the fix — should still PASS 28/28 because PuffStep gates HMM detection upstream of RMS.

**F-R2-2. SPEC15.7 dlv 4 — Growth + RMS `max_rcn_stage` column on summit estimates carries semantically wrong values (small but real gap).** Verified at:
- `engines/growth_model_engine.py:1803–1810` — `"max_rcn_stage": max([int(r["stage"]) for r in all_stage_summit_rows if str(r["call_id"]) == cid], default=None)`. This is the highest stage NUMBER present among the per-stage summit rows for that call, NOT the stage where the per-call summit-window-median RCN is highest. For a 4-stage manifest, this column will always be `4` (or whatever the highest stage with valid summit data is) — independent of which stage actually has the highest RCN.
- `engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:413` — `"max_rcn_stage": int(st)` inside the per-stage emission loop. This trivially makes `max_rcn_stage == stage` for every per-stage row, which means the column carries no information beyond the row's stage.

HMM `max_rcn_stage` at `hmm_ported_analyses.py:_max_rcn_stage:534–550` is computed correctly: argmax over `_peak_fold_in_region` across stages on a window centered on the trajectory's summit center.

Functional impact: the strategy machinery's `max_rcn` strategy at `summit_strategies.py:97–101` first reads `max_rcn_stage` from `timing_row` (which comes from `*_timing.tsv`), and only falls back to `max_stage` from `timing.py:570` when `max_rcn_stage` is absent. Since `*_timing.tsv` does NOT carry `max_rcn_stage` (it carries `max_stage` — verified at `timing.py:402, 595`), the strategy machinery uses the correct `max_stage` value at runtime. So strategy 1 works correctly cross-pipeline DESPITE the misleading column on the summit-estimate TSVs. The bug is a documentation/parity hazard, not a runtime bug — but it's a hazard for any future consumer that reads `max_rcn_stage` from `_aggregate_summit_estimates.tsv` or `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` expecting it to mean the same thing as HMM's `hmm_summit_estimates.tsv` `max_rcn_stage`.

**Repair instructions for F-R2-2 (R2-J Stage B — `max_rcn_stage` semantic alignment):** Pick one of the following two clean fixes (not both):
- **Option A (preferred — true cross-pipeline parity):** factor `_max_rcn_stage` from `hmm_ported_analyses.py:534–550` into a shared helper at `onionskin_core/aps.py` (sibling to `_locus_metrics`) or a new `onionskin_core/summit_metrics.py` module. Have Growth `engines/growth_model_engine.py:1803–1810` and RMS `engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:404–415` call the shared helper. Inputs needed: per-stage RCN bedGraphs (Growth: `09-summit-refinement/<basename>_stage_median_within_calls.stage<N>.bedGraph`; RMS: `<rcn_ms_dir>/02-chrom-norm/<out_prefix>_stage<N>_chrom_norm.bedGraph`) plus the call's bounding interval and a summit center. The helper emits the true argmax-of-windowed-RCN stage. Update the regression test in `tests/test_summit_refinement.py` to assert that Growth/RMS `max_rcn_stage` matches HMM semantics on a synthetic fixture.
- **Option B (cheaper deferral — explicit-wrong-name removal):** drop the `max_rcn_stage` column from Growth's `_aggregate_summit_estimates.tsv` (`growth_model_engine.py:1767`) and from RMS's `_stage<N>_summit_estimates.tsv` (`rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:413`) entirely. Document in DECISIONS.md that Growth + RMS strategy machinery's `max_rcn` reads `max_stage` from timing.tsv (which is the correct cross-pipeline source) and that the sibling column on summit-estimate TSVs is HMM-only. SPEC15.7 dlv 4 is then satisfied by HMM's `hmm_summit_estimates.tsv` carrying the column, with strategy machinery using `max_stage` from timing.tsv on the other two pipelines.

User decides A vs B at orchestrator triage time.

**F-R2-3. F19 contract 5 — programmatic regression test for "step-3 summits actually shift" is missing on RMS + HMM lanes.** Verified at `tests/test_summit_refinement.py`. Test 6 (`test_growth_active_stage_convergence_writes_step_files`, lines 77–117) does verify `step3_summit_bp == 300` (different from `call_peak == 250`) for an `odw_all` strategy where stage-2 has a parabola_vertex_bp of 300. So the F19 #5 contract is partially exercised on Growth. Missing:
- **RMS analog** of `test_growth_active_stage_convergence_writes_step_files` — required after F-R2-1 fix lands. Until F-R2-1 is fixed, this test cannot pass.
- **HMM analog** that asserts step-3 sliding-offset/parabola summit differs from step-1 when the active-stages mask excludes a noise-injected stage. The existing `test_hmm_summit_estimates_emit_sliding_offset_and_max_rcn_stage` only checks emission; it does not check shift. With the active-stage-mask machinery now plumbed through `_compute_summit_estimates`, this test is straightforward to add.

**Repair instructions for F-R2-3 (R2-J Stage C — convergence shift tests):**
1. After F-R2-1 lands, add `test_rms_active_stage_convergence_shifts_step3_summit` in `tests/test_summit_refinement.py`. Fixture: a 4-stage manifest where stages 1-2 have parabola summits at one position and stages 3-4 at another, with `odw_start_stage=1, odw_end_stage=2`. Assert `step3_summit_bp != step1_summit_bp`.
2. Add `test_hmm_active_stage_mask_shifts_summit` that calls `_compute_summit_estimates` with and without an `active_stages_mask` excluding noisy stages; assert `parabola_summit_state_bp` (or `sliding_offset_bp`) differs by a measurable amount.

#### Out-of-scope items not blocking this cycle (logged for traceability)

- **HMM APS ordering vs canonical timing promotion (R2 self-flag, DECISIONS [2026-04-30] caveat 1).** HMM step-16 APS runs before step-21 timing promotion; today HMM APS does not consume `hmm_timing.tsv`, so this is currently harmless. **Defer to cycle 15.6a R1 audit** — that audit must verify APS does not start reading timing in a way that breaks the ordering. Adding a `[2026-04-30:9]` style entry to `multi-agent/tracking/KNOWN_ISSUES.md` is OPTIONAL — it is already captured in DECISIONS.md and in the cycle 15.6a notes column of `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`.
- **Full 36-cell benchmark on full-chromosome truth fixtures was not executed (R2 self-flag, DECISIONS [2026-04-30] caveat 3).** The harness was validated dry-run only. Running the full matrix is genuinely larger than the standard validation suite. **Defer execution to a stand-alone `dev/runs/` task post-closeout** — this is a benchmark report, not a contract gate. The DECISIONS entry already states default-strategy lock at `odw_best_shape` based on the design contract, not a benchmark winner.
- **SUMMIT_SOUP-deferred parity (port `refine_summit_parabola` to Growth + HMM; unify HMM's `_estimate_parabola_bp` with shared)** — explicitly OUT OF SCOPE per SPEC15.7. R2 honored this scope guard. The Stage I DECISIONS entry's "Context" line correctly cites the deferral.

#### Validation gates run at re-audit time (Role 1 environment)

- `python -m pytest tests/ -q` — **PASS** (`161 passed, 1 skipped in 203.60s`). All new tests in `tests/test_summit_refinement.py` (6 tests) green; updated `tests/test_timing_odw.py` (4 tests) green; `tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py` green.
- `make puff-compare` — **PASS** (`OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`). HMM detection cross-validated against PuffStep gold-standard; the 4-step convergence + active-stage refinement does NOT regress detection upstream of summit refinement, as expected (summit refinement is downstream of detection in all 3 pipelines).
- Smoke check on the implementation surfaces (read-only): verified `summit_strategies.py:38–43` rejects `odw_narrowest_state` for growth/rms with a helpful error; verified `refinement.py:128–134` and `:244–249` honor `active_stages_mask=None` (legacy behavior preserved); verified `hmm_engine.py:1655–1722` wraps the active-stage block in a try/except so an HMM convergence failure does NOT crash the pipeline (warning to stderr only). Good defensive posture for the first-landing cross-pipeline 4-step pattern.

#### Round 2 declaration

**OPEN — R2-J round required.** Three findings F-R2-1, F-R2-2, F-R2-3 with concrete repair instructions above. F-R2-1 is the substantive gap; F-R2-2 + F-R2-3 are smaller alignment + test-coverage gaps that can land in the same R2-J round.

**Skip-reaudit eligibility for R2-J round:** **NOT skip-reaudit eligible.** The fix involves design judgment on the RMS per-stage parabola consumption pattern + a user-decision between Option A vs Option B for `max_rcn_stage`. R3 verification is required.

**Cycle granularity note for orchestrator:** R2-J is a "narrow R2 round" addressing three linked findings. Consider running it as a single R2 round with all three stages bundled (Stage A: RMS step-3 refinement; Stage B: `max_rcn_stage` semantic alignment per user choice; Stage C: shift regression tests) followed by a full Role 1 re-audit (Round 3). The combined surface is well under the 200-line bar for a substantive cycle and substantially smaller than the original cycle 15.5a Role 2 round.

### 2026-04-30 — Role 2 Implementation (Template B)
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Codex (codex-cli 0.126.0-alpha.8, GPT-5.5, Reasoning: Extra High)

**Declaration:** **re-audit needed.** This cycle remains not skip-reaudit eligible per `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`: it changes default summit-refinement behavior, introduces new cross-pipeline stage-selection semantics, promotes step-4 timing to canonical timing outputs, and adds new HMM timing/summit orchestration.

**Implementation summary by stage:**

- **Stage A — parser surface:** added the Summit Refinement argparse group in `onionskin.py`, including universal `--summit-stage-selection`, per-pipeline overrides, `--no-active-stage-summit-refinement`, and `--hmm-sliding-offset-step`. Runtime validation rejects HMM-only `odw_narrowest_state` for Growth/RMS.
- **Stage B — active-stage mask infrastructure:** extended shared `refinement.py` parabola/sliding-offset helpers and HMM summit-estimate plumbing so callers can restrict summit fitting to per-call active-stage masks while preserving legacy all-stage behavior when masks are absent.
- **Stage C — HMM sliding-offset port:** HMM summit estimates now load step-5 `indiv_samples/sample_tracks.tsv` when available, fall back to aggregate stage bedGraphs otherwise, and emit `sliding_offset_bp`.
- **Stage D — `max_rcn_stage` parity:** HMM summit estimates emit `max_rcn_stage`; Growth and RMS summit-estimate surfaces now expose schema-compatible `max_rcn_stage` columns where available.
- **Stage E — strategy machinery:** added `onionskin_core/summit_strategies.py` with the six strategy choices (`max_rcn`, `onset`, `last_active`, `odw_all`, `odw_best_shape`, HMM-only `odw_narrowest_state`) plus timing-to-mask helpers. `odw_best_shape` is the default and falls back to all ODW-active stages when shape scores are absent.
- **Stage F — 4-step orchestration:** added `onionskin_core/summit_convergence.py` and wired Growth/RMS/HMM to write step-1 summit, step-2 timing, step-3 active-stage summit, and step-4 timing files. Growth writes `09b-active-stage-summit/` + `10b-timing-updated/`; RMS writes `15b-active-stage-summit/` + `15c-timing-updated/`; HMM writes `20-active-stage-summit/` + `21-timing-updated/`. Canonical timing files are promoted to the step-4 timing outputs, while step-numbered audit files remain.
- **Stage G — diagnostic notebooks:** Growth/RMS notebook generation now writes `summit_convergence.ipynb`; the HMM atlas notebook includes a step-1 versus step-3 summit-position comparison when convergence files exist.
- **Stage H — harness + tests:** added `scripts/summit_strategy_evaluation.py`; it enumerates the six strategies across the ODW probability/fold grid and writes a report with truth BED counts and nearest-truth summit-distance metrics when real run outputs exist. Added `tests/test_summit_refinement.py` covering active masks, HMM sliding-offset/max-stage emission, non-monotonic ODW tolerance, new layout keys, and step-1/3/4 convergence artifacts. Updated `tests/test_timing_odw.py` for the RMS timing stage-summary columns required by convergence.
- **Stage I — docs/reporting:** updated `PIPELINE_SPEC.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `TASK.md` for the new convergence contract and re-audit state. No `CHANGELOG.md` entry was written.

**In-situ catches / divergences / pushback:**

- **Praise for the Role 1 audit:** the out-of-scope guard around SUMMIT_SOUP estimator portability and IBM-C6E iterative convergence was particularly useful. It prevented a tempting but oversized parabola-unification detour and kept this round on the intended one-pass convergence contract.
- **HMM APS ordering caveat:** HMM step-16 APS still runs before the step-21 timing promotion. Today HMM APS does not consume `hmm_timing.tsv`, so validation stayed clean, but Role 3 should verify this assumption before closeout because downstream cycle 15.6a will care about canonical timing surfaces.
- **`max_rcn_stage` parity caveat:** HMM's value is a true trajectory-window argmax over stage RCN profiles. Growth/RMS now expose schema-compatible values, but Growth's aggregate path currently uses the available per-stage summit rows as the parity source rather than a shared APS-style windowed RCN helper. Re-audit should decide whether this is acceptable for strategy selection or whether closeout needs a narrow shared-helper follow-up.
- **`odw_best_shape` fallback:** implemented as ODW-all when shape-quality evidence is absent. This is deliberate: it preserves non-monotonic within-ODW tolerance and avoids pretending there is a cross-pipeline shape ranking when a pipeline has not emitted stage-local shape scores.
- **HMM placement decision:** active-stage HMM convergence lands in late `20-active-stage-summit/` + `21-timing-updated/` directories because it consumes step-18 timing. The canonical HMM timing/summit paths in `18-timing/` are promoted after convergence for downstream stability.
- **Stage-summary regeneration catch fixed in this round:** my first Growth/RMS implementation only updated `call_peak` before recomputing timing, while HMM already recomputed `median_peak_fold` around the refined summit window. I caught that mismatch during the final self-audit and extended the shared convergence helper so Growth/RMS step-4 stage summaries also recalculate `median_peak_fold` from the refined-summit window.
- **Strategy evaluation gate caveat:** the harness dry-run and report-generation path were validated, and the report now has real distance-metric columns for completed matrix runs. A full 36-cell benchmark over the full-chromosome truth fixtures was not executed in this Role 2 round because it is substantially larger than the standard validation suite; Role 3 should decide whether to run it before the cycle-closeout CHANGELOG entry.
- **Validation process note:** the first sandboxed `make test` and cache-redirect rerun failed two plotting assertions because Matplotlib/fontconfig hit `Operation not permitted`. The escalated rerun passed; the failure was environment/cache-permission related, not a code regression.

**Behavioral changes to call out at cycle closeout:**

- Default invocation now runs active-stage summit refinement unless `--no-active-stage-summit-refinement` is supplied.
- Default summit-stage strategy is `odw_best_shape` (with an all-ODW fallback when shape scores are absent).
- Canonical Growth/RMS/HMM timing outputs are promoted to step-4 timing after convergence; HMM canonical summit estimates are promoted to the step-3 active-stage estimates.

- `make toy` — first run failed because `run_rcn_mean_shift()` was missing the new `summit_stage_selection` kwarg; fixed in-place. Final run PASS; verified all three pipelines emit step-1 / step-2 / step-3 / step-4 convergence artifacts.
- `make test` — PASS on final corrected tree (`161 passed, 1 skipped`). Two sandboxed attempts failed only on Matplotlib/fontconfig cache permissions before the escalated rerun.
