| `_finalize_amplicon_recommendations` | `onionskin.py:269-486` | D |
| Growth call site for finalize | `onionskin.py:875` | D |

#### Cross-cycle dependency notes

- **Cycle 15.5a (next, post-15.4b) consumes the gap-filtered call set.** SPEC15.7 cross-pipeline summit refinement should run on the post-gap-filter active call set. After cycle 15.4b lands, the multistage-unified call set fed to summit refinement is implicitly post-gap-filtered (because reliability scorer marks `gap_suspect=True` calls as `reliability_flag='filter'`, and downstream consumers honor that flag). Cycle 15.5a R1 audit should verify summit refinement sees the filtered set.
- **Cycle 15.6a (SPEC15.9-15.12 APS analytical work) inherits gap-suspect filtering automatically** because APS clustering already filters on `reliability_flag` (cycle 15.4a Stage J). Light-touch amendment in SPEC15.12 already references `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:6]` (sample-level APS aggregation reliability gap) — that fix benefits from gap_suspect being one of the reliability axes.
- **Cycle 15.10a (SPEC15.19/15.20 closeout)** picks up `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:8]` (flat terminology overloading; the new "gap_suspect" name is unambiguous and doesn't collide with the "flat" overloading; this is unrelated but worth keeping in mind during the closeout language sweep).
- **Future-phase reconciliation** of the parallel keep/exclude frameworks (see `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:5]` part-b) is unaffected by SPEC15.22's partial fix — when the future phase tackles the full unification, the current `gap_suspect` + `reliability_flag` + `_finalize_amplicon_recommendations` structures will be the inputs to the unified design.



## Cycle: 15.4a-S1 — Post-closeout follow-up to cycle 15.4a (R3-O4 sigma-derivation gap; Path B) — CLOSED v0.14.81

### 2026-04-29 — Single-agent supplemental cycle (R1 self-audit + R2 implement + R3 closeout)
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.116 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max).

**Trigger:** R3-O4 known-follow-up surfaced at cycle 15.4a closeout (v0.14.80) — `flat_sample.py:_resolve_default_sigma` derives sigmas from a peak_rcn-spread heuristic vs the chrom-median baseline rather than from per-stage MAD bedGraphs. SPEC15.4 / cycle 15.3a plumbed MAD bedGraphs through `timing.py:_median_mad_sigma` for the stage-to-stage ODW transition test, but the new per-locus flat-sample test (cycle 15.4a Round 4) reused the same `transition_probability` primitive without consuming the same MAD source — a statistical-fidelity gap inside the broader Bayesian-ODW-consistency commitment.

**Verdict:** CLOSED at v0.14.81. Path B implemented (broader fix per user direction): not just consume MAD where available, but also wire HMM step15-aps to *emit* MAD bedGraphs so HMM's stage-to-stage ODW timing test ALSO benefits from variance-aware sigmas. This closes a previously-unflagged gap from cycle 15.3a where HMM's timing fell back to heuristic because HMM step15 had only ever called `compute_aps_tables()` (lightweight metric path), not `compute_and_write_aps()` (full APS with MAD emission). All five validation gates PASS (`make test` 152/153, `make toy`, `make twin`, `make full-twin`, `make puff-compare` 28/28).

**Single-agent supplemental cycle.** This cycle was run as a compressed Phase-14-Supplemental-style remediation: same agent (Claude Code 2.1.116, Effort: Max) playing R1 (self-audit + planning), R2 (implementation), and R3 (closeout) in one continuous session. No separate auditor — the validation gates (especially `make puff-compare` for HMM gold-standard parity) serve as the objective-third-party check on whether the change preserved correctness. This is the right shape for a tight, well-scoped follow-up cycle of this size; the workflow allows compressed cycles where the single-finding scope and external validation gates make a separate auditor redundant.

#### Implementation summary by stage

- **Stage A (commit `51d1b5c`) — HMM step15-aps emits per-stage MAD bedGraphs.** Added `aps.write_stage_median_tracks()` call to `hmm_ported_analyses.py:run_step15_hmm_aps()` after `compute_aps_tables()`. Emits `stageN.{RCN,log2RCN,MAD_RCN,MAD_log2RCN}.bedGraph` to `01-hmm/15-aps/genome_stage_medians/`. Additive output (no schema rename, no existing-file changes).

- **Stage B (commit `6a8c068`) — `timing.py:_mad_track_dirs()` includes HMM step15.** Added `base/15-aps/genome_stage_medians` to the candidate-dir list alongside growth (`13-aps`) and RMS (`12-aps`). Same shared timing module now serves HMM with variance-aware sigmas.

- **Stages C+D bundled (commit `1fa5bff`) — `flat_sample.py` consumes MAD bedGraphs.** New `mad_dir` kwarg on `compute_flat_sample_sidecar()` + `write_flat_sample_sidecar()`. Per-row sigma resolution caches per-stage MAD bedGraphs and reads MAD at locus intervals (median MAD over `[start, end)` × 1.4826). Fallback chain: per-locus MAD → caller-supplied sigma overrides → peak_rcn-spread heuristic → module-level constants. Helpers `_load_locus_mad_sigma`, `_median_mad_at_locus`, `_median_mad_from_cached` mirror `timing.py:_median_mad_sigma`. Threaded through `aps.py:compute_and_write_aps` and `hmm_ported_analyses.py:run_step15_hmm_aps`. Stages C+D bundled because Stage C's signature change requires Stage D's call-site update to compile (workflow-permitted atomicity merge).

- **Stage E — Tests + KNOWN_ISSUES + closeout** (this commit). Two new regression tests in `tests/test_reliability.py`:
  - `test_flat_sample_consumes_mad_bedgraphs_when_available` — synthetic MAD bedGraphs with prescribed-narrow vs prescribed-wide spread cause the verdict to flip at peak_rcn=1.5 (tight MAD → confident growth → not flat; wide MAD → uncertain growth → still flat). Proves the MAD path is actually being read and used.
  - `test_flat_sample_falls_back_when_mad_dir_missing` — `mad_dir=None` and `mad_dir=str(does_not_exist)` produce identical verdicts, both equal to the v0.14.80 heuristic-only behavior. Proves the fallback chain is intact.

  Two KNOWN_ISSUES entries written for separately-actionable items the cycle surfaced (see "Surprises" below).

#### Behavioral changes at default invocation

- HMM `01-hmm/15-aps/` now contains `genome_stage_medians/` with four per-stage bedGraph files (RCN + log2RCN + MAD_RCN + MAD_log2RCN).
- HMM stage-to-stage ODW transition test (`timing.py`) transitions from heuristic-fallback sigmas to MAD-derived sigmas. Could shift `last_active_stage` / `amplicon_class` assignments on real HMM data in a biologically-more-faithful direction.
- HMM per-locus flat-sample test (`flat_sample.py`) similarly transitions to MAD-derived sigmas. Could change `flat_sample_flag` assignments at the (sample, locus) level on real HMM data.
- Posterior stage-1 anchoring inherits the new flat-sample verdicts.
- Growth and RMS pipelines: minor differences possible vs v0.14.80 because the per-locus flat-sample test now reads MAD per-locus instead of using a global heuristic sigma. Same MAD source the timing test was already using, so internal consistency improves.

#### Surprises surfaced during cycle (planning misses worth recording)

User-corrected definition: a "surprise" is something the planning routine should have anticipated but didn't. Two real planning misses, each captured as a KNOWN_ISSUES entry for follow-up:

- **`[ISSUE:2026-04-29:3]` — `--compute-aps` flag is more vestigial than its name suggests.** Code-dive verified that `--compute-aps` only suppresses the growth APS step at `onionskin.py:716`. RMS APS at `onionskin.py:3428` is gated on `n_non_ref >= 1 and manifest_path_str and os.path.exists(aps_loci_bed)`, NOT on `args.compute_aps`. HMM step15-aps at `hmm_engine.py:1581` is gated on `len(_hmm_non_ref_stages) >= 1`, NOT on `args.compute_aps`. So the flag does not actually gate cross-pipeline APS — only growth-pipeline APS. Proposed flag inversion to `--skip-aps` / `--skip-posterior` aligns the gate semantics across all pipelines uniformly.

- **`[ISSUE:2026-04-29:4]` — Posterior-regression tracking gap.** PuffStep parity validates HMM detection (steps 06-10) but NOT the timing/classification/flat-sample/posterior-anchoring layer downstream. `make twin`/`make full-twin` cover algorithmic correctness but not version-to-version stability. If a future cycle accidentally introduced a posterior-output regression, no test would catch it. Proposed posterior-vs-prior A/B regression test layer for any test that exercises posterior. Cross-references `[ISSUE:2026-04-29:3]` because the flag rename naturally surfaces the question.

These KNOWN_ISSUES entries don't block this cycle's close — they're follow-up work that this cycle's code-dive surfaced. Cycle 15.4a-S1 closes with a clean validation pass; the issues are queued for future cycles.

#### Validation

- `python -m pytest tests/test_reliability.py -q` — PASS (`10 passed, 1 skipped` after the +2 new MAD tests).
- `python -m pytest tests/test_reliability.py tests/test_aps.py tests/test_hmm_multistage_unification.py tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py tests/test_timing_odw.py tests/test_pipeline.py::test_help_runs tests/test_pipeline.py::test_help_step_mentions_per_group -q` — PASS (`19 passed, 1 skipped`).
- `make test` — PASS (`152 passed, 1 skipped in 56.76s`; +2 vs the v0.14.80 baseline of 150).
- `make toy` — PASS.
- `make twin` — PASS (perfect twin peak resolution).
- `make full-twin` — PASS (`Passed: 4, Failed: 0, Informational: 2`).
- `make puff-compare` — PASS (`OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`). **Critical gate confirming HMM detection (steps 06-10) is unchanged by the new MAD-aware behavior at step 15+.**

#### Closeout

Cycle 15.4a-S1 closes at **v0.14.81**. Cycle 15.4a remains CLOSED at v0.14.80 (the parent cycle's verdict is not retroactively modified). The CHANGELOG entry under `[v0.14.81]` documents the supplemental fix; the AUDIT_LOG entry above provides the round-by-round history. No SPEC priority status changes — SPEC15.5 + SPEC15.6 remain `DONE (v0.14.80)` with the cycle 15.4a-S1 follow-up recorded in the cycle 15.4a closeout subsection's "Known follow-up" paragraph (now historical, since this cycle resolves it). The next queued work is **cycle 15.5a Role 1 initial audit** (SPEC15.7 + SPEC15.8) per `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`.

## Cycle: 15.4a — HMM shape-filter + multistage unification + cross-pipeline reliability (SPEC15.5 + SPEC15.6) — CLOSED v0.14.80

### 2026-04-29 — Role 1 Re-Audit (closeout)
**Authors:** John M. Urban, GitHub Copilot (GPT-5.4 ; Reasoning: Xhigh)

**Verdict:** CLOSED at v0.14.80. All six Round 4 refinement findings (R3-F1 through R3-F6) verified clean against the live tree at HEAD `73c4bec` (Role 2 Stage Q validation/docs commit). The original cycle-15.4a core surfaces from the first R2 round remain intact under the refined contract. Archive-timing check PASS: no premature `git mv` of `PHASE15_SPEC.md`, `PHASE15_AUDIT_LOG.md`, `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`, or `PHASE15_FEEDBACK.md` into `multi-agent/plans/archived/` was detected; the live phase files remain at their active `multi-agent/plans/` paths.

#### Verification

**R3-F1 + R3-F3 (Bayesian per-locus flat test + new `flat_sample.py` module).** Verified in `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py` at the controlling code path: `per_locus_flat_test()` (lines 110-153), `compute_flat_sample_sidecar()` (155-275), `write_flat_sample_sidecar()` (277-301), and `count_flat_samples()` (321-324). The per-locus verdict uses the shared ODW detector path via `transition_is_active(...)` from `onionskin_core.odw`, with `previous_rcn=baseline_rcn=1.0` and `previous_log2=baseline_log2=0.0`, exactly matching the Round 4 baseline-anchor contract. Sample-level rollup consumes `min_flat_fraction` and records the per-run detector knobs in the sidecar schema (`sample_id`, `prior_stage`, `n_reliable_loci`, `n_flat_loci`, `flat_fraction`, `flat_sample_flag`, `detector`, `prob_threshold`, `fold_threshold`, `min_flat_fraction`). `aps.py:1373-1398` and `hmm_ported_analyses.py:179-203` both follow the required two-pass order: reliability sidecar first, then flat-sample sidecar against the reliable locus set, then `populate_flat_flag_from_ids()` backfill into the reliability sidecar. CLEAN.

**R3-F2 (`--aps-preamp-threshold` retirement + new flat-sample flag set).** Verified absent from live argparse and runtime code paths. Grep over `onionskin.py`, `onionskin_core/**/*.py`, and `tests/**/*.py` found no live consumer references beyond explanatory text asserting retirement. The new argparse surfaces are present in `onionskin.py`: `--flat-sample-prob-threshold` (1757), `--flat-sample-fold-threshold` (1769), `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction` (1781), and `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` (1794) in the dedicated `Flat-sample detection` group. Growth, RMS, and HMM APS call sites all thread the three new flat-sample knobs plus `keep_amplicons_bed` into their APS steps (`onionskin.py:736-757`, `3438-3461`, `3632-3651`; `hmm_engine.py:1598-1601`). CLEAN.

**R3-F4 (posterior stage-1 anchoring).** Verified in `onionskin_core/posterior.py:23-188`. `build_posterior_manifest(..., flat_sample_ids=...)` computes `stage_offset = 2 if have_flats else 1`, shifts cluster-derived stages to `2..k+1` when any flat sample is present, re-inserts flat samples at `posterior_stage=1` with `cluster_raw=NaN`, and writes `anchor_pinned` into the cluster-map sidecar. `onionskin.py:_read_flat_sample_ids_from_sidecar()` (145-168) feeds this contract into both posterior flows: `_run_posterior_rms()` (4039-4047) and `_run_posterior()` (4145-4153). Regression tests `tests/test_reliability.py::test_posterior_anchor_pins_flat_samples_to_stage_1` and `::test_posterior_no_flats_keeps_legacy_stage_numbering` cover both anchor and legacy paths. CLEAN.

**R3-F5 (auto-switch-to-chrom-median + override flag).** Verified in `onionskin.py:_check_flat_sample_auto_switch()` (92-143). The helper reads `flat_samples.tsv`, prints the flat-sample count to stderr, sets `args._flat_sample_auto_switch = True` when zero flat samples are detected and `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` is not set, and emits distinct warning text for the override and auto-switch cases. The helper is invoked immediately after APS output generation in all three pipeline flows: growth (`onionskin.py:760`), RMS (`onionskin.py:3462`), and HMM (`onionskin.py:3695`). Posterior consumption is implemented in `_run_posterior()` via in-place norm-mode mutation for `norm_mode`, `growth_norm_mode`, `rms_norm_mode`, and `hmm_norm_mode` when the auto-switch flag is set (`onionskin.py:4104-4118`). Tests `tests/test_reliability.py::test_flat_sample_auto_switch_zero_flats_triggers_chrom_median` and `::test_flat_sample_auto_switch_with_flats_present` verify trigger / no-op / override behavior. CLEAN.

**R3-F6 (`--known-reliable-amplicons` synonym).** Verified at `onionskin.py:1727-1741`: `aps.add_argument("--keep-amplicons-bed", "--known-reliable-amplicons", dest="keep_amplicons_bed", ...)` accepts both spellings and routes them to the same destination. Downstream consumers only read `keep_amplicons_bed`, so both CLI forms are functionally identical. `reliability.py:_overlaps_any()` and `filter_contributions_for_reliability()` continue to honor `keep_override` without schema divergence. HELP regression at `tests/test_pipeline.py:205-213` asserts the synonym is present and the retired flag is absent. CLEAN.

**Original 15.4a core surfaces (SPEC15.5 + pre-refinement SPEC15.6) remain intact.** Re-spot-checked the decision-making surfaces most exposed to the follow-up wiring: `onionskin_core/hmm_multistage_unification.py` still owns the HMM shape-score sink and 4-mode multistage unification contract; `onionskin_core/hmm_fork_travel.py` still emits `12-fork-travel/width_progression_metrics.tsv`; `onionskin_core/reliability.py` still enforces the class-aware reliability thresholds and keep-override semantics; `onionskin.py` still exposes `--multistage-unification-mode` with default `either-suffices`. The accepted Stage-F step-12 placement deviation remains sound, and the Round 4 follow-up does not regress the earlier R3-round-1 clean verdict. CLEAN.

#### Implementer praise

1. **Bundling Stages M+N was the right atomicity call.** The new flag surface, new module ownership, and APS/HMM call-site signature changes genuinely do not compile cleanly as separable concepts. Keeping them together preserved bisectable, working intermediate states.
2. **The posterior anchoring wire-up was cleanly minimal.** Extending `build_posterior_manifest()` with `flat_sample_ids` and the explicit `anchor_pinned` audit column solved the biological requirement without duplicating clustering machinery per pipeline.
3. **The toy-posterior test relaxation was correctly reasoned, not a dodge.** Accepting contiguous `[1, 2]` or `[1, 2, 3]` stage labels reflects the new biology-aware anchor contract under default thresholds rather than weakening coverage arbitrarily.

#### Validation gates re-run by Role 1

- `make test` — PASS (`150 passed, 1 skipped in 46.62s`).
- `make toy` — PASS.
- `make twin` — PASS (perfect twin-peak resolution on both datasets).
- `make full-twin` — PASS (`Passed: 4`, `Failed: 0`, `Informational: 2`).
- `make puff-compare` — PASS (`OVERALL: PASS (28 files match)`).

These five required targets were re-run on the current tree after the R2 follow-up and satisfy the full validation parity requirement for cycle closeout.

#### Known follow-up (R3-O4 — sigma derivation in `flat_sample.py`)

Surfaced post-closeout-audit by orchestrator review (Claude Code 2.1.116, claude-opus-4-7 — same agent that emitted the R3 round 1 audit + R2 follow-up implementation): the Bayesian per-locus flat-sample test in `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py:_resolve_default_sigma` derives RCN/log2 sigmas from a peak_rcn-spread heuristic vs the chrom-median baseline rather than from per-stage MAD bedGraphs. SPEC15.4 / cycle 15.3a explicitly plumbed `stageN.MAD_RCN.bedGraph` and `stageN.MAD_log2RCN.bedGraph` through `onionskin_core/timing.py:_median_mad_sigma` for the stage-to-stage ODW transition test; the new per-locus flat-sample test reuses the same `transition_probability` primitive but does not yet consume that MAD source.

This was originally framed in the R2 follow-up implementation report below as observation O4 ("`flat_sample.py` sigma derivation uses peak_rcn-spread fallback rather than per-locus per-sample MAD bedGraphs. Acceptable conservative default; future refinement candidate."). The R3 round 2 audit above (Copilot GPT-5.4 Xhigh) verified all six R3-F1..R3-F6 findings clean against the refined contract but did not surface O4 as a follow-up requiring action. Post-audit orchestrator review judged the framing too soft: the locked SPEC15.6 Round 4 phrasing ("Bayesian-ODW-consistent per-locus test") implies consistent variance treatment, not just consistent threshold defaults, and the stage-to-stage path now consumes MAD-derived sigmas while the per-locus path runs with module-level constants (`_DEFAULT_RCN_SIGMA = 0.1`, `_DEFAULT_LOG2_SIGMA = 0.15`) plus a per-call peak_rcn-spread estimate. This is a real statistical-fidelity gap inside the same Bayesian framework, even if cycle 15.4a's contract did not lock the sigma source explicitly.

User authorized addressing it as a small post-closeout follow-up cycle (cycle 15.4a-S1 or equivalent — orchestrator picks the slot) rather than re-opening cycle 15.4a. Cycle 15.4a stays CLOSED at v0.14.80 with this known-follow-up note recorded for traceability; the follow-up cycle wires per-stage MAD bedGraphs into `flat_sample.py` so the per-locus Bayesian test consumes the same MAD source as the stage-to-stage ODW test. The DS1 chr II calibration regression test currently skips when the calibration fixture is absent, so the locked acceptance criterion ("the 4 calibration amplicons must come out weight ≈ 1.0") is not yet biologically gated against the heuristic sigma either way; the follow-up cycle will be the right window to commit a fixture if the user has it ready.

The CHANGELOG `[v0.14.80]` entry was extended with a matching "Known follow-up" paragraph at orchestrator-edit time (post-Copilot-audit, pre-commit) so the closeout entry honestly surfaces the gap.

#### Closeout

Cycle 15.4a closes at **v0.14.80**. The `PHASE15_SPEC.md` priority status table now marks **SPEC15.5** and **SPEC15.6** as `DONE (v0.14.80)`. `CHANGELOG.md` now carries the cycle-closeout entry under `[v0.14.80]` with consolidated authorship across the full cycle plus the orchestrator-edit "Known follow-up" paragraph. The next queued work is the **R3-O4 follow-up cycle** (small; wires MAD-derived sigmas into `flat_sample.py`), then **cycle 15.5a Role 1 initial audit** (SPEC15.7 + SPEC15.8) per `PHASE15_STRATEGY.md`.

### 2026-04-29 — Role 2 R2-follow-up Implementation — Stages M–Q
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.116 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max)

**Verdict:** IMPLEMENTED; **R3 round 2 re-audit needed** per the explicit ruling at R3 round 1. No CHANGELOG written this round (per cycle instruction; CHANGELOG batches at R3 round 2 closeout). All six R3 findings (R3-F1 → R3-F6) are addressed in the live tree below.

#### Implementation summary by stage

- **Stage M+N (bundled, commit `7ceebf3`) — `flat_sample.py` module + flag retirement + new flag set.** Created `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py` (321 lines) with the Bayesian-ODW per-locus flat-sample test reusing `odw.transition_probability` with `previous_rcn=chrom_median=1.0` and `previous_log2=0.0` baseline anchors (matches the SPEC15.4 stage-1-vs-background path at `timing.py:504-519`). Public surface: `per_locus_flat_test`, `compute_flat_sample_sidecar`, `write_flat_sample_sidecar`, `flat_sample_ids`, `count_flat_samples`. The new sidecar schema is `(sample_id, prior_stage, n_reliable_loci, n_flat_loci, flat_fraction, flat_sample_flag, detector, prob_threshold, fold_threshold, min_flat_fraction)` — every row records the knobs used so re-runs are reproducible. Sigma defaults: derived per-call from `peak_rcn` MAD-style spread vs baseline; falls back to module-level `_DEFAULT_RCN_SIGMA=0.1` / `_DEFAULT_LOG2_SIGMA=0.15` on small/missing data; matches conservative ODW fallbacks from `aps.py:946-955`. Retired `--aps-preamp-threshold` + `auto` mode entirely from `onionskin.py:argparse` and threading at call-site lines 660 + 3485 + `aps.py:1157, 1368` + `reliability.py:122, 225` + `hmm_ported_analyses.py:128` + `hmm_engine.py:1256, 1593`. Added five new flags: `--flat-sample-prob-threshold` (default inherits `--odw-active-prob-threshold`), `--flat-sample-fold-threshold` (default inherits `--odw-active-fold-threshold`), `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction` (default 1.0), `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` (default off), `--known-reliable-amplicons` (synonym for `--keep-amplicons-bed` — both spellings target the same dest). New "Flat-sample detection" argparse group housing the four `--flat-sample-*`/`--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` flags with Universal-in-spirit framing referencing SPEC15.6 Round 4. Updated `reliability.py` to drop the old `write_flat_sample_sidecar` (used APS-area threshold), drop `_flat_sample_ids` + `_parse_threshold` helpers, and add `populate_flat_flag_from_ids` backfill helper that mirrors the original "all observers flat" semantics for the per-call_id `flat_sample_flag` column (driven by the new Bayesian sidecar instead of an APS-area gate). Two-pass call-site ordering at `aps.py:1358+` and `hmm_ported_analyses.py:148+`: reliability_df first (no flat info) → flat_df via the new module (consumes reliability_df to filter to reliable loci) → backfill reliability_df.flat_sample_flag → write reliability_df. Tests migrated: `test_reliability.py` `test_flat_sample_sidecar_numeric_threshold` retired and replaced by `test_flat_sample_sidecar_bayesian_per_locus` + `test_flat_sample_min_flat_fraction_relaxation`; `test_pipeline.py::test_help_step_mentions_per_group` extended to assert presence of all five new flags + the `--known-reliable-amplicons` synonym + absence of `--aps-preamp-threshold`. Stages M and N were bundled into a single commit because the call-site signature changes can't compile cleanly without the new flag wiring landing together (workflow line 1245-1258 explicitly allows this where work is genuinely atomic).

- **Stage O (commit `1842088`) — auto-switch-to-chrom-median controller logic.** Two new helpers in `onionskin.py`: `_check_flat_sample_auto_switch(args, aps_dir, pipeline_name)` reads the flat-sample sidecar after each pipeline's APS run, prints the flat count to stderr, and -- when 0 flats detected AND `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` is NOT set -- sets `args._flat_sample_auto_switch = True`. `_resolve_posterior_norm_mode(args, requested_norm_mode)` is a small consumer that maps `"ref-stage" → "chrom-median"` when the auto-switch is active (kept as a separate symbol so future call-sites can adopt it without re-implementing the logic). Wired into three APS call sites: growth Phase-2 post-processing (`onionskin.py:735`), RMS APS (`onionskin.py:3439` after the `compute_and_write_aps` call), and HMM step15 (end of `_run_hmm_engine` at the new auto-switch try/except guard). `_run_posterior` consults the flag and overrides any norm-mode attribute resolving to ref-stage (`norm_mode`, `growth_norm_mode`, `rms_norm_mode`, `hmm_norm_mode`) to chrom-median, with explicit stderr reporting per attribute switched. Two new tests cover the helper: `test_flat_sample_auto_switch_zero_flats_triggers_chrom_median` (zero flats triggers; `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` opts out) and `test_flat_sample_auto_switch_with_flats_present` (≥1 flat is a no-op). Closes R3-F5 in full.

- **Stage P (commit `ceaf96c`) — cross-pipeline posterior stage-1 anchoring.** Extended `onionskin_core/posterior.py:build_posterior_manifest` with `flat_sample_ids: Optional[Iterable[str]] = None` parameter. Inside the builder: when any flat sample is present in the prior APS order table, cluster-derived stages shift from `1..k → 2..k+1` (`stage_offset = 2 if have_flats else 1`), and flat samples are added back to the merged DataFrame at `posterior_stage=1` with `cluster_raw=NaN` and `anchor_pinned=True`. The `anchor_pinned` boolean is added unconditionally (False for cluster-derived rows) and propagates through `posterior_manifest_cluster_map.tsv` for auditing. Hires manifests inherit the same permutation through the existing `sid_to_post_stage` mapping unchanged. New helper `_read_flat_sample_ids_from_sidecar(aps_dir)` in `onionskin.py` reads the prior APS flat sidecar; threaded into both `_run_posterior` (line 4118) and `_run_posterior_rms` (line 4015) call sites. Closes IBM-2026-04-14:1's "Stage 1 of the posterior contains only pre-amplification samples" exit condition for Growth + RMS via the shared cluster-map machinery; HMM follows the same posterior manifest. Two new regression tests: `test_posterior_anchor_pins_flat_samples_to_stage_1` (verifies anchor + 2..k+1 shift + cluster_map flag) and `test_posterior_no_flats_keeps_legacy_stage_numbering` (verifies legacy 1..k path unchanged when `flat_sample_ids=None`).

- **Stage Q — tests + validation + report.** Migrated tests landed alongside Stages M+N, O, and P (5 new tests total: `test_flat_sample_sidecar_bayesian_per_locus`, `test_flat_sample_min_flat_fraction_relaxation`, `test_flat_sample_auto_switch_zero_flats_triggers_chrom_median`, `test_flat_sample_auto_switch_with_flats_present`, `test_posterior_anchor_pins_flat_samples_to_stage_1`, `test_posterior_no_flats_keeps_legacy_stage_numbering`). One pre-existing test pair (`test_posterior_rcn_mean_shift_output_exists`, `test_posterior_rcn_ms_standalone`) updated to reflect the new biological correctness — the toy `--aps-cluster-k 2` path now produces either `[1, 2]` (no flats detected) OR `[1, 2, 3]` (≥1 flat sample anchored at stage 1, clusters at 2 + 3). Either is correct under the SPEC15.6 Round 4 contract; assertions relaxed to "stages start at 1 and are contiguous; length in {2, 3}". This commit also updates HANDOFF.md + TASK.md to reflect the new state per the workflow Template B requirement.

#### In-situ catches, divergences, and observations

- **Bundled commit M+N is a deliberate deviation** from the audit's stage-per-commit suggestion. Documented inline in the commit message; the work is genuinely atomic (call-site signatures change in lockstep with the new flag set, so splitting would leave the codebase non-compiling between commits). Workflow rule "One concept per commit" allows this where the conceptual unit can't be split without breaking the build.
- **Stage F's audit suggestion to extend the posterior cluster_map header was preemptively fulfilled** by Codex's original Stage P design — the existing `_cluster_map.tsv` schema accommodates the new `anchor_pinned` column without any breaking schema change.
- **Test expectation update for `test_posterior_rcn_mean_shift_output_exists` + `test_posterior_rcn_ms_standalone`** is a behavioral-change-fallout from the new Bayesian per-locus detector firing on the toy dataset. With `--aps-cluster-k 2` and the toy-data peak_rcn distributions, the detector legitimately flags 1+ stage-1 samples as flat under default thresholds (prob 0.9 / fold 1.25). The test assertion was tightened to "contiguous from 1; length in {2, 3}" to accept both biologically-defensible outcomes. This is NOT a false positive — the toy stage-1 samples have low peak_rcn at amplicon loci by design (they represent earlier developmental stages), which is exactly what the SPEC15.6 Round 4 detector is designed to identify.
- **Sigma-derivation pragma in `flat_sample.py:_resolve_default_sigma`** uses a peak_rcn MAD-style spread vs baseline rather than fetching per-locus per-sample MAD bedGraphs. This matches the conservative pattern in `reliability.py` (which also doesn't reach into MAD bedGraphs) and keeps the module self-contained. Per-locus MAD-aware sigma is a future refinement worth tracking; flagged as observation R3-O4 below for the closeout audit's consideration.
- **`flat_sample.py:per_locus_flat_test`** treats `peak_rcn=NaN` (i.e., locus has no measurement in this sample) as "flat at this locus" — a sample with no signal at a reliable amplicon is the canonical pre-amplification case. Tested implicitly via the relaxation test.
- **Architectural call (intentional):** `populate_flat_flag_from_ids` in `reliability.py` keeps the per-call_id `flat_sample_flag` column populated from the per-sample sidecar via "every observing sample is flat" → True. This preserves the original semantics for any downstream consumer that reads the reliability sidecar's flat column. The authoritative per-(sample, prior_stage) verdict lives in `flat_samples.tsv`.

#### Behavioral changes to call out at cycle closeout

- HMM/Growth/RMS APS now run a Bayesian per-locus flat-sample test by default (consuming `--odw-active-prob-threshold` + `--odw-active-fold-threshold` defaults). Replaces the legacy APS-area-gate "auto"-mode no-op.
- `flat_samples.tsv` schema changed: now per-(sample, prior_stage) with explicit `n_reliable_loci`, `n_flat_loci`, `flat_fraction` columns; legacy `aps_preamp_threshold` + `APS_area` columns removed.
- `--aps-preamp-threshold` flag removed entirely. Five new flags replace it: `--flat-sample-prob-threshold`, `--flat-sample-fold-threshold`, `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction`, `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage`, `--known-reliable-amplicons` (synonym for `--keep-amplicons-bed`).
- Posterior pipeline now anchors flat samples to `posterior_stage=1`; cluster-derived stages shift to `2..k+1` when any flats are present. New `anchor_pinned` column in `posterior_manifest_cluster_map.tsv`.
- When 0 flat samples are detected at any APS run, the controller auto-switches any `ref-stage` posterior norm-mode to `chrom-median`. `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` opts out. Loud stderr warnings in both the auto-switch firing and override paths.
- Pre-existing test pair `test_posterior_rcn_mean_shift_output_exists` + `test_posterior_rcn_ms_standalone` now accept `[1, 2]` OR `[1, 2, 3]` for the toy posterior (both biologically correct under the new contract).

#### R3 observations recorded for closeout consideration

- **R3-O1 (carried from R3 round 1):** `reliability.py` BIC-evidence neutrality when a pipeline's APS contribution lacks a BIC-like column — UNCHANGED in this round. Tracked for cycle 15.6a.
- **R3-O2 (carried):** DS1 chr II calibration fixture not yet committed — the calibration regression test still skips gracefully. UNCHANGED.
- **R3-O3 (carried):** `--aps-preamp-threshold` migration consequences — handled in Stage M+N. CLOSED.
- **R3-O4 (new):** `flat_sample.py` sigma derivation uses peak_rcn-spread fallback rather than per-locus per-sample MAD bedGraphs. Acceptable conservative default; future refinement candidate. Tracked as a follow-up — could land alongside `--flat-sample-mad-source` flag in a closeout cycle if user wants higher-fidelity sigmas.

#### Validation

- `python -m compileall onionskin_core onionskin.py` — implicit via test imports; no syntax errors.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_reliability.py -q` — PASS (`8 passed, 1 skipped` with the new tests).
- `python -m pytest tests/test_aps.py tests/test_hmm_multistage_unification.py tests/test_hmm_ported_analyses.py tests/test_pipeline.py::test_help_step_mentions_per_group tests/test_pipeline.py::test_help_runs tests/test_timing_odw.py -q` — PASS (17 passed, 1 skipped).
- `python -m pytest tests/test_pipeline.py -q` — PASS (`106 passed in 210.69s` after the two posterior-test expectation updates).
- Targeted re-run of the two updated posterior tests: `python -m pytest tests/test_pipeline.py::test_posterior_rcn_mean_shift_output_exists tests/test_pipeline.py::test_posterior_rcn_ms_standalone -q` — PASS (2 passed in 32s) on the post-update tree.

`make test` / `make toy` / `make twin` / `make full-twin` / `make puff-compare` (gold-standard parity): not re-run in this round because (a) the prior R2 round (commits a905388 → 3c721de) already validated those at the live tree's HEAD `3c721de`, and (b) this round's work touches only post-APS reliability/flat-sample/posterior-anchor surfaces — `make puff-compare` parity is gated on HMM-state-path-emission code which this round does not touch. R3 round 2 should re-run those for full confidence at closeout.

**Round declaration:** R3 round 2 re-audit needed.

### 2026-04-29 — Role 1 Re-Audit (R3, round 1) — OPEN (Round 4 gaps)
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.116 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max)

**Verdict:** **OPEN.** The original-contract R2 implementation (commits `a905388` → `3c721de`) is substantially clean against the SPEC15.5 + original-SPEC15.6 deliverables — Stages A–L verified with three minor caveats noted below, none of them blockers. However, the six **Round 4 SPEC15.6 design refinements** locked by the orchestrator round (commit `e783f50`, post-R2 / pre-R3) are unimplemented in the live code — as expected per the user's explicit confirmation that Codex finished R2 BEFORE the Round 4 adjustment round. R3 declares OPEN and emits new repair instructions (Stage M–Q) for an R2 follow-up round that brings live code into alignment with the refined contract.

Audited at HEAD `e783f50` (Phase 15 cycle 15.4a Orchestrator round / SPEC15.6 Round 4 refinements commit, on `main`). Working tree clean.

#### Verification of original R2 implementation (Stages A–L vs original SPEC contract)

**F1+F2 (SPEC15.5 a + b — HMM shape-filter sink + cross-stage shape meta-analysis port).** Verified in `onionskin_core/hmm_multistage_unification.py` (621 lines, commit `a905388`). Module exposes shape-score computation via `_shape_score_for_row` (line 181), `_mean_shape_profile` (line 157), and `_load_stage_profiles` (line 113) consuming step-4 HMM stage-median bedGraphs through `_iter_stage4_median_files` (line 97). Reuses the shared `dBIC_flat_vs_tri` primitive per audit instruction Stage A. The two HMM shape-score CLI flags at `onionskin.py:2084-2110` are now behavioral — placeholder language verified absent (see commit `a905388` diff). CLEAN.

**F3 (SPEC15.5 c — HMM-specific state-path growth credibility / ghost levels).** Verified in `hmm_multistage_unification.py:357-396` (`_state_path_checks` function). Aggregates `state_path_persistence_after_popup`, `state_path_pyramid_shaped`, `state_path_width_grows`, `state_path_more_than_one_step`, `level_growth`, `fork_growth` into `state_path_growth_credibility_score`. Consumes Bayesian ODW active-stage windows via `_active_stage_window` (line 249), NOT a contiguous-monotonic-growth check. CLEAN per audit instruction Stage C.

- Check 3 (multi-step state-path evolution) — `_trajectory_stage_nlevels` line 204 + `more_than_one_step` aggregation.

All four operate across the full ODW window via `_active_stage_window` (line 249), per SPEC line 411-415 within-ODW non-monotonicity rule. CLEAN.

**F6 (SPEC15.5 f — HMM fork/width progression outputs).** Codex deviated from audit Stage F's "new HMM step `<step>-progression/`" suggestion: instead emits `12-fork-travel/width_progression_metrics.tsv` (`hmm_fork_travel.py:473-541`, commit `822697b`) keeping the new output adjacent to its existing source rows. **Deviation accepted at re-audit** — Codex's reasoning (avoid unnecessary step renumber when the source data already lives in step-12) is sound; the per-amplicon TSV product requested by SPEC15.5(f) is delivered with `leading_left_bp`, `leading_right_bp`, `state_path_width_bp` columns. The audit's preference for a separate step was a layout-organization choice, not a contractual requirement. SPEC line 421 explicitly says "step number TBD post-SPEC15.3 renumbering" so step-12 location is in-spec. **Praise:** clean architectural call to consume the same source rows once.

Module-level docstring at lines 1-7 explicitly honors deliverable 10 ("does not use low absolute RCN as a penalty. A weak but consistently increasing amplicon can therefore remain high reliability"). RELIABILITY_COLUMNS schema at lines 19-33 includes `reliability_score`, `reliability_flag`, the 5 per-axis sub-scores (`rcn_increase_score`, `width_increase_score`, `area_increase_score`, `bic_increase_score`, `parabola_height_increase_score`), `flat_sample_flag`, `keep_override`. HMM-only extra columns confined to `hmm_multistage_unification.py` outputs per audit instruction Stage K — does not pollute the shared `amplicon_class` schema. CLEAN.

The test exists at `tests/test_reliability.py`. #### Round 4 SPEC15.6 refinement gaps (R3 findings — repair contract for R2 follow-up)

These six gaps reflect the orchestrator-round design refinements that locked AFTER R2 finished. R2 follow-up implementation (Stages M–Q below) brings the live code into alignment with the refined contract from `DECISIONS.md [2026-04-29] Phase 15 cycle 15.4a — SPEC15.6 Round 4 design refinements`.

**R3-F1. Per-locus flat-sample test mechanism is APS-area-threshold-based, NOT Bayesian-ODW per-locus.** Live code at `reliability.py:222-247 write_flat_sample_sidecar` uses `flat_sample_flag = bool(threshold is not None and aps_area <= threshold)`. Refined contract (SPEC15.6 deliverable 3, post-Round-4): per-locus "is this sample showing growth at this locus?" reuses `onionskin_core/odw.py:transition_probability` with `previous_rcn = chrom_median = 1.0` and `previous_log2 = 0.0`. Required in R2 follow-up: replace the APS-area test with the Bayesian ODW per-locus test; add three new flags (`--flat-sample-prob-threshold` / `--flat-sample-fold-threshold` defaults inheriting from `--odw-active-{prob,fold}-threshold`; `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction` default 1.0). Sample-level rollup: sample is flat iff it tests flat at ≥ `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction` of reliable amplicon loci.

**R3-F2. `--aps-preamp-threshold` flag still present; should be removed.** Live code at `onionskin.py:1632` + threading at `:660` + `:3485` + `aps.py:1157` + `reliability.py:122, 225` + `hmm_ported_analyses.py:128`. Refined contract (SPEC15.6 deliverable 4, post-Round-4): RETIRED. Required in R2 follow-up: remove the flag from argparse; remove all consuming references; tests that exercise it must migrate to the new `--flat-sample-*` flags or be retired.

**R3-F3. `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py` module does not exist.** Refined contract (SPEC15.6 implementation notes, post-Round-4): flat-sample logic relocates to a new sister module to `reliability.py`, reflecting the multistage-unification + reliability-consumer ownership architecture. Required in R2 follow-up: create `flat_sample.py`; move `write_flat_sample_sidecar` (current home: `reliability.py:222-247`) and the new Bayesian ODW per-locus test into it; update imports.

**R3-F4. Posterior stage-1 anchoring not implemented.** Live code: zero hits for `anchor_flat`, `stage_1_anchor`, `posterior_anchor`, or equivalent. `aps.py:_filter_flat_samples_from_matrix` only DROPS flat samples from the matrix (line 1395-1408 logic) — it does not ASSIGN them to a stage-1 anchor for downstream posterior consumption. Refined contract (SPEC15.6 deliverable 6, post-Round-4): each pipeline's posterior/clustering surface anchors flat-flagged samples to its stage-1 / pre-amp slot BEFORE clustering remaining samples into k-1 stages; cluster-map sidecar records anchor-pinned vs clustered. Required in R2 follow-up: add anchoring logic in APS clustering machinery (used by Growth + RMS) + HMM posterior/stage-assignment surface. Cross-pipeline contract: identical anchoring semantics; per-pipeline implementation. Closes IBM-2026-04-14:1's exit condition.

**R3-F5. Auto-switch-to-chrom-median + `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` override not implemented.** Live code: zero hits for `allow_nonflat`, `nonflat_ref_stage`, or equivalent. Refined contract (SPEC15.6 deliverable 3 final paragraph, post-Round-4): pipeline prints flat-sample count to stderr; if 0 flats, emits loud warning AND auto-switches any posterior analyses that would otherwise use ref-stage normalization to chrom-median. `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` (default off) opts out. Required in R2 follow-up: controller logic in `onionskin.py` reads flat-sample sidecar after detection runs, counts flats, decides auto-switch; new flag in argparse adjacent to flat-sample group.

**R3-F6. `--known-reliable-amplicons` synonym not present.** Live code: only `--keep-amplicons-bed` exists at `onionskin.py:1643`. Refined contract (SPEC15.6 deliverable 9, post-Round-4): both flag spellings accepted as aliases pointing to the same underlying argparse dest. Required in R2 follow-up: add `--known-reliable-amplicons` as a synonym (argparse supports this via multiple option strings on the same `add_argument` call OR via two separate add_argument calls with shared dest).

#### Implementation instructions for R2 follow-up

Suggested 5-stage commit split (Role 2's call to merge stages where work is genuinely atomic):

- **Stage M — `flat_sample.py` module + Bayesian ODW per-locus test.** Create `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py`; move `write_flat_sample_sidecar` from `reliability.py:222-247` (and update `reliability.py` imports + exports). Replace the APS-area test inside it with the Bayesian ODW per-locus test consuming `onionskin_core/odw.py:transition_probability`. New per-locus and rollup logic. Keep the `flat_samples.tsv` schema-identical insofar as possible to preserve cross-pipeline-sidecar consumers; new columns may be added (per-locus flat verdicts, per-sample flat fraction). Implements R3-F1 + R3-F3.

- **Stage N — New CLI flags + `--aps-preamp-threshold` retirement.** Add `--flat-sample-prob-threshold` (default: inherit `--odw-active-prob-threshold`), `--flat-sample-fold-threshold` (default: inherit `--odw-active-fold-threshold`), `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction` (default 1.0), `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` (default off), `--known-reliable-amplicons` (synonym for `--keep-amplicons-bed`). Remove `--aps-preamp-threshold` from `onionskin.py:1632` + all consuming references at `onionskin.py:660, 3485`, `aps.py:1157, 1368`, `reliability.py:122, 225`, `hmm_ported_analyses.py:128`. Update tests that referenced `--aps-preamp-threshold` to use the new flags. Implements R3-F2 + R3-F5 (flag) + R3-F6.

- **Stage O — Auto-switch-to-chrom-median controller logic.** In `onionskin.py` controller, after flat-sample detection runs, read the flat-sample sidecar, count flats, emit stderr message ("Detected N flat samples"). If N == 0: emit loud warning + auto-switch any pipeline whose `--*-norm-mode` resolves to ref-stage AND whose posterior analysis is gated downstream of flat-sample detection. `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` overrides the auto-switch. Implements R3-F5 (logic).

- **Stage P — Cross-pipeline posterior stage-1 anchoring.** Extend `aps.py:_filter_flat_samples_from_matrix` (or add a sibling function) to ANCHOR flat-flagged samples to a stage-1 slot in the cluster map BEFORE the remaining samples cluster into k-1 stages. Apply via `aps.py:1133+ compute_and_write_aps` so Growth + RMS inherit. HMM via `engines/hmm_engine.py` posterior/stage-assignment surface — Role 2 picks the exact wire-up location (likely the HMM clustering step). Cluster-map sidecar records anchor-pinned vs algorithmically-clustered samples. Cross-pipeline schema-identical anchor flag. Implements R3-F4.

- **Stage Q — Tests + validation + audit-log implementation report.** New tests: per-locus Bayesian flat test (use synthetic data); flag behavior for the 5 new flags + the retired flag; auto-switch-to-chrom-median behavior with N=0 flats and N>=1 flats; posterior anchoring places flat-flagged samples in stage 1. Run `make test`, `make toy`, `make twin`, `make full-twin`, `make puff-compare` (parity must remain unchanged from `e783f50` baseline). Append implementation report to AUDIT_LOG cycle 15.4a section under the existing "2026-04-29 — Role 2 Implementation" subsection (newest-first: this round's report goes ABOVE the existing report) OR as a new subsection if Role 2 prefers, dated 2026-04-29 or later — whatever date the implementation actually fires.

**Test coverage required for R3 closeout:**
- Synthetic per-locus Bayesian flat test (≥1 flat sample, ≥1 amplified sample, expected verdicts).
- `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction` boundary test (default 1.0 vs relaxed values).
- Auto-switch test: 0 flats + ref-stage default → chrom-median switched.
- Auto-switch override test: 0 flats + `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` → ref-stage retained.
- Posterior anchoring: flat samples placed in cluster-map stage 1 deterministically.
- Synonym test: `--known-reliable-amplicons` and `--keep-amplicons-bed` produce identical outputs.
- Existing `--aps-preamp-threshold` tests: retired or migrated.
- Existing `make puff-compare` parity: unchanged from baseline (no HMM-state-path-emission regressions).

#### R3 observations (non-blocking; tracking)

- **O1 — BIC-evidence neutrality across pipelines.** `reliability.py` returns a neutral score for the BIC axis when a pipeline's APS contribution table doesn't carry a BIC-like column. Acceptable per Codex's implementation report; tracked for cycle 15.6a (SPEC15.9–15.12 APS analytical work) where APS schema can be uniformly extended. Record in `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` if not already.
- **O2 — DS1 chr II calibration fixture not yet committed.** The calibration regression test skips gracefully. The locked acceptance criterion (4 DS1 chr II loci weight ≈ 1.0) cannot be biologically gated until the fixture lands. Tracking-file follow-up; can be a closeout-cycle task or an explicit fixture-commit cycle.
- **O3 — Round 4 retirement of original deliverable 4 means tests that exercise the retired flag must be migrated, not just removed.** Stage N must update `tests/test_reliability.py` and `tests/test_pipeline.py` references; do not lose test coverage when migrating. Verified test files exist; specific assertions to migrate are R2 follow-up's call.

#### Skip-reaudit eligibility

R2 follow-up is **NOT skip-reaudit eligible**. The Round 4 refinements introduce new statistical machinery (Bayesian per-locus test), new controller logic (auto-switch + posterior anchoring), and new CLI surfaces. R3 must verify the live code against the refined contract; the work is too algorithmic + cross-pipeline for skip-reaudit safety.

#### Round declaration

R3 OPEN — R2 follow-up implementation needed for Stages M–Q. After R2 follow-up closes, R3 will re-fire to verify the Round 4 contract is met, then write the cycle-closeout CHANGELOG entry with consolidated authorship.

---

### 2026-04-29 — Orchestrator round (post-R2, pre-R3): SPEC15.6 Round 4 design refinements
**Authors:** John M. Urban (decisions); Claude Code 2.1.116 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max) (transcription).

**Trigger:** R2 implementation closed at commit `3c721de` (Stage L) per the implementation report below. Between R2 close and R3 re-audit, user surfaced four sharp design questions about SPEC15.6 deliverables 3 + 4 + 6 + 7 + 9 that produced six locked design refinements. SPEC15.6 + SPEC15.13 + STRATEGY + DECISIONS were updated before R3 fires so the re-audit verifies live code against the **refined** contract, not the original.

**Round 4 SPEC15.6 design refinements (all locked; full rationale in `DECISIONS.md [2026-04-29] Phase 15 cycle 15.4a — SPEC15.6 Round 4 design refinements`):**

1. **Bayesian-ODW-consistent per-locus flat test (SPEC15.6 deliverable 3 reworked).** Per-locus "is this sample showing growth at this locus?" uses the SPEC15.4 Bayesian ODW transition primitive with `previous_rcn = chrom_median = 1.0` and `previous_log2 = 0.0`. Defaults inherit from `--odw-active-prob-threshold` (0.9) and `--odw-active-fold-threshold` (1.25). NEW per-locus override flags `--flat-sample-prob-threshold` and `--flat-sample-fold-threshold`. NEW sample-level rollup flag `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction` (default 1.0). The hard-threshold "Summit RCN < 1.5×" form from BRAINSTORM 2026-04-19 is RETIRED.
2. **`--aps-preamp-threshold` retired (SPEC15.6 deliverable 4 reworked).** The flag (with `auto` gap-detection mode) is dropped from SPEC scope. Flat-sample logic relocates to a new `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py` module (sister to `reliability.py`).
3. **Auto-switch-to-chrom-median when zero flats detected, with `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` override.** Pipeline prints flat count to stderr; if 0, warns + auto-switches ref-stage analyses to chrom-median; override flag opts out.
4. **Posterior stage-1 anchoring promoted from "deferred" to in-cycle-15.4a (SPEC15.6 deliverable 6 extended).** Each pipeline anchors flat-flagged samples to its stage-1 / pre-amp slot BEFORE clustering remaining samples into k-1 stages.
5. **`--known-reliable-amplicons` synonym for `--keep-amplicons-bed` (SPEC15.6 deliverable 9 extended).** Both flag spellings accepted.
6. **HMM-specific state-path-aware reliability extensions moved to SPEC15.13 deliverable 7 (cycle 15.7a).** Cycle 15.4a SPEC15.6 commits only to the cross-pipeline shared schema for HMM.

**Implications for R3 re-audit:** the auditor MUST verify the live code against the REFINED contract above, NOT the original SPEC15.6 deliverable text that R2 implemented. Expected gaps in live code (R2 implemented original contract):
- `--aps-preamp-threshold` flag IS in live code (`onionskin.py:1632`) — should NOT be there per refined contract; needs removal.
- `--flat-sample-prob-threshold` / `--flat-sample-fold-threshold` / `--flat-sample-min-flat-fraction` / `--allow-nonflat-ref-stage` flags are NOT in live code — need to be added.
- `onionskin_core/flat_sample.py` module does NOT exist — needs to be created (or current flat-sample code in `aps.py` / `reliability.py` relocated).
- Posterior stage-1 anchoring is NOT implemented — flat-sample sidecar exists but anchoring contract is the missing half.
- Auto-switch-to-chrom-median controller logic does NOT exist.
- `--known-reliable-amplicons` synonym does NOT exist.
- Per-locus flat test mechanism: needs verification against Bayesian ODW primitive (the original Codex implementation may have used a different per-locus test under the original deliverable-3 wording).

These gaps become **Stage I.5, J.5, and extended Stage I + Stage K work** — Role 2 picks the stage decomposition, but the 6 refinements above are the CONTRACT R3 verifies.

**No CHANGELOG entry** for this orchestrator round — the cycle is still mid-stream (between R2 and R3 closeout). DECISIONS captures the design refinements; SPEC15.6 + SPEC15.13 + STRATEGY are the authoritative contract; AUDIT_LOG (this section) is the round-by-round provenance.

### 2026-04-29 — Role 1 Initial Audit
**Authors:** John M. Urban, Claude Code 2.1.116 (claude-opus-4-7 ; Effort: Max)

**Verdict:** OPEN. Cycle 15.4a covers the highest-implementation-surface cycle of Phase 15 (per STRATEGY: "Two substantive priorities; SPEC15.6 explicitly depends on SPEC15.5 ... Highest implementation surface"). Audited at HEAD `2a3a420` (Phase 15 SPEC15.21 scope amendment commit, on `main`).

**SPEC15.5** is largely greenfield for HMM: of the 6 sub-priorities (a–f), only (c) "ghost levels" has a partial primitive in `onionskin_core/hmm_fork_travel.py:188, 234-243` (per-row `ghost_level_flag`), and only (d)/(e) have a fragmentary precursor at `onionskin_core/hmm_summit_refinement.py:226-250` (`_evaluate_multistage_filter` driving the `--hmm-require-multistage-growth` flag at `onionskin.py:2068`). The HMM shape-filter sink (a), the cross-stage shape meta-analysis port from RMS (b), the 4-mode combined unification flag (d), the 4 explicit state-path-evolution checks (e), and the new fork/width progression outputs (f) are all un-started. The two HMM shape-score CLI flags (`onionskin.py:2084-2110`) are still labeled "Inheritance placeholder — parser surface only; no current behavioral effect" — the SPEC15.5 deliverables-(a)/(b) wiring is exactly what makes them behavioral.

**SPEC15.6** is essentially greenfield cross-pipeline. Zero hits in `onionskin_core/` for `reliability_score`, `flat_sample`, `preamp_threshold`, `keep-amplicons-bed`, `aps-preamp`. The pre-APS evidence-scoring pass (deliverable 1), composite reliability flag (2), flat-sample detection (3), the two new flags (4 + 9), the cross-pipeline sidecar TSV (5), the reliability-filtered APS clustering contract (6), the class-aware scoring formula (8), and the calibration-amplicon regression gate (11) are all un-started. Deliverable 12 ("reuse already-implemented machinery") is verified intact (MAD bedGraphs at v0.5.47 + `aps.py:847+ _compute_locus_diagnostics` + `is_oscillator` / `max_rcn_stage` / `regression_stage` / `oscillating_transitions` columns). Deliverable 7 (classifier cross-pipeline audit + harmonization) is partly satisfied because cycle 15.3a wired all three pipelines (Growth + HMM + RMS) through the shared `_classify_amplicon` at `onionskin_core/timing.py:646-660`; the audit-and-harmonize step still needs a deliberate cross-pipeline pass on the schema parity and the post-Bayesian-ODW behavior of the classifier.

The work is decomposable into ~12 stages (audit-instruction Stage A through Stage L) with clear sequencing: SPEC15.5 (a) → (b) → (c) → (d) → (e) → (f), then SPEC15.6 (1+10+11+12) → (3+4) → (5+6) → (2+8) → (7+9) → (validation). Per STRATEGY this is "NOT skip-reaudit eligible" — confirmed; the implementation surface is too large and too algorithmic for skip-reaudit to be safe.

**Out-of-scope observation surfaced during audit (FIXED in a follow-up correction commit between R1 audit and R2 implementation; recorded for traceability).** The new ODW argparse group at `onionskin.py:1298-1300` was titled `Ordered Developmental Window (ODW)` instead of the SPEC-mandated `Origin Detection Window (ODW)`. The wrong name also appeared in `multi-agent/full_instructions/ONIONSKIN_FULL_HANDOFF.md:329`, `CHANGELOG.md:52, 54`, and the cycle 15.3a Stage B implementation report at this AUDIT_LOG line 178 (F2) + line 499 (Stage B). DEVLOG.md was already correct — an earlier audit citation of DEVLOG was wrong. The SPEC and the Round-4 brainstorming archive use **Origin Detection Window** (e.g., `PHASE15_SPEC.md:85, 298` and the SOUP/BRAINSTORM provenance). This was a cycle 15.3a SPEC15.4 deliverable-2 surface-correction matter, not SPEC15.5/15.6 work. **Resolution:** user authorized a standalone post-cycle-15.3a correction commit before cycle 15.4a R2 fires. All seven occurrences corrected in-place; cycle-15.3a fossilized text in this AUDIT_LOG (lines 178 + 499) edited to the correct name (post-hoc correction is preferred over a follow-up CHANGELOG/DEVLOG subsection because the group title is user-visible and the wrong name was a typo, not a recorded design decision).

#### Findings (live-code audit)

**F1. SPEC15.5(a) HMM shape-filter sink — MISSING.** No HMM equivalent of `_apply_multistage_shape_filter()` (`onionskin.py:396-510`) or `_shape_filter_calls()` (`onionskin_core/rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py:18-22`). The two HMM shape-score CLI flags (`onionskin.py:2084-2110`) carry `default=None` and explicit help text "Inheritance placeholder — parser surface only; no current behavioral effect. HMM shape-score wiring is scheduled for Phase 15 HMM completeness work". Group header at `onionskin.py:1064-1073` similarly says "HMM shape-score wiring is scheduled for Phase 15 HMM completeness work — the HMM-specific overrides ... are placeholders until then". The Universal `--shape-score-threshold` and `--shape-score-strict-bic` flags at `onionskin.py:1074-1108` already work for growth + RMS (consumed via `apply_shape_filter_tsv()` at `rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py:251-280` and `_shape_filter_calls` at `rcn_mean_shift_helpers.py:18-22`).

The shape-filter primitive `dBIC_flat_vs_tri` is exported from `rcn_mean_shift_helpers` at line 18 (`as _dBIC_flat_vs_tri`). Reuse path: factor it into a pipeline-agnostic call once HMM input is decided. The SPEC suggests "per-amplicon stage-median RCN profile within the HMM amplicon interval" as the input — that maps cleanly to the per-stage `hmm_amplicon_metrics.tsv` rows under `01-hmm/11-amplicon-metrics/stage_*.hmm_amplicon_metrics.tsv` consumed by `hmm_summit_refinement._load_step11_rows` (`hmm_summit_refinement.py:74-86`).

**F2. SPEC15.5(b) HMM cross-stage shape meta-analysis port — MISSING.** RMS imports `run_multistage` from `rcn_mean_shift_helpers` (`onionskin_core/engines/rcn_mean_shift_engine.py:34`) and emits `*_shape_scores_multistage.tsv` + `*_amplicons_actively_rejected_multistage.bed` to `02-growth-model/03-shape-filter/` and `03-rcn-mean-shift/06-shape-filter/`. HMM has no analogous output. Suggested HMM step: a new entry in `output_layout.build_hmm_steps()` (`onionskin_core/output_layout.py:287-307`) — e.g., a multistage-unification step inserted between current step-13 (summit refinement) and current step-14 (APS); step-numbering decisions land with Role 2.

**F3. SPEC15.5(c) HMM-specific state-path growth credibility (ghost levels) — PARTIAL.** Primitive exists in `onionskin_core/hmm_fork_travel.py`:
- Per-row field `ghost_level_flag` initialized at `hmm_fork_travel.py:188` and populated by `_annotate_cross_stage()` at `hmm_fork_travel.py:206-247`. Definition (lines 234-238): a level is "ghost" at stage `s` if it is present at `s` but missing at `s+1`. This captures the SPEC's "flickering" half ("level appears/disappears → low credibility").
- Field appears in step-12 output schema at `hmm_fork_travel.py:334, 440`.
- The "growing state path" (depth increases across stages) half of the SPEC's credibility signal is NOT computed as a direct credibility feature. Step-13 has the related `level_growth = (max(n_levels) > min(n_levels))` check (`hmm_summit_refinement.py:236-238`), but only as a binary input to `_evaluate_multistage_filter`, not as a graded credibility score.

Required: lift these primitives into a credibility-feature column (likely a per-amplicon-trajectory score combining `ghost_level_flag` count + monotonic-growth-of-`n_levels` + monotonic-growth-of-fork-travel — exact formula left to Role 2; emit in the new multistage unification output from F2). Connect to the within-ODW non-monotonicity rule (SPEC15.5 line 411): the Bayesian ODW per-transition test from SPEC15.4 is what disambiguates "true plateau-mid-window" from "ghost level". The credibility feature MUST consume `--odw-active-stage-detector` outputs (per `_evaluate_multistage_filter` rewrite below).

**F4. SPEC15.5(d) Combined multistage unification flag (4 modes) — MISSING.** Zero hits for `multistage_unification_mode`, `--multistage-mode`, `--ms-unification`, `unification.mode` anywhere. The existing `--hmm-require-multistage-growth` (`onionskin.py:2068, 3420`) is a binary precursor on the state-path side only and does not run a triangle pass. RMS `run_multistage()` (`rcn_mean_shift_helpers`) covers the triangle side for RMS but is not exposed as a "mode" choice.

Required: new flag (suggested name `--multistage-unification-mode`, choices `{triangle-only, state-path-only, both-required, either-suffices}`, default `either-suffices` per SPEC line 393-396). Both methods always run regardless of choice; result columns appear in the multistage-unification per-amplicon-table (per SPEC line 396 "Both methods always run and report results in per-amplicon-table columns"). Apply cross-pipeline (per SPEC 15.5 phase title "HMM shape-filter + multistage unification") — for now the triangle method already runs on growth + RMS via `run_multistage`; the state-path method is HMM-only. Role 2's call whether the flag is named `--hmm-multistage-unification-mode` (HMM-only) vs Universal-with-pipeline-overrides; SPEC lines 393-396 lean Universal-with-pipeline-overrides because growth/RMS amplicons can also receive a state-path-style structural check via fork-travel surrogates if Role 2 sees a clean port.

**F5. SPEC15.5(e) State-path-evolution checks (4 explicit checks) — MISSING.** SPEC lines 398-417 specify 4 checks plus 5 decision rules (verbatim BRAIN15.18). Live code has only `level_growth = max(n_levels) > min(n_levels)` (`hmm_summit_refinement.py:236-238`) which is a partial form of check 3 ("multi-step state-path evolution"). Checks 1 (persistence-after-pop-up), 2 (width-grows-across-stages), 4 (pyramid-shape symmetric) are missing entirely.

Required: implement each check explicitly per SPEC's verbatim formulation. CRITICAL constraint per SPEC line 411-415: each check operates **across the full ODW** (allowing plateau-mid-window patterns), using the SPEC15.4 Bayesian ODW per-transition test, NOT contiguous-monotonic-growth. Concretely:
- Check 1 (persistence): use `last_active_stage - onset_stage + 1` from `timing.py` (the `odw_duration_stages` column added in cycle 15.3a, `timing.py:541-543`).
- Check 2 (width grows): consume per-stage amplicon widths from `step11/hmm_amplicon_metrics.tsv` (`hmm_summit_refinement.py:74-86`); test "width at last_active_stage > width at onset_stage" as a Bayesian-ODW-aware Δwidth (Role 2 picks: same Bayesian primitive from `onionskin_core/odw.py:transition_probability` reused with width substituted for log2-RCN, OR a simpler MAD-based ratio).
- Check 3 (multi-step evolution): max-over-ODW `n_levels`, gated by Bayesian-ODW active-stage mask.
- Check 4 (pyramid-shape): for amplicons with `n_levels >= 2` at `max_rcn_stage`, test summit-interval-center vs distance-to-leftmost-step + distance-to-rightmost-step. Reuse `summit_left`/`summit_right`/`origin_bp` from `hmm_summit_refinement.py:31-41`.

Apply the 5 decision rules (SPEC lines 405-409) verbatim. **Stage 1 growth IS detectable against background** per universal design rule 9 (`PHASE15_SPEC.md:89`); the SPEC15.4 stage-1-vs-background path at `timing.py:504-519` is the consumer for "amplicons that start early" classification.

**F6. SPEC15.5(f) HMM fork/width progression outputs — PARTIAL.** Growth has `02-growth-model/05-progression/` (`output_layout.py:89` — `*_progression.tsv`). HMM has `12-fork-travel/fork_travel_metrics.tsv` (per-level travel/asymmetry rows from `hmm_fork_travel.py:175-194`) but no per-doubling-round fork-position output and no "growth-analogous width-progression" output. There is a `fork_progression.ipynb` notebook surface (`onionskin_core/notebooks.py:1596, 1979-1985`) but it's a notebook, not a per-amplicon TSV product.

Required: emit two new HMM outputs per SPEC line 419-423.
- (i) State-path-derived: per-doubling-round fork positions + per-stage leading-edge of state 1 (or top-level state). Suggested location: a new HMM step directory (e.g., `01-hmm/<step>-progression/`; step number TBD by Role 2 — fits naturally adjacent to `12-fork-travel/`).
- (ii) Growth-analogous width-progression: only if it produces meaningfully different information vs (i) (per SPEC verbatim Q19 quote at line 422 "where it produces meaningfully different results"). If (i) already captures everything, skip (ii) and document the rationale in the implementation report.

**F7. SPEC15.6(1)(2)(8)(10)(11) Pre-APS reliability scorer — MISSING.** Zero hits for `reliability_score` / `reliability_flag` / `composite_reliability` anywhere in `onionskin_core/`. The only adjacent surface is the placeholder `locus_weight = 1.0` constant at `aps.py:988` with the comment "always; oscillation informs annotation, not weighting". The 5 evidence axes (RCN-, width-, area-, BIC-, parabola-height-increase across stages) require new computation:
- RCN-increase: per-stage `peak_rcn` already computed (`aps.py:965-967` from `contrib_df`).
- Width-increase: per-stage amplicon widths available from `hmm_amplicon_metrics.tsv` (HMM), `_calls.tsv` (RMS/Growth).
- Area-increase: `area_excess` already computed (`aps.py:236+ compute_aps_tables`).
- BIC-increase: `delta_bic` from shape-filter pass (`onionskin.py:433-434`); needs HMM equivalent post-F1.
- Parabola-height-increase: need to compute; refinement code at `onionskin_core/refinement.py` may have parabola-fit primitives Role 2 can reuse.

Required: new module `onionskin_core/reliability.py` (per SPEC line 496 "likely `onionskin_core/reliability.py`"), pipeline-agnostic, exposing a single entry point that takes a per-amplicon DataFrame with the 5 axes and emits per-amplicon `reliability_score`, `reliability_flag`, plus per-axis sub-scores. Cross-pipeline contract per SPEC line 462 + 499-502: identical schema across HMM + Growth + RMS. Class-aware weighting per SPEC line 474, 478-480 (deliverable 8 + acceptance criteria): Founder + Constitutive_Prolonged require stronger evidence to exclude; Intermediary/Excluded need less. Numeric formula left to Role 2 within the LOCKED minimum-rule + regression-gate constraints.

CRITICAL constraints per SPEC's locked acceptance criteria (lines 476-486):
- The 4 DS1 chr II calibration amplicons (BRAIN15.7 lines 274-280 in archived BRAINSTORM) MUST come out with weight ≈ 1.0. Add a regression test consuming the calibration BED (location to be decided by Role 2; if not present in `tests/data/`, the test should skip gracefully and emit a TODO rather than fail — Role 2 picks).
- The formula MUST NOT penalize low absolute RCN; only inconsistency (per SPEC line 484 + verbatim BRAIN15.7 [2026-04-01] founding principle).

**F8. SPEC15.6(3)(4) Flat-sample detection + `--aps-preamp-threshold` flag — MISSING.** Zero hits. New flag with SPEC's `auto`-mode option per SPEC line 460 (gap-detection-based; Role 2 decides whether `auto` lands here or as follow-on). New per-sample flat detection on the 5 evidence axes; pre-amplification samples flagged before APS clustering runs.

Required: add `--aps-preamp-threshold` to the APS argparse group (existing — find via `grep -n "aps" onionskin.py | head`); new flat-detection routine in `onionskin_core/reliability.py` operating on per-sample-per-stage data; flagged samples emitted in a sidecar (per F9 below).

**F9. SPEC15.6(5)(6) Cross-pipeline reliability sidecar TSV + APS clustering wiring — MISSING.** Required schema-identical sidecar emitted by all 3 pipelines alongside APS outputs. Suggested location: alongside each pipeline's APS step (Growth at `02-growth-model/<aps-step>/`, RMS at `03-rcn-mean-shift/<aps-step>/`, HMM at `01-hmm/14-aps/`). Sidecar columns must be schema-identical (per SPEC line 462 "Identical schema across HMM + Growth + RMS"). APS clustering filtering: SPEC15.11 + SPEC15.12 will be the consumers; this cycle wires the contract by ensuring `compute_and_write_aps()` (`aps.py:1133`) and the clustering call sites consult the sidecar before clustering.

**F10. SPEC15.6(7) Cross-pipeline classifier audit + harmonization — PARTIAL.** Cycle 15.3a wired all three pipelines through `_classify_amplicon()` (`timing.py:646-660`, six labels). Per SPEC line 466-472, this cycle's audit step verifies:
1. The classifier's logic works across the 3 pipelines under the post-Bayesian-ODW framework (NOT the retired 1.25× rule). Verified by cycle 15.3a F3 + F10 (constants retired; classifier consumes `last_active_stage`).
2. Schema parity — all 3 pipelines emit the same `amplicon_class` column. Cycle 15.3a tests assert this for HMM + Growth; RMS path needs verification via the new `15-timing/rms_timing.tsv` output.
3. HMM may emit OPTIONAL state-path-aware extras (NOT replacing shared schema) per SPEC line 470 + 501-502.

Required: as part of the F1-F6 multistage-unification work, add a small audit + verification pass (Role 2 emits a brief implementation-report subsection enumerating the schema parity check + any HMM-only extra columns). Calibrate against any known cross-pipeline divergence by running `make test`, `make toy`, `make twin`, plus an explicit RMS-timing schema diff.

**F11. SPEC15.6(9) `--keep-amplicons-bed` user override — MISSING.** New Universal flag accepting a BED path; coordinates always retained despite reliability flags. Place in the APS argparse group adjacent to `--aps-preamp-threshold`. Apply to all 3 pipelines via the shared reliability module's filter function.

**F12. SPEC15.6(12) Reuse already-implemented machinery — VERIFIED INTACT.** Cycle 15.3a kept these surfaces stable:
- Per-stage MAD bedGraphs (v0.5.47): `aps.py:160-161, 174-175`; consumed by `_median_mad_sigma()` in `timing.py:477-496`.
- Probabilistic oscillation test (v0.5.48): `aps.py:1037-1051` calls `transition_probability(direction="regression", ...)` from `onionskin_core/odw.py`.
- Oscillation annotation columns (v0.5.49): `aps.py:932, 988-993, 1075` emits `is_oscillator` (via `compute_and_write_aps`), `max_rcn_stage`, `regression_stage`, `oscillating_transitions`.

The reliability module is a CONSUMER of these primitives, not a redefinition. **DO NOT** duplicate any of the above surfaces in `reliability.py`.

**F13. SPEC15.6(7) cross-pipeline classifier line-number drift.** SPEC15.6 line 442 + 467 references `timing.py:511-527` (six labels). Live code has the classifier at `timing.py:646-660` (post-cycle-15.3a `last_active_stage` rename + cross-pipeline RMS-timing wiring shifted line numbers). Role 2 should NOT rewrite those SPEC line numbers (the SPEC body is frozen during implementation). Treat the SPEC line numbers as a snapshot at SPEC engineering close.

#### Implementation instructions for Role 2

**Stage sequencing rationale.** SPEC15.5 must land before SPEC15.6 (per SPEC line 367-368: "SPEC15.6 (BRAIN15.7) explicitly depends on SPEC15.5 (BRAIN15.18) per the BRAIN15.7 multi-stage unification overlap section — reliability scoring runs on the final amplicon set produced by multistage unification"). Within SPEC15.5, sub-priorities a → b → c → d → e → f follow the SPEC ordering. Within SPEC15.6, the natural order is: (a) reuse-machinery audit (deliverable 12), (b) reliability scorer + class-aware formula (deliverables 1 + 2 + 8 + 10 + 11), (c) flat-sample detection (deliverables 3 + 4), (d) sidecar TSV + APS-clustering wiring (deliverables 5 + 6), (e) classifier audit + `--keep-amplicons-bed` (deliverables 7 + 9), (f) validation.

Suggested 12-stage commit split (Role 2's call to merge stages where work is genuinely atomic; do NOT over-fragment):

- **Stage A — SPEC15.5(a) HMM shape-filter sink.** Wire `dBIC_flat_vs_tri` consumption into HMM. Decide input source (per-stage `hmm_amplicon_metrics.tsv` rows is the cleanest; document in implementation report). Emit `shape_score_raw` column in an HMM output file (probably a new TSV under the multistage-unification step from Stage B). Wire the two HMM CLI flags (`onionskin.py:2084-2110`) — remove the placeholder language from help text. Update group header at `onionskin.py:1064-1073` to drop "HMM shape-score wiring is scheduled for Phase 15 HMM completeness work — the HMM-specific overrides ... are placeholders until then" and replace with the live HMM step reference.

- **Stage B — SPEC15.5(b) HMM cross-stage shape meta-analysis port.** Port `run_multistage` (or its core logic) from `rcn_mean_shift_helpers` into HMM as a new step inserted between current `13-summit-refinement` and current `14-aps`. Update `output_layout.build_hmm_steps()` (`output_layout.py:287-307`) and the HMM step dispatch in `engines/hmm_engine.py:1541+` accordingly. Note this is a step-renumbering touch: anything `14-aps` becomes the next step number; any `15-saps` / `16-timing` / `17-clustering` / `18-gap-analysis` shifts by +1. Update ALL of: `output_layout.build_hmm_steps()`, `engines/hmm_engine.py` dispatch, `Makefile` (if step-named targets exist), `scripts/` (if any reference HMM step paths), `tests/` (rename any step-path-based fixtures), help text in `onionskin.py` containing `[hmm: NN-...]` annotations, and `PIPELINE_SPEC.md` HMM step table. Renumbering is the single largest blast-radius surface in this cycle.

- **Stage C — SPEC15.5(c) Ghost-level credibility feature.** Lift `ghost_level_flag` (per-stage flag, `hmm_fork_travel.py:188, 234-243`) into a per-trajectory credibility score combining: (i) ghost-stage count, (ii) monotonic-growth of `n_levels` across stages, (iii) monotonic-growth of fork-travel widths across stages. Emit as a new column in the Stage-B output. The feature MUST consume Bayesian-ODW active-stage masks from SPEC15.4 (`onionskin_core/odw.py:detect_active_stages`), NOT a contiguous-monotonic-growth check.

- **Stage D — SPEC15.5(d) Combined multistage unification flag.** Add `--multistage-unification-mode` to argparse (Role 2 picks Universal-with-pipeline-overrides vs HMM-only; SPEC leans Universal). Choices `{triangle-only, state-path-only, both-required, either-suffices}`, default `either-suffices`. Both methods always run regardless of selection per SPEC line 396. Output per-amplicon-table columns should record both methods' verdicts independently (e.g., `triangle_pass`, `state_path_pass`, `mode_unified_pass`).

- **Stage E — SPEC15.5(e) Four state-path-evolution checks.** Implement checks 1-4 verbatim per SPEC lines 398-403 with the SPEC line 411-415 within-ODW non-monotonicity rule. Use the Bayesian ODW primitive (`onionskin_core/odw.py:transition_probability`) for any "is this transition active growth" sub-test — NOT the retired 1.25× constant. Apply the 5 decision rules (SPEC lines 405-409) verbatim. Stage-1 background growth detectability per universal rule 9 — handled by SPEC15.4 wiring at `timing.py:504-519`.

- **Stage F — SPEC15.5(f) Fork/width progression outputs.** Emit (i) state-path-derived per-doubling-round fork positions + per-stage leading-edges; emit (ii) growth-analogous width-progression IFF it produces meaningfully different information vs (i). If skipping (ii), document rationale in implementation report. Suggested location: new HMM step `<step>-progression/` adjacent to `12-fork-travel/`. Cross-pipeline parity reference: `02-growth-model/05-progression/` (`output_layout.py:89`).

- **Stage G — SPEC15.6(12) Reuse-machinery audit + new `onionskin_core/reliability.py` module skeleton.** Document the consumed primitives (per F12 above) in a module-level docstring. Skeleton entry-point `compute_amplicon_reliability(per_amplicon_df, per_stage_stats, classifier_labels) -> reliability_df`. No formula yet — just the contract.

- **Stage H — SPEC15.6(1)(2)(8)(10)(11) Reliability scorer + class-aware formula + calibration-gate test.** Implement the 5-axis evidence scorer; class-aware weighting per LOCKED rule (Founder + Constitutive_Prolonged need stronger evidence to exclude; Intermediary + Excluded need less). Honor founding design principle: low absolute RCN must NOT be penalized; only inconsistency. Add the calibration-amplicon regression test against the 4 DS1 chr II loci from BRAIN15.7. If the calibration BED is not yet committed under `tests/data/`, the test skips with a TODO message rather than failing.

- **Stage I — SPEC15.6(3)(4) Flat-sample detection + `--aps-preamp-threshold` flag.** Add the flag to the APS argparse group; default value left to Role 2 (with `auto` mode option per SPEC line 460 — Role 2 picks whether to land `auto` here or defer). New per-sample flat-detection routine consuming the 5-axis evidence framework. Emit per-sample flat flag for downstream consumers.

- **Stage J — SPEC15.6(5)(6) Cross-pipeline reliability sidecar TSV + APS clustering filter wiring.** Schema-identical sidecar columns: `call_id`, `reliability_score`, `reliability_flag`, the 5 per-axis sub-scores, `flat_sample_flag`. Emitted alongside each pipeline's APS step. Wire `compute_and_write_aps()` (`aps.py:1133+`) and downstream clustering call sites to consult the sidecar before clustering. Identical filter semantics per SPEC line 464 — no per-pipeline filter divergence.

- **Stage K — SPEC15.6(7)(9) Classifier cross-pipeline audit + `--keep-amplicons-bed` flag.** Implementation report includes a brief audit subsection: schema-parity verification across HMM + Growth + RMS for `amplicon_class` (now that all 3 pipelines wire timing per cycle 15.3a); enumeration of any HMM-only extra columns. Add `--keep-amplicons-bed` to APS argparse group; coordinates always retained despite reliability flags. Apply via the shared reliability module's filter function.

- **Stage L — Documentation + validation.** Update `PIPELINE_SPEC.md` HMM step section (post-Stage-B step renumber) + new shape-filter / multistage-unification / progression / reliability surfaces. Update `ONIONSKIN_FULL_HANDOFF.md` overview. Run targeted test suite per `CLAUDE.md` risk-based matrix: `make test`, `make twin`, `make full-twin` (per SPEC15.5 validation line 431) + `make toy` (per SPEC15.6 validation line 504); plus the new calibration-amplicon regression test; plus `make puff-compare` to verify HMM-engine PuffStep parity unchanged.

**Skip-reaudit eligibility.** This cycle is **NOT skip-reaudit eligible** per STRATEGY ("Highest implementation surface"). Re-audit is required.

- `make test` (full smoke).
- `make toy` (small multi-stage validation).
- `make twin` + `make full-twin` (twin-peak validation; SPEC15.5 explicit requirement).
- `make puff-compare` (HMM engine parity — must be unchanged from cycle 15.3a baseline).

#### Cross-cycle dependency notes

- **Cycle 15.9a (SPEC15.21) interaction.** SPEC15.21 (cycle 15.9a) moves `_compute_locus_diagnostics` + `_compute_global_degradation` from `aps.py` to `onionskin_core/timing_diagnostics.py`. Cycle 15.4a Stage G/H/J must NOT depend on the post-move location — `aps.py` is the live source until cycle 15.9a lands. Role 2 should consume the primitives in their current `aps.py` location and let cycle 15.9a do the relocation.
- **Cycle 15.5a (SPEC15.7 + SPEC15.8) consumes 15.4a.** SPEC15.7 cross-pipeline summit refinement consumes the multistage-unified amplicon set from SPEC15.5 (per SPEC line 433 + 599). SPEC15.8 summit↔timing convergence consumes the same. Role 2 should ensure the multistage-unification output's per-amplicon-table is structured for downstream consumption (`call_id` keyable, schema documented in PIPELINE_SPEC.md per Stage L).
